title: Epic: Cupertino-style navigation — frosted glass tab bar, CupertinoIcons, and iOS-native nav bars
description:
## Goals
Refactor the navigation shell and screen scaffolds to use iOS-native Cupertino widgets while preserving all business logic, auth, and Supabase integration.

### 1. Main shell (app.dart)
- Import cupertino.dart and dart:ui
- Replace Material NavigationBar with CupertinoTabBar wrapped in ClipRRect + BackdropFilter for frosted glass effect
- Replace Material AppBar with CupertinoNavigationBar (clean, no heavy shadows)
- Replace all Material Icons with CupertinoIcons equivalents
- Keep MaterialApp wrapper (needed for theme, SnackBars, dialogs)
- Keep all auth logic, deep links, callbacks untouched

### 2. Pushed screens (notifications, admin screens)
- Replace Scaffold + AppBar with CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar
- Keep all body content and business logic intact

### 3. Tab screens with own Scaffold (calendar)
- CalendarScreen has its own Scaffold for FAB — update to use CupertinoPageScaffold or keep Scaffold but with Cupertino nav bar

## Constraints
- Do NOT modify any Supabase logic, auth flow, or business logic
- Do NOT change the AuthScreen (it's a separate flow)
- Keep MaterialApp as the root (needed for Material theme, SnackBars, showDialog)
- All UI text stays in Spanish
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor app.dart main shell — CupertinoTabBar with frosted glass, CupertinoNavigationBar, CupertinoIcons
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Add imports
Add at the top of the file:
```dart
import 'dart:ui';
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
```

### 2. Replace AppBar with CupertinoNavigationBar
In _buildMainShell(), replace the Material AppBar (lines 233-246) with a CupertinoNavigationBar. Since we're inside a MaterialApp Scaffold, use it as the appBar parameter (CupertinoNavigationBar implements ObstructingPreferredSizeWidget which extends PreferredSizeWidget):

```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: const Text('Limpy'),
  trailing: CupertinoButton(
    padding: EdgeInsets.zero,
    child: const Icon(CupertinoIcons.bell),
    onPressed: () {
      _navigatorKey.currentState?.push(
        CupertinoPageRoute(builder: (_) => const NotificationsScreen()),
      );
    },
  ),
  backgroundColor: CupertinoColors.systemBackground.withOpacity(0.8),
  border: null,
),
```

Note: CupertinoNavigationBar works as a PreferredSizeWidget inside Material Scaffold's appBar parameter.

### 3. Replace NavigationBar with CupertinoTabBar + frosted glass
Replace the entire bottomNavigationBar (lines 248-296) with:

```dart
bottomNavigationBar: ClipRect(
  child: BackdropFilter(
    filter: ImageFilter.blur(sigmaX: 15.0, sigmaY: 15.0),
    child: CupertinoTabBar(
      currentIndex: _currentIndex,
      onTap: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
      activeColor: CupertinoColors.activeBlue,
      backgroundColor: CupertinoColors.systemBackground.withOpacity(0.6),
      items: const [
        BottomNavigationBarItem(
          icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.calendar),
          activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.calendar_today),
          label: 'Calendario',
        ),
        BottomNavigationBarItem(
          icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.checkmark_square),
          activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.checkmark_square_fill),
          label: 'Actividades',
        ),
        BottomNavigationBarItem(
          icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.house),
          activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.house_fill),
          label: 'Inicio',
        ),
        BottomNavigationBarItem(
          icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.chat_bubble_2),
          activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.chat_bubble_2_fill),
          label: 'Comentarios',
        ),
        BottomNavigationBarItem(
          icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.gear),
          activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.gear_solid),
          label: 'Configuración',
        ),
      ],
    ),
  ),
),
```

Remove the old Container wrapper with margin, BoxDecoration, boxShadow, and ClipRRect — the frosted glass BackdropFilter replaces all of that.

### 4. Update _themeModeIcon to use CupertinoIcons
Change the getter (lines 193-197):
```dart
IconData get _themeModeIcon => switch (_themeMode) {
  AppThemeMode.system => CupertinoIcons.circle_lefthalf_fill,
  AppThemeMode.light => CupertinoIcons.sun_max_fill,
  AppThemeMode.dark => CupertinoIcons.moon_fill,
};
```

### 5. Update _buildRetryScreen icons
In _buildRetryScreen (line 207), change:
`Icons.error_outline` → `CupertinoIcons.exclamationmark_circle`

### 6. Update notification button route
Change `MaterialPageRoute` to `CupertinoPageRoute` for the notifications push (already shown in step 2).

### 7. Keep everything else unchanged
- Keep MaterialApp as root widget (needed for Theme, SnackBar, showDialog)
- Keep all auth logic, deep links, callbacks
- Keep _showFeedbackDialog (uses showDialog which needs MaterialApp)
- Keep IndexedStack for screen management
- Keep _screens list order and content

## Don't
- Don't change MaterialApp to CupertinoApp (SnackBars and showDialog need Material)
- Don't modify any screen files
- Don't change any auth, Supabase, or business logic
- Don't change the _screens list content or order
- Don't remove any existing methods or fields
- Don't change _currentIndex default (stays 2)
- Don't use CupertinoTabScaffold (it manages its own tab state — we need manual control via _currentIndex + IndexedStack)
acceptance_criteria:
1. app.dart imports dart:ui and package:flutter/cupertino.dart
2. AppBar replaced with CupertinoNavigationBar with middle='Limpy' and trailing bell button
3. Bell button uses CupertinoIcons.bell and pushes NotificationsScreen via CupertinoPageRoute
4. CupertinoNavigationBar has semi-transparent background and no border
5. bottomNavigationBar uses CupertinoTabBar wrapped in ClipRect + BackdropFilter with blur(15,15)
6. CupertinoTabBar has activeColor=CupertinoColors.activeBlue and semi-transparent background
7. All 5 tab items use CupertinoIcons (calendar, checkmark_square, house, chat_bubble_2, gear)
8. _themeModeIcon uses CupertinoIcons (circle_lefthalf_fill, sun_max_fill, moon_fill)
9. Retry screen uses CupertinoIcons.exclamationmark_circle
10. MaterialApp is still the root widget
11. All auth logic, deep links, and callbacks are unchanged
12. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 537bcbc refactor: replace AppBar/NavigationBar with Cupertino equivalents + frosted glass

title: Update pushed screens to use CupertinoPageScaffold and CupertinoNavigationBar
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/feedback_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/user_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/task_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/extension_requests_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

For each file listed above, make these changes:

### 1. Add imports
Add to each file:
```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
```

### 2. Replace Scaffold + AppBar with CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar
For each screen that has `return Scaffold(appBar: AppBar(...),...)`, replace with:
```dart
return CupertinoPageScaffold(
  navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
    middle: Text('<existing title>'),
    // Keep any existing actions as trailing widgets
    backgroundColor: CupertinoColors.systemBackground.withOpacity(0.8),
    border: null,
  ),
  child: SafeArea(
    child: <existing body content>,
  ),
);
```

### Specific changes per file:

#### notifications_screen.dart
- Replace Scaffold + AppBar('Notificaciones') with CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar(middle: Text('Notificaciones'))
- Replace Icons.system_update with CupertinoIcons.arrow_down_circle_fill
- Replace Icons.campaign with CupertinoIcons.speaker_fill  
- Replace Icons.download with CupertinoIcons.arrow_down_to_line

#### feedback_screen.dart
- This screen uses TabBar which is Material-only. Keep the Scaffold but replace AppBar with a styled one that has no elevation. Add `import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';` for any CupertinoIcons used.
- Actually, since TabBar requires a Material Scaffold, KEEP the Scaffold here but set `appBar: AppBar(elevation: 0, scrolledUnderElevation: 0, ...)` to remove heavy shadows. This is the pragmatic approach since CupertinoPageScaffold doesn't support TabBar.

#### user_management_screen.dart
- Replace Scaffold + AppBar('Gestionar Usuarios') with CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar

#### task_management_screen.dart
- Replace Scaffold + AppBar('Gestionar Actividades') with CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar

#### extension_requests_screen.dart
- Replace Scaffold + AppBar('Solicitudes de Prórroga') with CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar

#### schedule_management_screen.dart
- This file has TWO Scaffolds (main screen + cycle generator dialog). Replace both AppBars.
- The main screen has a FAB — CupertinoPageScaffold doesn't support FAB natively. Use a Stack with Positioned to place the FAB button.

#### calendar_screen.dart
- Has its own Scaffold with a FAB. Same approach: use Stack with Positioned for the FAB, or keep Scaffold but remove AppBar elevation.
- Actually, CalendarScreen is a TAB screen (rendered inside IndexedStack in app.dart). It already has its own Scaffold for the FAB. Since the main shell already provides the CupertinoNavigationBar, CalendarScreen should NOT add another nav bar. Keep its Scaffold but set `appBar: null` (or remove the appBar parameter) since the parent shell provides the nav bar. The Scaffold is only needed for the FAB.

### Important notes on CupertinoPageScaffold
- CupertinoPageScaffold's child needs SafeArea to avoid content going under the nav bar
- The body content (ListView, Column, etc.) goes inside the child
- Material widgets (Card, ListTile, FilledButton, etc.) work fine inside CupertinoPageScaffold as long as MaterialApp is the root

## Don't
- Don't modify any business logic, Supabase calls, or state management
- Don't change the AuthScreen (separate flow)
- Don't change home_screen.dart, activities_screen.dart, comments_screen.dart, or settings_screen.dart (tab screens without own Scaffold, except calendar)
- Don't remove Material widgets from body content (Card, ListTile, etc. are fine)
- Don't change any TabBar/TabBarView usage (keep Material for those)
acceptance_criteria:
1. notifications_screen.dart uses CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar
2. user_management_screen.dart uses CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar
3. task_management_screen.dart uses CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar
4. extension_requests_screen.dart uses CupertinoPageScaffold + CupertinoNavigationBar
5. feedback_screen.dart keeps Scaffold but with elevation: 0 on AppBar (TabBar compatibility)
6. schedule_management_screen.dart uses CupertinoPageScaffold or Scaffold with no-shadow AppBar
7. calendar_screen.dart Scaffold has no AppBar (parent shell provides nav bar)
8. All CupertinoNavigationBars have semi-transparent background and no border
9. All files import package:flutter/cupertino.dart
10. No business logic or Supabase calls modified
11. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: ac9a89b migrate pushed screens to CupertinoPageScaffold/CupertinoNavigationBar

title: Replace Material Icons with CupertinoIcons in settings_screen.dart and home_screen.dart

```dart
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
```

### 3. home_screen.dart — Replace Icons with CupertinoIcons
Make these replacements:

title: Epic: Notifications screen, nav bar redesign, update announcement lifecycle, and admin announcement editing
description:
## Goals

### 1. Notifications screen accessible to all users
Add a 'Notificaciones' item to the 3-dot PopupMenuButton in the AppBar. Tapping it opens a new NotificationsScreen that shows the full history of all announcements (both active and inactive). Update-type announcements always appear at the top of the list, followed by aviso-type announcements. Each entry shows title, message, type chip, date, and link (if applicable).

### 2. Update announcement lifecycle change
Updates stay active forever (remove the 7-day auto-expire filter from home_screen.dart). When a NEW update announcement is published, the PREVIOUS update announcement is automatically deactivated (replaced). Aviso announcements are unaffected — they remain active until manually deactivated.

### 3. Navigation bar redesign
Move profile from the 3-dot menu to the bottom NavigationBar. Reorder tabs to: Actividades – Calendario – Inicio – Comentarios – Perfil. Inicio (index 2) is the default selected tab on app launch. The profile menu item and its push-navigation in app.dart are removed. ProfileScreen becomes an inline tab (not a pushed route). The deep link handler must also be updated since it currently pushes ProfileScreen.

### 4. Admin announcement editing and deletion
Admins can edit any announcement (active or inactive) — title, message, type, link. Admins can delete any announcement (active or inactive), not just inactive ones. Add an updateAnnouncement service method.

## Success Criteria
- All users see 'Notificaciones' in 3-dot menu and can view announcement history
- Updates shown at top in notifications screen
- Update announcements never auto-expire; publishing new update deactivates previous
- Nav bar order: Actividades-Calendario-Inicio-Comentarios-Perfil with Inicio as default
- Profile removed from 3-dot menu, shown as nav bar tab
- Admins can edit and delete any announcement
- All UI text in Spanish
- Quality gate passes
close_reason: Closed

title: Redesign navigation bar — move profile to tab, reorder to Actividades-Calendario-Inicio-Comentarios-Perfil
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Change _currentIndex default to 2
Line 28: Change `int _currentIndex = 0;` to `int _currentIndex = 2;` so Inicio is the default tab.

### 2. Update _screens list (currently lines 163-171)
Change from:
```dart
List<Widget> get _screens => [
  HomeScreen(
    currentUser: _currentUser!,
    onNavigateToActivities: () => setState(() => _currentIndex = 1),
  ),
  ActivitiesScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  CalendarScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  CommentsScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
];
```
To (new order: Actividades=0, Calendario=1, Inicio=2, Comentarios=3, Perfil=4):
```dart
List<Widget> get _screens => [
  ActivitiesScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  CalendarScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  HomeScreen(
    currentUser: _currentUser!,
    onNavigateToActivities: () => setState(() => _currentIndex = 0),
  ),
  CommentsScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
];
```
Note: onNavigateToActivities now points to index 0 (Actividades).

### 3. Update NavigationBar destinations (currently lines 345-365)
Replace the 4 destinations with 5 in this order:
```dart
destinations: const [
  NavigationDestination(
    icon: Icon(Icons.checklist_outlined),
    selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.checklist),
    label: 'Actividades',
  ),
  NavigationDestination(
    icon: Icon(Icons.calendar_month_outlined),
    selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.calendar_month),
    label: 'Calendario',
  ),
  NavigationDestination(
    icon: Icon(Icons.home_outlined),
    selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.home),
    label: 'Inicio',
  ),
  NavigationDestination(
    icon: Icon(Icons.comment_outlined),
    selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.comment),
    label: 'Comentarios',
  ),
  NavigationDestination(
    icon: Icon(Icons.person_outline),
    selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.person),
    label: 'Perfil',
  ),
],
```

### 4. Remove 'profile' from PopupMenuButton
Remove the 'profile' case from onSelected switch (lines 238-249).
Remove the PopupMenuItem with value 'profile' from itemBuilder (lines 271-280).

### 5. Update _handleDeepLink (lines 104-114)
Since ProfileScreen is now at index 4 in the nav bar, change the deep link handler from pushing a route to switching tabs:
```dart
void _handleDeepLink(Uri uri) {
  if (uri.scheme == 'limpy' && uri.host == 'reset-callback') {
    if (_isAuthenticated && _currentUser != null) {
      setState(() => _currentIndex = 4);
    }
  }
}
```

### 6. Update signOut handler
In the onAuthStateChange listener (line 50), change `_currentIndex = 0` to `_currentIndex = 2` so it resets to Inicio on logout.

### 7. Handle ProfileScreen data refresh
Currently ProfileScreen returns pop(true) to signal changes. Since it's now an inline tab, we need a different approach. Add a callback parameter. In _screens, pass a callback:
```dart
ProfileScreen(
  currentUser: _currentUser!,
  onProfileChanged: _loadCurrentUser,
),
```
This requires adding an `onProfileChanged` VoidCallback parameter to ProfileScreen.

## Files also modified
- lib/screens/profile_screen.dart (modify)

In ProfileScreen:
a) Add `final VoidCallback? onProfileChanged;` field
b) Update constructor: `const ProfileScreen({super.key, required this.currentUser, this.onProfileChanged});`
c) In _saveProfile() success path (where it currently does Navigator.pop(context, true)), replace with:
```dart
widget.onProfileChanged?.call();
ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(
  const SnackBar(content: Text('Perfil actualizado exitosamente')),
);
```
d) Remove the Navigator.pop(context, true) call from _saveProfile
e) Change the Scaffold's appBar from having a back button to NOT having one (remove the leading/automaticallyImplyLeading if present). Actually, since it will be in an IndexedStack, the AppBar from app.dart's _buildMainShell will be shown instead. So ProfileScreen should NOT have its own Scaffold with AppBar when used as a tab. 

WAIT — ProfileScreen currently has its own Scaffold+AppBar. Since it's now embedded in IndexedStack inside _buildMainShell's Scaffold, it will create a double-Scaffold situation. The solution: ProfileScreen should keep its own content but NOT wrap in Scaffold when used as a tab. The simplest approach: remove the Scaffold wrapper from ProfileScreen and just return the body content (SingleChildScrollView). The AppBar title 'Administrar perfil' is no longer needed since the main AppBar says 'Limpy'.

Actually, looking at the other screens (HomeScreen, ActivitiesScreen, etc.), they do NOT have their own Scaffold — they just return content widgets that go inside the main Scaffold's body via IndexedStack. So ProfileScreen should follow the same pattern.

Changes to ProfileScreen:
- Remove the Scaffold wrapper and its AppBar
- Return the body content directly (the SingleChildScrollView or whatever the body currently is)
- Add onProfileChanged callback
- Replace Navigator.pop(context, true) with widget.onProfileChanged?.call()
- Keep all the password/email change functionality intact

## Don't
- Don't modify HomeScreen, ActivitiesScreen, CalendarScreen, or CommentsScreen
- Don't change the PopupMenuButton items other than removing 'profile'
- Don't change the theme toggle, feedback, admin_feedback, or logout menu items
- Don't remove the import for ProfileScreen from app.dart (it's still used)
- Don't change any auth logic
acceptance_criteria:
1. Default tab on app launch is Inicio (index 2)
2. NavigationBar has 5 tabs in order: Actividades, Calendario, Inicio, Comentarios, Perfil
3. Tapping Perfil tab shows ProfileScreen content inline (no route push)
4. 3-dot menu no longer contains 'Administrar perfil' item
5. Deep link handler switches to tab index 4 instead of pushing route
6. Sign-out resets to index 2 (Inicio)
7. ProfileScreen has no Scaffold/AppBar wrapper (content only, like other tab screens)
8. ProfileScreen has onProfileChanged callback that triggers _loadCurrentUser
9. onNavigateToActivities callback points to index 0
10. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 059e3e3 redesign nav bar: 5 tabs, ProfileScreen inline, remove profile menu item

title: Change update announcement lifecycle — stay active forever, new update replaces previous

2. supabase_service.dart createAnnouncement deactivates all existing active update announcements before inserting a new update
3. Aviso announcements are NOT deactivated when a new update is published
4. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test

title: Add Notificaciones screen and menu item — announcement history for all users
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart (create)
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Create lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart
A new screen that shows the full history of all announcements (active and inactive).

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/data/supabase_service.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/announcement.dart';
import 'package:url_launcher/url_launcher.dart';

/// Screen showing the full history of announcements for all users.
class NotificationsScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const NotificationsScreen({super.key});

  @override
  State<NotificationsScreen> createState() => _NotificationsScreenState();
}
```

State class:
- Field: `List<Announcement> _announcements = [];`
- Field: `bool _isLoading = true;`
- initState calls _loadAnnouncements
- _loadAnnouncements: calls `SupabaseService.instance.getAllAnnouncements()`, then sorts so update-type announcements come first, then avisos. Within each group, sort by createdAt descending (newest first). Set state.
- _openLink(String url): same pattern as home_screen.dart — `launchUrl(uri, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication)`
- _formatDate(DateTime dt): returns 'dd/MM/yyyy'

Build method:
- Returns a Scaffold with AppBar title 'Notificaciones'
- Body: if loading, show CircularProgressIndicator. If empty, show centered text 'No hay notificaciones'. Otherwise, ListView.separated with announcement cards.
- Each card shows:
  - Row with icon (Icons.system_update for update, Icons.campaign for aviso) + title (bold) + type Chip ('Actualización' or 'Aviso')
  - Message text
  - Date text (bodySmall style)
  - If inactive, show a subtle 'Inactivo' chip with muted color
  - If update type with link, show a 'Descargar actualización' FilledButton.icon
- Use Card with Padding, similar style to _buildAnnouncementBanner in home_screen.dart but without dismiss button

### 2. app.dart — Add 'notifications' menu item
In the PopupMenuButton's onSelected switch, add a case:
```dart
case 'notifications':
  _navigatorKey.currentState?.push(
    MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => const NotificationsScreen()),
  );
```

In itemBuilder, add a PopupMenuItem BEFORE the 'feedback' item:
```dart
PopupMenuItem(
  value: 'notifications',
  child: const Row(
    children: [
      Icon(Icons.notifications_outlined),
      SizedBox(width: 12),
      Text('Notificaciones'),
    ],
  ),
),
```

Add import at top: `import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/notifications_screen.dart';`

## Don't
- Don't modify any existing screens
- Don't add admin-only restrictions — this screen is for ALL users
- Don't add edit/delete functionality to this screen (that's admin-only in FeedbackScreen)
- Don't use ListTile inside PopupMenuItem (use Row with Icon + Text)
- Don't add realtime subscriptions to this screen (it loads on open)

The actual RealtimeService Dart implementation (lib/core/realtime_service.dart, screen subscriptions in home_screen.dart, activities_screen.dart, comments_screen.dart) was bundled into the beads close commit for cx3.11 (addf7e3 'beads: close flutter_cleanapp-cx3.11'). title: Epic: CleanApp Frontend — Cleaning Schedule Management UI
description:
## Goal
Build the complete frontend for a bathroom cleaning schedule management app for an apartment building. The app allows residents to see when it is their turn to clean, track cleaning tasks via a checklist, and view the upcoming schedule on a calendar.

## Success Criteria
- App has 3 screens accessible via a BottomNavigationBar: Home, Activities, Calendar
- Blue-themed color palette with light/dark mode toggle
- All screens use mock data (no backend yet — Supabase integration is future work)
- App passes flutter analyze, dart format, and flutter test

## Key Constraints
- Frontend only — no Supabase integration yet
- Mock data provider supplies all data (users, schedules, tasks)
- Must follow project AGENTS.md code style (package imports, const constructors, super.key, etc.)
- Material Design 3 with blue color scheme
- Spanish-language UI strings (the app is for Spanish-speaking users)
close_reason: Closed

title: Define app theme system with light/dark mode and blue color palette
description:
## Files
- lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart (create)

## What to do
Create a theme configuration file that provides both light and dark ThemeData objects using Material Design 3.

### Class: `AppTheme`
Abstract final class (no instantiation) with these static members:

```dart
static const Color seedColor = Color(0xFF1565C0); // Blue 800
static final ThemeData lightTheme = ThemeData(...)
static final ThemeData darkTheme = ThemeData(...)
```

Both themes MUST use:
- `useMaterial3: true`
- `ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: seedColor, brightness: ...)` for the respective brightness
- `fontFamily: "Roboto"` (default, just be explicit)
- BottomNavigationBar theme: selected item uses `colorScheme.primary`, unselected uses `colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant`
- Card theme: elevation 1, rounded corners (12 radius)
- AppBar theme: `centerTitle: true`, `scrolledUnderElevation: 2`

### Enum: `AppThemeMode`
```dart
enum AppThemeMode { light, dark, system }
```

## Do not
- Import any external packages (only flutter/material.dart)
- Create a ChangeNotifier or state management here — that goes in the shell task
- Use hardcoded colors beyond the seed color — let ColorScheme.fromSeed generate the palette
- Use deprecated ThemeData fields

title: Create mock data provider with sample users, schedules, and tasks
description:
## Files
- lib/data/mock_data.dart (create)

## What to do
Create a class that provides hardcoded mock data for the entire app. This replaces Supabase until backend integration.

### Class: `MockData`
Abstract final class with static getters/methods:

```dart
abstract final class MockData {
  /// The currently "logged in" user (always the first user for now)
  static UserModel get currentUser => users.first;

  /// All residents in the building
  static List<UserModel> get users => [ ... ];

  /// Cleaning schedule entries — one per user, rotating weekly
  /// Generate entries for the past 2 weeks and next 6 weeks (8 total entries)
  static List<CleaningSchedule> get schedules => [ ... ];

  /// The 8 cleaning tasks for a given schedule
  static List<CleaningTask> tasksForSchedule(String scheduleId) => [ ... ];

  /// Default task titles in Spanish
  static const List<String> defaultTaskTitles = [
    "Lavar el retrete",
    "Lavar el lavamanos",
    "Lavar la regadera",
    "Barrer",
    "Trapear",
    "Secar el piso",
    "Sacar la basura",
    "Limpiar las jergas",
  ];
}
```

### Mock users (4 residents):
- id: "u1", name: "Carlos García", apartment: "Depto 1A"
- id: "u2", name: "María López", apartment: "Depto 2B"
- id: "u3", name: "Juan Martínez", apartment: "Depto 3A"
- id: "u4", name: "Ana Rodríguez", apartment: "Depto 4B"

### Schedule generation logic:
- Start from the Monday of 2 weeks ago
- Each user gets assigned one week in rotation (u1, u2, u3, u4, u1, u2, u3, u4)
- Past schedules are marked isCompleted: true
- The current week and future weeks are isCompleted: false
- Use DateTime.now() to determine "current week"

### Task generation:
- tasksForSchedule returns 8 CleaningTask objects, one per defaultTaskTitles entry
- Task IDs: "{scheduleId}_t{index}" (e.g. "s3_t0", "s3_t1", ...)
- All tasks start with isCompleted: false

## Do not
- Use any external packages
- Make network calls or async operations
- Add randomness — data must be deterministic for a given week
- Import flutter/material.dart (only import the model files)
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/data/mock_data.dart exists
2. MockData.users returns exactly 4 UserModel objects with the specified names and apartments
3. MockData.currentUser returns the first user (Carlos García)
4. MockData.schedules returns exactly 8 CleaningSchedule entries spanning 8 weeks
5. Past schedules have isCompleted == true, current and future have isCompleted == false
6. MockData.tasksForSchedule("s0") returns exactly 8 CleaningTask objects
7. Task titles match the 8 Spanish cleaning tasks listed in defaultTaskTitles
8. MockData.defaultTaskTitles has exactly 8 entries
9. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
10. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed

title: Build Home/Dashboard screen with cleaning status notification
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create the Home screen that shows the current user their cleaning status.

### Class: `HomeScreen` (StatelessWidget)
```dart
class HomeScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  final VoidCallback onNavigateToActivities;
  const HomeScreen({super.key, required this.onNavigateToActivities});
}
```

The screen reads mock data to determine the current user status and renders one of two states:

### State A — User IS responsible for cleaning this week:
Layout (top to bottom, centered, with padding 24):
1. Large icon: `Icons.warning_amber_rounded` (size 80, color `colorScheme.error`)
2. SizedBox(height: 16)
3. Text "¡Te toca hacer el aseo!" (headlineMedium, bold, centered)
4. SizedBox(height: 8)
5. Text "Semana del {startDate} al {endDate}" (bodyLarge, centered) — dates formatted as "dd/MM/yyyy"
6. SizedBox(height: 24)
7. Card containing:
   - ListTile with leading Icon(Icons.person), title: user name, subtitle: user apartment
8. SizedBox(height: 24)
9. FilledButton.icon(
     icon: Icons.checklist,
     label: "Ir a Actividades",
     onPressed: onNavigateToActivities,
   ) — this button calls the callback to switch to the Activities tab

### State B — User is NOT responsible this week:
Layout (top to bottom, centered, with padding 24):
1. Large icon: `Icons.check_circle_outline` (size 80, color `colorScheme.primary`)
2. SizedBox(height: 16)
3. Text "¡Estás libre esta semana!" (headlineMedium, bold, centered)
4. SizedBox(height: 8)
5. Text "Tu próximo turno es la semana del {nextDate}" (bodyLarge, centered) — date formatted as "dd/MM/yyyy"
6. SizedBox(height: 24)
7. Card containing:
   - ListTile with leading Icon(Icons.person), title: user name, subtitle: user apartment

### Logic to determine state:
- Get current user from MockData.currentUser
- Find the schedule entry for the current week (where schedule.date falls in this Monday-Sunday range)
- If that schedule userId == currentUser.id AND isCompleted == false → State A
- Otherwise → State B (find next future schedule where userId == currentUser.id for the "next date")

Wrap the body in a SingleChildScrollView for safety.

## Do not
- Use any state management — this is a StatelessWidget reading MockData directly
- Add any async operations
- Handle the case where no schedule exists (mock data always has schedules)
- Import or reference Supabase
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/screens/home_screen.dart exists with HomeScreen StatelessWidget
2. HomeScreen constructor takes required VoidCallback onNavigateToActivities
3. When current user has cleaning duty: shows warning icon, "¡Te toca hacer el aseo!" text, date range, user card, and "Ir a Actividades" button
4. When current user is free: shows check icon, "¡Estás libre esta semana!" text, next turn date, and user card
5. "Ir a Actividades" button calls onNavigateToActivities callback
6. Dates are formatted as dd/MM/yyyy
7. All text is in Spanish
8. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
9. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed

title: Build Activities screen with cleaning task checklist and finalize button
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/activities_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create the Activities screen showing a to-do checklist of cleaning tasks with a Finalize button.

### Class: `ActivitiesScreen` (StatefulWidget)
```dart
class ActivitiesScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  const ActivitiesScreen({super.key});
  @override
  State<ActivitiesScreen> createState() => _ActivitiesScreenState();
}
```

### State management:
- On initState, load tasks from MockData for the current user current-week schedule
- Store tasks in a `List<CleaningTask>` state variable
- If the current user has no schedule this week, show an empty state

### Layout:
The screen has two sections inside a Column:

**Section 1 — Header (inside Padding 16):**
- Text "Actividades de Aseo" (titleLarge, bold)
- SizedBox(height: 4)
- Text "Marca cada tarea al completarla" (bodyMedium, secondary color)

**Section 2 — Task list (Expanded, inside ListView.builder):**
Each task is a CheckboxListTile:
```dart
CheckboxListTile(
  value: task.isCompleted,
  onChanged: (value) => _toggleTask(index),
  title: Text(task.title, style: TextStyle(
    decoration: task.isCompleted ? TextDecoration.lineThrough : null,
  )),
  secondary: Icon(
    task.isCompleted ? Icons.check_circle : Icons.circle_outlined,
    color: task.isCompleted ? colorScheme.primary : colorScheme.outline,
  ),
  controlAffinity: ListTileControlAffinity.leading,
)
```

**Section 3 — Finalize button (inside Padding 16, at bottom):**
```dart
SafeArea(
  child: Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
    child: SizedBox(
      width: double.infinity,
      height: 48,
      child: FilledButton.icon(
        onPressed: _allCompleted ? _finalize : _showIncompleteWarning,
        icon: Icon(Icons.done_all),
        label: Text("Finalizar Aseo"),
      ),
    ),
  ),
)
```

### Behavior:
- `_toggleTask(int index)`: calls setState, replaces task at index with `task.copyWith(isCompleted: !task.isCompleted)`
- `_allCompleted`: getter that returns true if every task in the list has isCompleted == true
- `_showIncompleteWarning()`: shows a SnackBar with text "Aún tienes tareas pendientes por completar" and a warning icon
- `_finalize()`: shows a SnackBar with text "¡Aseo completado! Gracias por tu colaboración" with a success color (colorScheme.primary), and optionally marks all tasks visually as done

### Empty state (no schedule this week):
Show centered Column with:
- Icon(Icons.event_available, size: 64, color: colorScheme.outline)
- Text "No tienes aseo asignado esta semana" (bodyLarge)

## Do not
- Persist state to any backend
- Use any external state management packages
- Navigate away from this screen on finalize
- Add animations or transitions
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/screens/activities_screen.dart exists with ActivitiesScreen StatefulWidget
2. Shows 8 CheckboxListTile items matching the 8 cleaning tasks in Spanish
3. Tapping a checkbox toggles the task completed state and shows strikethrough text
4. Completed tasks show a check_circle icon in primary color
5. "Finalizar Aseo" button exists at the bottom, full width
6. Pressing Finalizar when NOT all tasks are checked shows SnackBar "Aún tienes tareas pendientes por completar"
7. Pressing Finalizar when ALL tasks are checked shows success SnackBar "¡Aseo completado! Gracias por tu colaboración"
8. When user has no schedule this week, shows empty state with "No tienes aseo asignado esta semana"
9. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
10. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed
close_reason: 7561298 Add Activities screen with cleaning task checklist and finalize button

title: Build Calendar screen showing upcoming cleaning schedule
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create the Calendar screen that shows the upcoming cleaning schedule for all residents.

### Class: `CalendarScreen` (StatelessWidget)
```dart
class CalendarScreen extends StatelessWidget {
  const CalendarScreen({super.key});
}
```

### Layout:
The screen shows a list of upcoming schedule entries. No external calendar package — build a simple custom list view.

**Header (inside Padding 16):**
- Text "Calendario de Aseo" (titleLarge, bold)
- SizedBox(height: 4)
- Text "Próximas fechas de limpieza" (bodyMedium, secondary color)

**Schedule list (ListView.builder inside Expanded):**
Get all schedules from MockData.schedules, sorted by date ascending. For each schedule entry, find the corresponding user from MockData.users.

Each entry is a Card (margin: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 16, vertical: 4)):
```dart
Card(
  child: ListTile(
    leading: CircleAvatar(
      backgroundColor: _isCurrentWeek(schedule) ? colorScheme.primary : colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest,
      foregroundColor: _isCurrentWeek(schedule) ? colorScheme.onPrimary : colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant,
      child: Text(user.name[0]), // First letter of name
    ),
    title: Text(user.name),
    subtitle: Text("${user.apartment} — Semana del ${_formatDate(schedule.date)}"),
    trailing: schedule.isCompleted
      ? Icon(Icons.check_circle, color: colorScheme.primary)
      : _isCurrentWeek(schedule)
        ? Chip(label: Text("Esta semana"), backgroundColor: colorScheme.primaryContainer)
        : null,
  ),
)
```

### Helper methods:
- `_isCurrentWeek(CleaningSchedule schedule)`: returns true if schedule.date falls within the current Monday-Sunday range
- `_formatDate(DateTime date)`: returns date formatted as "dd/MM/yyyy"
- `_isCurrentUser(CleaningSchedule schedule)`: returns true if schedule.userId == MockData.currentUser.id

### Visual distinction for current user entries:
If the schedule belongs to the current user, add a subtle left border or use `Card` with a `shape` that has a left-side colored border (4px wide, colorScheme.primary). This helps the user quickly spot their own entries.

### Month separator:
When the month changes between entries, insert a Padding with a Text showing the month name in Spanish (e.g., "Marzo 2026", "Abril 2026") styled as titleSmall with secondary color. Use a simple map for Spanish month names:
```dart
static const List<String> _monthNames = [
  "Enero", "Febrero", "Marzo", "Abril", "Mayo", "Junio",
  "Julio", "Agosto", "Septiembre", "Octubre", "Noviembre", "Diciembre",
];
```

## Do not
- Use any external calendar packages (no table_calendar, syncfusion, etc.)
- Add date picker or interactive calendar grid
- Make any async calls
- Import Supabase
acceptance_criteria:
1. File lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart exists with CalendarScreen StatelessWidget
2. Shows header "Calendario de Aseo" with subtitle "Próximas fechas de limpieza"
3. Lists all 8 schedule entries from MockData sorted by date
4. Each entry shows user name, apartment, and week start date formatted as dd/MM/yyyy
5. Completed schedules show a check_circle icon
6. Current week entry shows a "Esta semana" chip
7. Current user entries have a visual distinction (colored left border on card)
8. Month separators appear when month changes between entries (Spanish month names)
9. CircleAvatar shows first letter of user name with primary color for current week
10. flutter analyze passes with zero issues
11. dart format --set-exit-if-changed reports no changes needed

title: Integrate screens into app shell — replace placeholders with real screen widgets
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do
Update the CleanApp widget in lib/app.dart to replace the placeholder widgets with the real screen widgets.

### Changes to _CleanAppState:

1. Replace the `_screens` placeholder list. The screens list must be rebuilt on each build() call because HomeScreen needs a callback that calls setState:

```dart
List<Widget> get _screens => [
  HomeScreen(onNavigateToActivities: () => setState(() => _currentIndex = 1)),
  const ActivitiesScreen(),
  const CalendarScreen(),
];
```

2. Add these imports at the top of lib/app.dart:
```dart
import "package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/home_screen.dart";
import "package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/activities_screen.dart";
import "package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/calendar_screen.dart";
```

3. Keep everything else unchanged — the AppBar, NavigationBar, theme toggle all stay the same.

### Important: IndexedStack
Consider wrapping the body in an `IndexedStack` instead of just `_screens[_currentIndex]` to preserve state across tab switches (so Activities checkboxes are not reset when switching tabs):
```dart
body: IndexedStack(
  index: _currentIndex,
  children: _screens,
),
```

## Do not
- Change the NavigationBar destinations or labels
- Change the theme toggle behavior
- Modify any screen files — only modify lib/app.dart
- Add new dependencies or packages
- Remove the AppBar or any existing functionality

title: Epic: Fix prórroga swap display — correct date reordering in list and calendar views
description:
## Context
When user 1 requests a prórroga (extension) from user 2 and it's accepted, the schedule user_ids are swapped between the two involved periods. The expected visual result is:

Before swap (4 cycles, 2 users):
- Period: user1 dates (Mar 1-3)
- Period: user2 dates (Mar 8-10)
- Period: user1 dates (Mar 15-17)
- Period: user2 dates (Mar 22-24)

After swap (first cycle only):
- Period: user2 dates Mar 1-3 (marked as swapped)
- Period: user1 dates Mar 8-10 (marked as swapped)
- Period: user1 dates Mar 15-17
- Period: user2 dates Mar 22-24

## Current Bug
After the swap, the display shows:
- Period: user2 dates Mar 1-3
- Period: user2 dates Mar 8-10  ← WRONG, should be user1
- Period: user1 dates Mar 15-17
- Period: user2 dates Mar 22-24

## Root Causes
1. **List view grouping ignores date gaps**: calendar_screen.dart lines 895-906 group consecutive same-userId schedules into one period WITHOUT checking for date gaps. After swap, if the swapped next-user period (now requesterId=userA) is followed by another userA period, they merge into one visual period incorrectly.

2. **Potential swap detection issue**: _getRequestForSchedule() uses _findPeriodScheduleIds(anchor) which walks by anchor.userId. Post-swap, the anchor's userId has changed (was requester, now nextUser), so the period detection may collect wrong schedules.

## Key Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart — list/calendar views, period grouping, swap detection
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart — acceptExtensionRequest swap logic, _findPeriodSchedules
- test/data/supabase_service_swap_test.dart — swap unit tests
- test/screens/calendar_swap_detection_test.dart — calendar swap detection tests

## Success Criteria
- After an accepted prórroga, both list and calendar views correctly show swapped periods with proper user assignments
- Period grouping respects date gaps (consecutive same-user schedules with >1 day gap are separate periods)
- Swap detection correctly identifies both swapped periods post-swap
- All existing tests pass, new tests cover the post-swap grouping scenario

title: Fix period grouping in calendar list view to respect date gaps
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

### Problem
The period grouping logic in the `build()` method (lines 894-906) groups consecutive same-userId schedules into one `_PeriodEntry` WITHOUT checking for date gaps. After a prórroga swap, this causes incorrect grouping:

Example: Before swap:
```
a1: userA, Mar 1 | a2: userA, Mar 2 | a3: userA, Mar 3
b1: userB, Mar 8 | b2: userB, Mar 9 | b3: userB, Mar 10
a4: userA, Mar 15 | a5: userA, Mar 16 | a6: userA, Mar 17
b4: userB, Mar 22 | b5: userB, Mar 23 | b6: userB, Mar 24
```

After swap (first cycle):
```
a1: userB, Mar 1 | a2: userB, Mar 2 | a3: userB, Mar 3
b1: userA, Mar 8 | b2: userA, Mar 9 | b3: userA, Mar 10
a4: userA, Mar 15 | a5: userA, Mar 16 | a6: userA, Mar 17  ← same userId as b1-b3!
b4: userB, Mar 22 | b5: userB, Mar 23 | b6: userB, Mar 24
```

Current grouping merges b1-b3 and a4-a6 into ONE period (both userA), showing:
- Period: userB (Mar 1-3)
- Period: userA (Mar 8-17)  ← WRONG, should be two separate periods
- Period: userB (Mar 22-24)

### Fix
Replace the grouping condition on line 901 from:
```dart
if (periods.isNotEmpty && periods.last.user.id == schedule.userId) {
```

To also check that the date is consecutive (within 1 day of the last schedule in the period):
```dart
if (periods.isNotEmpty && periods.last.user.id == schedule.userId) {
  final lastDate = periods.last.schedules.last.date;
  final currentDate = schedule.date;
  final daysDiff = DateTime(
    currentDate.year,
    currentDate.month,
    currentDate.day,
  ).difference(DateTime(
    lastDate.year,
    lastDate.month,
    lastDate.day,
  )).inDays;
  if (daysDiff <= 1) {
    periods.last.schedules.add(schedule);
  } else {
    periods.add(_PeriodEntry(schedules: [schedule], user: user));
  }
} else {
  periods.add(_PeriodEntry(schedules: [schedule], user: user));
}
```

This ensures that same-user schedules with a date gap > 1 day are treated as separate periods.

### Important: The threshold is 1 day (not 0)
Within a period, dates are consecutive (Mar 1, Mar 2, Mar 3 → diffs of 1). Between periods of the same user, there's always a gap (Mar 3 to Mar 15 → diff of 12). Using `daysDiff <= 1` correctly handles both cases.

## Don't
- Don't change any other logic in calendar_screen.dart
- Don't modify _findPeriodScheduleIds, _findNextUserPeriodIds, or _getRequestForSchedule
- Don't modify the _PeriodEntry or _ListItem classes
- Don't change the grid/calendar view
- Don't change the list view rendering (_buildListView)
- Don't modify any other files
acceptance_criteria:
1. The period grouping loop in build() (around line 894-906) checks date gaps between consecutive same-user schedules
2. Same-user schedules with a date gap > 1 day are grouped into SEPARATE _PeriodEntry objects
3. Same-user schedules with date gap <= 1 day are still grouped together (existing behavior preserved)
4. After a prórroga swap where b1-b3 become userA and a4-a6 are also userA, they appear as TWO separate period cards in the list view (not merged into one)
5. No other logic in calendar_screen.dart is modified
6. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 55a2180 fix: period grouping respects date gaps in calendar list view

title: Add Comment model class
description:
## Files
- lib/models/comment.dart (create)

## What to do
Create an immutable data model for anonymous comments.

### Class: `Comment`
```dart
class Comment {
  final String id;
  final String scheduleId;   // which cleaning schedule this comment is about
  final String message;
  final DateTime createdAt;

  const Comment({
    required this.id,
    required this.scheduleId,
    required this.message,
    required this.createdAt,
  });
}
```

Key design decisions:
- No `senderId` field — comments are anonymous by design. The sender is never stored.
- `scheduleId` links the comment to a specific cleaning schedule (and thus to the responsible user for that week).
- No `recipientId` — the recipient is implicitly the user assigned to the schedule.

## Do not
- Add JSON serialization (future backend work)
- Add any flutter imports (only dart:core needed)
- Add a `senderId` or any field that could identify the sender
- Add business logic or validation

title: Epic: Bug fixes & comments redesign with reply threads
description:
## Goals
Fix 2 bugs and redesign the comments system:

### Bugs:
1. Profile navigation from 3-dot menu still doesn't work (second attempt)
2. Schedule management screen shows individual schedule rows instead of grouped period cards

### Comments redesign:
- Remove the Enviar/Recibir tabs — show a single view based on role
- Non-responsible users see the send form (current Enviar tab content)
- Responsible user sees the inbox (current Recibir tab content)
- Responsible user can reply to individual comments (expandable thread/cascade)
- Replies are NOT anonymous — they come from the responsible user
- The sender of the original comment sees the reply in a chat-like thread
- This requires adding sender_id to comments (nullable for backward compat) and parent_id for threading

### DB changes needed:
- Add sender_id (UUID, nullable, FK profiles) to comments table
- Add parent_id (UUID, nullable, FK comments) for reply threading
- Comments with sender_id=NULL are anonymous (original behavior)
- Replies have sender_id set to the responsible user's ID
- parent_id links a reply to its parent comment

## Context
- Comments table currently: id, schedule_id, message, created_at (no sender, no threading)
- Comments are anonymous by design — sender_id stays NULL for original comments
- Only replies from the responsible user have sender_id set
- The responsible user is determined by schedule.userId matching currentUser.id
- Realtime already subscribed to comments table changes

## Success Criteria
- Profile menu item navigates to ProfileScreen
- Schedule management shows grouped period cards with edit/delete per period
- Comments screen shows single view based on user role (no tabs)
- Responsible user can reply to individual comments
- Replies appear as expandable threads under the original comment
- Original commenter can see replies to their comment
- All existing functionality preserved

title: Fix profile navigation — debug and fix PopupMenuButton onSelected handler
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

The profile navigation from the 3-dot PopupMenuButton STILL doesn't work after the previous fix (xr5.1). The previous fix replaced ListTile with Row and made onSelected async. The code at lines 164-178 looks correct but navigation still fails.

### Debugging approach:

1. **Check if onSelected fires at all** — Add a `debugPrint('onSelected: $value');` at the top of the onSelected callback (line 164). If it doesn't print, the issue is still tap absorption.

2. **Check if the 'profile' case is reached** — Add `debugPrint('navigating to profile');` inside the profile case.

3. **Possible root cause: the PopupMenuItem with value 'profile' is const**. Look at line 191-199:
```dart
const PopupMenuItem(
  value: 'profile',
  child: Row(
    children: [
      Icon(Icons.person),
      SizedBox(width: 12),
      Text('Administrar perfil'),
    ],
  ),
),
```
The `const` keyword on the PopupMenuItem might cause issues with the value not being properly registered in some Flutter versions. **Remove the `const` keyword** from this PopupMenuItem.

4. **Alternative root cause: async onSelected with Navigator.push** — The `onSelected` callback is async and calls `Navigator.push`. The PopupMenuButton internally pops the popup menu, and then the async callback runs. But the `context` used in `Navigator.push` might be stale after the popup closes.

### Fix approach — use post-frame callback:

Replace the profile case with a post-frame callback to ensure navigation happens after the popup menu animation completes:

```dart
case 'profile':
  // Use addPostFrameCallback to navigate after popup menu closes
  WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback((_) async {
    if (!mounted) return;
    final changed = await Navigator.push<bool>(
      context,
      MaterialPageRoute(
        builder: (_) => ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
      ),
    );
    if (changed == true && mounted) _loadCurrentUser();
  });
```

### Also remove const from the profile PopupMenuItem:

Change line 191 from:
```dart
const PopupMenuItem(
```
to:
```dart
PopupMenuItem(
```

### Also add debugPrint statements temporarily to verify the fix works, then remove them before committing.

## Don't
- Do NOT change the theme toggle or logout logic
- Do NOT modify ProfileScreen itself
- Do NOT change the menu items or their order
- Do NOT leave debugPrint statements in the final commit
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping 'Administrar perfil' in the 3-dot menu navigates to ProfileScreen
2. ProfileScreen displays correctly with current user data
3. After saving profile changes and returning, user data is reloaded
4. Theme toggle still works from the menu
5. Logout still works from the menu
6. No debugPrint statements left in the code
7. flutter analyze reports no issues
8. flutter test — all tests pass
9. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes

title: Epic: Limpieza automática de periodos de aseo completados
description: Actualmente los schedules se acumulan indefinidamente en la base de datos. Se necesita eliminar automáticamente los periodos de aseo (schedules) completados después de 1 semana de haber sido completados, para no saturar la base de datos. La eliminación debe ser por periodos completos (todos los schedules consecutivos del mismo usuario). Éxito: los schedules completados hace más de 7 días se eliminan automáticamente, junto con sus extension_requests y comments asociados (cascade).

title: Crear RPC y migración SQL para limpieza de schedules completados
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/009_cleanup_old_schedules.sql (create)

## What to do

### 1. Create a Supabase RPC function to delete old completed schedules
Create a PostgreSQL function `cleanup_old_schedules()` that:
- Finds all schedules where `is_completed = true` AND `updated_at < now() - interval '7 days'` (or `date < now() - interval '7 days'` if updated_at is null)
- Deletes those schedules
- Returns the count of deleted rows
- The function uses `SECURITY DEFINER` to bypass RLS

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.cleanup_old_schedules()
RETURNS INT
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_deleted INT;
BEGIN
  -- Delete completed schedules older than 7 days.
  -- Use updated_at if available (set when marked completed),
  -- otherwise fall back to the schedule date itself.
  DELETE FROM schedules
  WHERE is_completed = true
    AND COALESCE(updated_at, date + interval '1 day') < now() - interval '7 days';
  
  GET DIAGNOSTICS v_deleted = ROW_COUNT;
  RETURN v_deleted;
END;
$$;

GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.cleanup_old_schedules() TO authenticated;
```

Note: The `ON DELETE CASCADE` on `extension_requests.schedule_id` and `comments.schedule_id` will automatically clean up related records.

### 2. Also add an updated_at trigger if not already present
Check if there is already a trigger that sets `updated_at` on schedules. If not, add one:

```sql
-- Ensure updated_at is set when a schedule is modified
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.set_updated_at()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Only create trigger if it does not exist
DO $$
BEGIN
  IF NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname = 'set_schedules_updated_at'
  ) THEN
    CREATE TRIGGER set_schedules_updated_at
      BEFORE UPDATE ON schedules
      FOR EACH ROW
      EXECUTE FUNCTION set_updated_at();
  END IF;
END;
$$;
```

## Don't
- Delete non-completed schedules
- Delete schedules that were completed less than 7 days ago
- Change any existing RLS policies
- Modify the schedules table schema (updated_at column already exists)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Migration file supabase/migrations/009_cleanup_old_schedules.sql exists
2. The cleanup_old_schedules() function deletes only completed schedules older than 7 days
3. The function returns the count of deleted rows
4. The function is SECURITY DEFINER to bypass RLS
5. The function is granted to authenticated users
6. An updated_at trigger exists on the schedules table

title: Invocar limpieza de schedules desde el servicio en segundo plano
acceptance_criteria:

title: Fix: _buildMainShell uses context above MaterialApp — Theme.of never sees dark mode
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## Root Cause
_buildMainShell() is called from build(BuildContext context) where context is the State context — ABOVE the MaterialApp. Therefore Theme.of(context).brightness always returns Brightness.light (framework default), never Brightness.dark. This is why all the dark mode conditionals in _buildMainShell never trigger.

## What to do

### 1. Change _buildMainShell signature to accept a BuildContext parameter
Change from:
```dart
Widget _buildMainShell() {
```
To:
```dart
Widget _buildMainShell(BuildContext context) {
```
This shadows the State context with the inner context from the Builder.

### 2. Wrap the _buildMainShell() call in a Builder
In the build() method (line 548-556), change:
```dart
home: _isLoadingUser
    ? const Scaffold(body: Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator()))
    : _isAuthenticated && _currentUser != null
    ? _buildMainShell()
    : !_isAuthenticated &&
          SupabaseConfig.client.auth.currentSession != null &&
          !(SupabaseConfig.client.auth.currentSession!.isExpired)
    ? _buildRetryScreen()
    : const AuthScreen(),
```
To:
```dart
home: _isLoadingUser
    ? const Scaffold(body: Center(child: CircularProgressIndicator()))
    : _isAuthenticated && _currentUser != null
    ? Builder(builder: (innerContext) => _buildMainShell(innerContext))
    : !_isAuthenticated &&
          SupabaseConfig.client.auth.currentSession != null &&
          !(SupabaseConfig.client.auth.currentSession!.isExpired)
    ? _buildRetryScreen()
    : const AuthScreen(),
```

### 3. Also add AnnotatedRegion for system UI overlay
Wrap the Scaffold returned by _buildMainShell with an AnnotatedRegion to also fix the Android system status bar and navigation bar:
```dart
Widget _buildMainShell(BuildContext context) {
  final isDark = Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark;
  return AnnotatedRegion<SystemUiOverlayStyle>(
    value: isDark
        ? SystemUiOverlayStyle.light.copyWith(
            statusBarColor: Colors.transparent,
            systemNavigationBarColor: Colors.black,
          )
        : SystemUiOverlayStyle.dark.copyWith(
            statusBarColor: Colors.transparent,
            systemNavigationBarColor: Colors.white,
          ),
    child: Scaffold(
      ... existing code using context (which now correctly reflects the theme) ...
    ),
  );
}
```

Add import at top of file:
```dart
import "package:flutter/services.dart";
```

## Dont
- Change any other files
- Change the theme definitions in app_theme.dart
- Change business logic, routes, or Supabase initialization
- Change _buildRetryScreen or AuthScreen
acceptance_criteria:
1. _buildMainShell accepts a BuildContext parameter
2. The call site uses Builder(builder: (innerContext) => _buildMainShell(innerContext))
3. Theme.of(context).brightness inside _buildMainShell now correctly returns Brightness.dark when dark mode is active
4. All dark mode conditionals in _buildMainShell (top bar, bottom bar, shadows, blur) now trigger correctly
5. AnnotatedRegion wraps the Scaffold for system UI overlay style
6. Android status bar icons are light in dark mode, dark in light mode
7. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 612b05b fix: _buildMainShell accepts BuildContext, Builder at call site, AnnotatedRegion for system UI

title: Fix dark mode contrast on top and bottom navigation bars
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do
The CupertinoNavigationBar (top) and CupertinoTabBar (bottom) use `Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85)` and `alpha: 0.7` respectively. In dark mode, the semi-transparent surface color creates too much contrast against the dark background — the bars appear lighter than expected because the frosted glass blur bleeds through light content.

### Fix: Use brightness-aware background colors

In the `_buildMainShell` method, make the bar backgrounds adapt based on the current theme brightness:

**Top bar (CupertinoNavigationBar, around line 372-374):**
Replace:
```dart
backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
```
With:
```dart
backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
    ? Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface
    : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
```
In dark mode: fully opaque surface (no transparency, no frosted glass bleed-through).
In light mode: keep the current semi-transparent frosted glass effect.

**Bottom bar (CupertinoTabBar, around line 398-400):**
Replace:
```dart
backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.7),
```
With:
```dart
backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
    ? Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface
    : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.7),
```
Same logic: opaque in dark mode, semi-transparent in light mode.

**Shadow (around line 384):**
Also make the shadow more visible in dark mode. Replace:
```dart
color: Theme.of(context).shadowColor.withValues(alpha: 0.1),
```
With:
```dart
color: Theme.of(context).shadowColor.withValues(alpha: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark ? 0.3 : 0.1),
```

## Don't
- Change the bar colors in light mode (keep frosted glass effect)
- Change the border radius (keep 28)
- Change the BackdropFilter blur values
- Modify any other files
acceptance_criteria:
1. In dark mode: top bar and bottom bar have fully opaque dark surface background (no transparency)
2. In light mode: bars retain semi-transparent frosted glass effect (unchanged)
3. Shadow is slightly more visible in dark mode (0.3 alpha vs 0.1)
4. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: bda5319 Fix dark mode contrast on top and bottom navigation bars

title: iOS-native dark mode: frosted glass bars (0xCC1C1C1E), dark cards (0xFF1C1C1E), accent blue (0xFF0A84FF)
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart (modify)

## What to do
Apply exact iOS dark mode colors to the main shell bars and the global dark theme. The user wants the native iOS dark aesthetic with frosted glass translucent bars.

### 1. app_theme.dart — Update dark theme colors

#### Update cupertinoOverrideTheme barBackgroundColor
Change from `Color(0xDD121212)` to `Color(0xCC1C1C1E)` (iOS system dark bar color with 80% opacity):
```dart
cupertinoOverrideTheme: const CupertinoThemeData(
  brightness: Brightness.dark,
  barBackgroundColor: Color(0xCC1C1C1E),
  scaffoldBackgroundColor: Colors.black,
  primaryColor: Color(0xFF0A84FF),
  textTheme: CupertinoTextThemeData(
    navTitleTextStyle: TextStyle(
      color: Colors.white,
      fontSize: 17,
      fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
    ),
    navLargeTitleTextStyle: TextStyle(
      color: Colors.white,
      fontSize: 34,
      fontWeight: FontWeight.w700,
    ),
  ),
),
```

#### Update cardTheme for dark mode
Add a `color` to the dark theme cardTheme:
```dart
cardTheme: const CardThemeData(
  elevation: 0,
  color: Color(0xFF1C1C1E),
  shape: ContinuousRectangleBorder(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.all(Radius.circular(40)),
  ),
),
```
Note: elevation 0 because iOS dark cards are flat.

#### Update appBarTheme for dark mode
Change backgroundColor from `Colors.black` to the translucent iOS color:
```dart
appBarTheme: const AppBarTheme(
  centerTitle: true,
  scrolledUnderElevation: 0,
  backgroundColor: Color(0xFF1C1C1E),
  foregroundColor: Colors.white,
  surfaceTintColor: Colors.transparent,
),
```

#### Update filledButtonTheme for dark mode
Use the iOS accent blue:
```dart
filledButtonTheme: FilledButtonThemeData(
  style: FilledButton.styleFrom(
    backgroundColor: const Color(0xFF0A84FF),
    foregroundColor: Colors.white,
    shape: RoundedRectangleBorder(borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(14)),
    padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 24, vertical: 14),
  ),
),
```

### 2. app.dart — Update _buildMainShell bars

#### Top bar (CupertinoNavigationBar)
Change the dark mode backgroundColor from `Color(0xFF000000)` to `Color(0xCC1C1C1E)` (translucent iOS dark). Also re-enable blur in dark mode for the frosted glass effect:
```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: Text(
    _currentTitle,
    style: isDark ? const TextStyle(color: Colors.white) : null,
  ),
  trailing: CupertinoButton(
    padding: EdgeInsets.zero,
    child: Icon(
      CupertinoIcons.bell,
      color: isDark ? Colors.white : null,
    ),
    onPressed: () {
      _navigatorKey.currentState?.push(
        CupertinoPageRoute(builder: (_) => const NotificationsScreen()),
      );
    },
  ),
  backgroundColor: isDark
      ? const Color(0xCC1C1C1E)
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  border: null,
  transitionBetweenRoutes: false,
),
```

#### Bottom bar (CupertinoTabBar)
Change colors to exact iOS values. Enable blur in dark mode too for frosted glass:
```dart
// In the BackdropFilter — REMOVE the dark mode conditional, always blur:
child: BackdropFilter(
  filter: ImageFilter.blur(sigmaX: 20.0, sigmaY: 20.0),
  child: CupertinoTabBar(
    currentIndex: _currentIndex,
    onTap: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
    activeColor: isDark
        ? const Color(0xFF0A84FF)
        : CupertinoColors.activeBlue,
    backgroundColor: isDark
        ? const Color(0xCC1C1C1E)
        : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.7),
    inactiveColor: isDark
        ? const Color(0xFF8E8E93)
        : CupertinoColors.inactiveGray,
    border: isDark ? const Border() : null,
    items: const [ ... keep existing items ... ],
  ),
),
```

#### Container decoration — keep shadow removal in dark mode as-is.

#### System navigation bar color
In the AnnotatedRegion, change systemNavigationBarColor from `Colors.black` to `Color(0xFF1C1C1E)`:
```dart
value: isDark
    ? SystemUiOverlayStyle.light.copyWith(
        statusBarColor: Colors.transparent,
        systemNavigationBarColor: const Color(0xFF1C1C1E),
      )
    : SystemUiOverlayStyle.dark.copyWith(
        statusBarColor: Colors.transparent,
        systemNavigationBarColor: Colors.white,
      ),
```

## Dont
- Change light mode appearance at all
- Change any business logic
- Change home_screen.dart or notifications_screen.dart (cards will inherit from cardTheme)
- Change any other screen files
- Remove the floating pill design of the bottom bar
acceptance_criteria:
1. In dark mode, top bar backgroundColor is Color(0xCC1C1C1E) — translucent dark with frosted glass blur
2. In dark mode, bottom bar backgroundColor is Color(0xCC1C1C1E) with blur enabled (sigmaX/Y: 20.0)
3. In dark mode, bottom bar activeColor is Color(0xFF0A84FF) (iOS blue)
4. In dark mode, bottom bar inactiveColor is Color(0xFF8E8E93) (iOS grey)
5. In dark mode, top bar title and bell icon are white
6. Dark theme cardTheme color is Color(0xFF1C1C1E) with elevation 0
7. Dark theme filledButtonTheme uses Color(0xFF0A84FF) background
8. Dark theme appBarTheme backgroundColor is Color(0xFF1C1C1E)
9. cupertinoOverrideTheme barBackgroundColor is Color(0xCC1C1C1E)
10. Light mode is completely unchanged
11. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 5e3de79 Apply iOS dark mode colors: frosted glass bars, dark cards, accent blue

title: Fix: swap icon missing on next-user schedule after prorroga accepted (from flutter_cleanapp-cx3.8)
description: Review of flutter_cleanapp-cx3.8 found: _getRequestForSchedule() in lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart only matches extension requests by scheduleId (exact match on line 104). After a prorroga is accepted and schedules are swapped, the 'next user' schedule has no extension_request pointing to it, so it never shows the 'Prorroga aceptada' chip with swap_horiz icon. Acceptance criterion #3 ('The swapped schedule (next user's) also shows the chip') is NOT met. Fix: extend _getRequestForSchedule() to also return accepted extension requests where the schedule's userId matches the nextUserId of an accepted request, or use a date-based lookup as described in the original spec.

2. After a prorroga is accepted, both affected calendar entries show the 'Prorroga aceptada' chip with swap_horiz icon
3. Pending prorrogas still show 'Prorroga pendiente' correctly (only on the original schedule)
4. Rejected prorrogas do NOT show any chip

title: Epic: Fix isResponsible date mismatch, verify prórroga swap, style empty states
description:
## Goals
Fix 3 user-reported issues:

1. **isResponsible date window mismatch (CRITICAL)**: app.dart _computeResponsibleStatus() uses a Monday-to-Sunday calendar week to find schedules, but the cleaning system uses rolling 3-day periods (cleaningPeriodDays=3). When a 3-day period crosses a Monday boundary, app.dart looks in the wrong week and returns false, causing the Activities screen to show 'libre' when the user IS responsible. Also missing isCompleted check.

2. **Prórroga swap scope**: The swap logic in acceptExtensionRequest() already uses maxSize:cleaningPeriodDays cap. Code review confirms it only swaps the involved periods (max 3 rows each). However, need to add a unit test to verify edge cases.

3. **Empty state styling**: NotificationsScreen and ExtensionRequestsScreen show plain unstyled Text for empty states. Need themed empty states with icons.

## Root Cause Analysis (Issue 1)
- app.dart uses: [currentMonday, currentMonday+7) — calendar week
- home_screen.dart uses: [today-2, today] — rolling 3-day window
- When a period starts on Saturday and extends to Monday, app.dart misses it
- Also: app.dart does NOT check !schedule.isCompleted, so completed schedules still count as responsible

## Success Criteria
- isResponsible matches home_screen.dart logic (rolling 3-day window + isCompleted check)
- Prórroga swap confirmed to only swap involved periods
- Empty states have themed icons and styled text
close_reason: All 3 tasks complete. isResponsible uses 3-day rolling window, swap test added (58 tests), empty states styled.

title: Fix _computeResponsibleStatus to use 3-day rolling window instead of calendar week
acceptance_criteria:
1. _computeResponsibleStatus uses rolling 3-day window: [today - (cleaningPeriodDays-1), today]
2. Schedule dates are normalized to midnight for comparison (DateTime(year, month, day))
3. isResponsible is false when schedule.isCompleted is true
4. isResponsible is false when no schedule exists in the 3-day window for the current user
5. isResponsible is true when user has an uncompleted schedule in the 3-day window
6. Prórroga check still works (uses schedule.id from the found schedule)
7. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 277b0aa fix _computeResponsibleStatus: 3-day rolling window + isCompleted check

title: Add unit test verifying prórroga swap only affects involved periods
description:
## Files
- test/data/supabase_service_swap_test.dart (modify or create)

## Context
The user reported that accepting a prórroga swaps ALL periods between two users, not just the involved ones. Code review of acceptExtensionRequest() in supabase_service.dart confirms the logic is correct — it uses _findPeriodSchedules with maxSize:cleaningPeriodDays (3) to cap each period. However, we need a unit test to verify this edge case.

## What to do
Check if test/data/supabase_service_swap_test.dart already exists. If it does, add a test case. If not, create it.

The test should verify that _findPeriodSchedules with maxSize:3 correctly caps the result even when there are adjacent same-user schedules beyond the 3-day period.

Since _findPeriodSchedules is a private method, we cannot test it directly. Instead, verify the behavior by testing the public interface or by making the method @visibleForTesting.

### Option A: If _findPeriodSchedules is already @visibleForTesting
Add test:
```dart
test("_findPeriodSchedules caps at maxSize even with adjacent same-user schedules", () {
  // Create 6 consecutive schedules for the same user (2 periods of 3)
  // Verify that with maxSize:3, only 3 are returned
});
```

### Option B: If not testable directly
Add a comment in the test file documenting that the swap logic was code-reviewed and confirmed correct. The maxSize cap at line 482 and 509 of supabase_service.dart ensures at most cleaningPeriodDays (3) schedules per period are swapped.

## Dont
- Change supabase_service.dart production code
- Change any screen files
- Remove existing tests
acceptance_criteria:
1. Test file exists with at least one test verifying period swap scope
2. Test passes
3. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 277b0aa test: verify prórroga swap only affects involved periods

title: Style empty state messages in NotificationsScreen and ExtensionRequestsScreen
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/extension_requests_screen.dart (modify)

## Problem
Both screens show plain unstyled Text for empty states:
- notifications_screen.dart line 152: `const Center(child: Text("No hay notificaciones"))`
- extension_requests_screen.dart line 161: `const Center(child: Text("No hay solicitudes de prórroga."))`

These look like large default-styled text that does not match the platform theme. They need themed empty states with icons and styled text, matching the pattern used elsewhere in the app (e.g., activities_screen.dart libre state).

title: Fix profile navigation using navigatorKey and enhance profile with password/email management
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/profile_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

### PART A: Fix profile navigation (THIRD ATTEMPT — different approach)

The profile navigation has failed twice. Previous approaches:
1. Replace ListTile with Row → didn't fix it
2. addPostFrameCallback → didn't fix it

**Root cause analysis**: The `MaterialApp` widget creates its own `Navigator`. The `context` inside `_buildMainShell()` is BELOW the `MaterialApp` in the widget tree, so `Navigator.push(context, ...)` should work. However, the `PopupMenuButton` may be using a different context.

**NEW APPROACH — Use a GlobalKey<NavigatorState>**:

1. Add a `GlobalKey<NavigatorState>` to `_LimpyAppState`:
```dart
final _navigatorKey = GlobalKey<NavigatorState>();
```

2. Pass it to `MaterialApp`:
```dart
return MaterialApp(
  navigatorKey: _navigatorKey,
  // ... rest unchanged
);
```

3. In the profile case of onSelected, use the navigatorKey:
```dart
case 'profile':
  _navigatorKey.currentState?.push<bool>(
    MaterialPageRoute(
      builder: (_) => ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
    ),
  ).then((changed) {
    if (changed == true && mounted) _loadCurrentUser();
  });
```

4. Remove the `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback` wrapper — it's no longer needed.

5. Make `onSelected` synchronous again (remove `async`):
```dart
onSelected: (value) {
  switch (value) {
    case 'theme':
      _toggleTheme();
    case 'profile':
      _navigatorKey.currentState?.push<bool>(
        MaterialPageRoute(
          builder: (_) => ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
        ),
      ).then((changed) {
        if (changed == true && mounted) _loadCurrentUser();
      });
    case 'logout':
      _logout();
  }
},
```

### PART B: Enhance ProfileScreen with password and email management

Add three new sections to the profile screen below the existing save button:

#### 1. Change Password section

Add a Card with:
- Title: 'Cambiar Contraseña'
- Current password field (obscured, with visibility toggle)
- New password field (obscured, with visibility toggle)
- Confirm new password field (obscured, with visibility toggle)
- Validation: new password must be at least 6 chars, confirm must match
- 'Cambiar Contraseña' FilledButton
- On submit:
  1. First verify current password by calling `SupabaseConfig.client.auth.signInWithPassword(email: currentEmail, password: currentPassword)`
  2. If verification succeeds, call `SupabaseConfig.client.auth.updateUser(UserAttributes(password: newPassword))`
  3. Show success SnackBar: 'Contraseña actualizada exitosamente'
  4. Clear all password fields
  5. If verification fails, show error: 'Contraseña actual incorrecta'

- Below the button, add a TextButton: '¿Olvidaste tu contraseña?'
  - On tap: call `SupabaseConfig.client.auth.resetPasswordForEmail(currentEmail)`
  - Show SnackBar: 'Se ha enviado un correo para restablecer tu contraseña'

#### 2. Change Email section

Add a Card with:
- Title: 'Cambiar Correo Electrónico'
- New email field
- 'Solicitar cambio' OutlinedButton
- On submit:
  1. Call `SupabaseConfig.client.auth.updateUser(UserAttributes(email: newEmail))`
  2. Show SnackBar: 'Se ha enviado un correo de verificación a tu correo actual'
  3. Clear the email field

#### State variables to add:
```dart
final _currentPasswordController = TextEditingController();
final _newPasswordController = TextEditingController();
final _confirmPasswordController = TextEditingController();
final _newEmailController = TextEditingController();
bool _isChangingPassword = false;
bool _isChangingEmail = false;
bool _obscureCurrentPassword = true;
bool _obscureNewPassword = true;
bool _obscureConfirmPassword = true;
```

#### Layout of the full profile screen:
```
[Avatar]
[Email (read-only)]
[Name field]
[Room field]
[Save button]

--- Divider ---

[Card: Cambiar Contraseña]
  [Current password]
  [New password]
  [Confirm password]
  [Change button]
  [Forgot password link]

--- Divider ---

[Card: Cambiar Correo Electrónico]
  [New email field]
  [Request change button]
```

### All text in Spanish:
- 'Cambiar Contraseña'
- 'Contraseña actual'
- 'Nueva contraseña'
- 'Confirmar nueva contraseña'
- 'Cambiar Contraseña' (button)
- '¿Olvidaste tu contraseña?'
- 'Contraseña actualizada exitosamente'
- 'Contraseña actual incorrecta'
- 'Las contraseñas no coinciden'
- 'Se ha enviado un correo para restablecer tu contraseña'
- 'Cambiar Correo Electrónico'
- 'Nuevo correo electrónico'
- 'Solicitar cambio'
- 'Se ha enviado un correo de verificación a tu correo actual'

### Import needed in profile_screen.dart:
```dart
import 'package:supabase_flutter/supabase_flutter.dart';
```
(for `UserAttributes` and `AuthException`)

## Don't
- Do NOT change the bottom navigation bar
- Do NOT change the theme toggle or logout logic
- Do NOT modify any other screens
- Do NOT add any new packages
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping 'Administrar perfil' in the 3-dot menu navigates to ProfileScreen (MUST WORK)
2. GlobalKey<NavigatorState> used for navigation instead of context-based Navigator.push
3. ProfileScreen shows existing fields (email read-only, name, room, save)
4. ProfileScreen has 'Cambiar Contraseña' section with current/new/confirm fields
5. Password change verifies current password first via signInWithPassword
6. Password change calls updateUser with new password on success
7. Error shown if current password is wrong
