title: Epic: Announcement toggle, user libre status, theme persistence
description:
## Goals
Fix 5 user-reported UX issues in Limpy:

1. **Announcement toggle** (DONE — 8yr.1): Admin can reactivate inactive announcements via Activar/Desactivar buttons.
2. **Notifications filtering** (DONE — 8yr.2): NotificationsScreen only shows active announcements (already uses getActiveAnnouncements).
3. **isResponsible centralization** (8yr.3): Compute isResponsible once in app.dart, pass to ActivitiesScreen and CommentsScreen. Fixes compilation error.
4. **Theme persistence** (8yr.4): Save theme preference to SharedPreferences so it survives app restart.
5. **Dark mode NotificationsScreen** (8yr.5): Fix CupertinoNavigationBar in NotificationsScreen to use dark colors in dark mode.
6. **Password section collapse** (8yr.6): Collapse password change section after successful password change in SettingsScreen.

## Current State
- 8yr.1 and 8yr.2 are CLOSED (already implemented)
- app.dart has a compilation error: ActivitiesScreen requires isResponsible but it is not passed
- shared_preferences is NOT in pubspec.yaml yet
- NotificationsScreen CupertinoNavigationBar uses hardcoded light colors
- Password section in SettingsScreen is always visible

## Success Criteria

- Theme preference survives app restart
- Password fields collapse after successful change
- Dark mode looks correct on NotificationsScreen

title: Fix dark mode CupertinoNavigationBar in NotificationsScreen
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do
The NotificationsScreen CupertinoNavigationBar does not respect dark mode. It uses a hardcoded semi-transparent surface color that looks washed out in dark mode. Apply the same dark mode treatment used in app.dart _buildMainShell().

### Current code (lines 141-147):
```dart
navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
    middle: const Text("Notificaciones"),
    backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
    border: null,
),
```

### Replace with:
```dart
navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
    middle: Text(
      "Notificaciones",
      style: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
          ? const TextStyle(color: Colors.white)
          : null,
    ),
    backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
        ? Colors.black
        : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
    brightness: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
        ? Brightness.dark
        : null,
    border: null,
    automaticBackgroundVisibility:
        Theme.of(context).brightness != Brightness.dark,
    enableBackgroundFilterBlur:
        Theme.of(context).brightness != Brightness.dark,
),
```

This matches the pattern used in app.dart lines 361-396 for the main shell CupertinoNavigationBar.

## Dont
- Change any other screen files
- Change the body content or announcement cards
- Change the sorting logic
acceptance_criteria:
1. In dark mode, NotificationsScreen nav bar background is solid black (Colors.black)
2. In dark mode, nav bar title text is white
3. In dark mode, blur and automatic background visibility are disabled
4. In light mode, nav bar still uses semi-transparent surface color with blur
5. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test

title: Epic: Notifications fix, cycle reorder, feedback system, announcements, and prórroga swap safeguard
description:
## Goals
Six areas of work:

### 1. Fix cleaning countdown notifications — only during active period
Currently scheduleCleaningCountdown fires notifications for all 3 days starting from startDate, even if some days are in the past. Should only send for days within the active cleaning period (today and future days within the period).

### 2. Cycle generator — allow admin to reorder users
Currently users in the cycle generator appear in alphabetical order with no way to reorder. Add drag-to-reorder (ReorderableListView) so admin can set the rotation order.

### 3. Fix comment notifications — ensure all important notifications fire
Comment notifications only fire when the responsible user has the comments screen open (they're triggered in CommentsScreen._loadData). They should fire from the realtime service or home screen so they work even when the user is on a different screen. Also ensure prórroga notifications work reliably.

### 4. Admin announcements system — app feedback + broadcast messages
- Add 'Comentarios de la App' menu item in 3-dot menu (admin only)
- Create feedback table in DB for anonymous app feedback
- All users can send anonymous feedback from 3-dot menu
- Admin sees all feedback + can send announcements (avisos) and update notifications
- Announcements appear as banners on home screen (like prórroga banners)
- Avisos are text-only; update notifications have a download link button

### 5. Fix prórroga swap — only swap ONE period per user
User reports that accepting a prórroga swaps ALL periods of both users instead of just one. Add safeguard to ensure only the immediate period (max cleaningPeriodDays entries) is swapped.

### 6. Anonymous app feedback from 3-dot menu
- Add 'Enviar Comentario' menu item for all users
- Opens a dialog to send anonymous feedback about the app
- Stored in the feedback table

## Context
- Realtime service has onCommentsChanged stream but it's only consumed in CommentsScreen
- NotificationService is a singleton accessible from anywhere
- CycleGenerator page uses widget.users order (alphabetical from getUsers)
- _findPeriodSchedules walks consecutive same-user schedules — may need a cap

## Success Criteria
- Cleaning notifications only fire during active period days
- Admin can reorder users in cycle generator
- Comment notifications fire even when not on comments screen
- Feedback table exists, users can send anonymous feedback
- Admin can send announcements visible on all users' home screens
- Prórroga swap only affects one period per user
- All UI text in Spanish
close_reason: Closed

title: Redesign comments screen from ExpansionTile cascade to flat chat-style bubbles
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do
Replace the current ExpansionTile-based comment display with a flat chat-style layout. The current design uses Card+ExpansionTile for each parent comment, with replies hidden inside the expansion. This creates a cascading/accordion feel. Replace it with a WhatsApp/iMessage-style chat view.

### Sender View (_buildSenderView, lines 211-293 + _buildSentCommentTile 296-360)
Replace the ExpansionTile approach. For each comment+replies pair, render as a chat conversation:
1. Show the user's sent message as a RIGHT-aligned bubble (colorScheme.primaryContainer background, borderRadius 20, max width 75% of screen)
2. Show each reply from the responsible user as a LEFT-aligned bubble (colorScheme.secondaryContainer background, borderRadius 20, max width 75% of screen)
3. Show timestamps below each bubble in small gray text
4. Messages should be ordered chronologically (parent first, then replies by createdAt)
5. Keep the send form at the TOP (TextField + FilledButton) — same as current
6. Keep the 'Mis comentarios' section header
7. Keep the responsible info Card at top

### Inbox View (_buildInboxView 363-396 + _buildInboxCommentTile 399-501)
Replace the ExpansionTile approach. For each comment+replies pair:
1. Show the anonymous comment as a LEFT-aligned bubble (colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest background, borderRadius 20, max width 75% of screen). Show 'Anónimo' label above in small text.
2. Show each reply from the responsible (current user) as a RIGHT-aligned bubble (colorScheme.primaryContainer background, borderRadius 20, max width 75% of screen). Show 'Tú' label above.
3. Chronological order within each conversation
4. Keep the quick-reply suggestion chips BELOW each conversation (not inside an expansion)
5. Keep the reply TextField + send IconButton below the chips
6. Separate each conversation with a Divider or SizedBox(height: 16)

### Bubble Widget
Create a private helper method `_buildChatBubble`:
```dart
Widget _buildChatBubble({
  required String message,
  required String time,
  required bool isMe,
  String? senderLabel,
})
```
- isMe=true → Align right, primaryContainer color
- isMe=false → Align left, secondaryContainer (sender view) or surfaceContainerHighest (inbox view)
- Container with padding 12, borderRadius 20, maxWidth constraints (0.75 * screen width)
- Show senderLabel if provided (small bold text above message)
- Show time below message in bodySmall

### Use CupertinoIcons
Replace any remaining Material Icons in this file:
- Icons.message_outlined → CupertinoIcons.bubble_left (line 220)
- Icons.inbox → CupertinoIcons.tray (line 372)
- Icons.person → CupertinoIcons.person_fill (line 239)
- Icons.person_off → CupertinoIcons.person_crop_circle_badge_xmark (line 412)
- Icons.send (lines 269, 491) → CupertinoIcons.paperplane_fill

## Don't
- Change the data model or how comments are loaded/sent
- Remove the quick-reply suggestion chips
- Change the _sendComment or _sendReply methods
- Remove the responsible info card from sender view
- Use ListView.builder (keep SingleChildScrollView since comment count is small)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Sender view shows chat bubbles: user messages right-aligned, responsible replies left-aligned — no ExpansionTile
2. Inbox view shows chat bubbles: anonymous messages left-aligned, responsible replies right-aligned — no ExpansionTile
3. Quick-reply chips still visible and functional below each inbox conversation
4. Reply TextField + send button still present and functional
5. All Material Icons in comments_screen.dart replaced with CupertinoIcons
6. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 1f19ead Redesign comments screen to flat chat-style bubbles

title: Epic: Prórroga full-period swap — swap entire cleaning periods instead of single days
description:
## Goals
When a user requests a prórroga and it is accepted, ALL schedule entries in both users' cleaning periods must be swapped — not just one day from each.

### Current behavior (broken):
- Extension request references a single schedule_id (one day)
- acceptExtensionRequest() swaps user_id on ONE requester schedule + ONE next-user schedule
- This fragments the 3-day period grouping in the calendar (e.g., after swap: User A has 1 day, User B has 2 days of what was A's period)

### Desired behavior:
- When requesting a prórroga, the system identifies the requester's full period (all consecutive same-user schedules around the referenced schedule)
- When accepting, ALL schedule rows in the requester's period swap user_id with ALL schedule rows in the next user's period
- The calendar shows complete swapped cards — no fragmentation
- If periods have different lengths, handle gracefully (swap min(len1, len2) days, or pad)

### Scope:
1. Fix acceptExtensionRequest() in SupabaseService to swap full periods
2. Fix _requestExtension() in HomeScreen to properly identify the full period
3. Update calendar display to correctly show swapped periods
4. Update extension_requests_screen to show period dates (not just creation date)
5. Add tests for the period swap logic

### Constraints:
- Do NOT change the extension_requests DB schema (schedule_id still references one row — we use it as an anchor to find the full period)
- The swap must be idempotent — swapping twice should not corrupt data
- All UI text in Spanish
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Fix prórroga swap — correct date reordering, visual indicators, and swap detection
description:
## Context
When a prórroga (extension) is accepted, the two users' cleaning periods should swap: User 1 gets User 2's dates and vice versa. The swapped entries must be visually marked and correctly reordered in both list and calendar views.

## Current Behavior
The swap logic in acceptExtensionRequest() correctly swaps user_id values between the two periods. However:
1. The swap detection logic in calendar_screen.dart (_findPeriodScheduleIds) does NOT use maxSize, risking over-collection of adjacent same-user schedules after the swap
2. The admin extension_requests_screen.dart _periodDates() method shows incorrect date ranges after swap because it walks by the anchor's current (post-swap) userId
3. The visual 'changed' indicator needs to be more prominent — a swap icon overlay on the period card
4. The calendar grid view needs a clearer swap indicator

## Success Criteria
- After a swap, the list view shows the two swapped periods in correct chronological order with the new user assignments
- Both swapped periods display a clear 'changed' visual indicator (swap icon + colored highlight)
- The calendar grid view shows swap indicators on affected dates
- The admin extension requests screen shows correct period dates even after the swap
- All existing tests pass; new tests cover swap detection edge cases
- Only the first/current cycle is affected; subsequent cycles remain unchanged

## Scope
Files: calendar_screen.dart, extension_requests_screen.dart, supabase_service_swap_test.dart
No changes to the swap logic itself (acceptExtensionRequest) — it already works correctly.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix _findPeriodScheduleIds in calendar_screen to use maxSize and handle post-swap userId
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do
The `_findPeriodScheduleIds(CleaningSchedule anchor)` method (line 277) walks backward/forward from the anchor collecting consecutive same-user schedules, but does NOT cap the result with `maxSize`. After a prórroga swap, if the same user has adjacent periods with no gap, this method over-collects schedules from multiple periods.

### Changes required:
1. Add an optional `int? maxSize` parameter to `_findPeriodScheduleIds`, defaulting to `SupabaseService.cleaningPeriodDays` (which is 3).
2. Add date-range bounding logic identical to `_findPeriodSchedules` in `supabase_service.dart` (lines 451-509):
   - Compute `earliestDate = anchorDate - (maxDays - 1)` and `latestDate = anchorDate + (maxDays - 1)`
   - Stop backward/forward walk if date is outside the allowed range
   - Stop if `result.length >= maxSize`
3. Update all call sites of `_findPeriodScheduleIds` (line 258 in `_getRequestForSchedule`) to pass `maxSize: SupabaseService.cleaningPeriodDays`.

### Edge cases:
- Anchor not found in list → return `{anchor.id}` (already handled)
- Period shorter than maxSize → return all found (already handled)
- Adjacent same-user periods with no gap → maxSize prevents crossing into next period

## Don't
- Change the `_findNextUserPeriodIds` method (it already works correctly by position)
- Change the swap logic in `supabase_service.dart`
- Change any visual rendering code in this task
acceptance_criteria:
1. _findPeriodScheduleIds has maxSize parameter defaulting to SupabaseService.cleaningPeriodDays
2. Backward walk stops when result.length >= maxSize or date outside range
3. Forward walk stops when result.length >= maxSize or date outside range
4. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
5. flutter analyze passes with no errors
6. flutter test passes (all existing tests)
close_reason: 392d3f9 Fix _findPeriodScheduleIds to use maxSize and date-range bounding

title: Add unit tests for swap detection logic in calendar_screen after prórroga acceptance
description:
## Files
- test/screens/calendar_swap_detection_test.dart (create)

## What to do
Create a new test file that unit-tests the swap detection logic used by CalendarScreen. Since the methods `_findPeriodScheduleIds` and `_findNextUserPeriodIds` are private to `_CalendarScreenState`, replicate them as standalone functions (same pattern as `test/data/supabase_service_swap_test.dart` which replicates `_findPeriodSchedules`).

### Functions to replicate and test:
1. `findPeriodScheduleIds(List<CleaningSchedule> schedules, CleaningSchedule anchor, {int? maxSize})` — returns `Set<String>` of schedule IDs in the period around anchor.
2. `findNextUserPeriodIds(List<CleaningSchedule> schedules, Set<String> requesterPeriodIds, String nextUserId)` — returns `Set<String>` of IDs in the next period after the requester's.
3. `getRequestForSchedule(CleaningSchedule schedule, List<CleaningSchedule> schedules, List<ExtensionRequest> requests)` — returns the matching `ExtensionRequest?` for a schedule.

### Test cases to write:
1. **Pre-swap detection**: Given schedules [A:Mar7-9, B:Mar14-16] and a pending request anchored at A:Mar8, verify all 3 A-schedules return the request.
2. **Post-swap detection**: Given schedules after swap [B:Mar7-9, A:Mar14-16] and an accepted request (scheduleId=original anchor, requesterId=A, nextUserId=B), verify:
   - All 3 schedules in Mar7-9 (now userId=B) return the request
   - All 3 schedules in Mar14-16 (now userId=A) return the request
3. **maxSize prevents over-collection**: Given 6 consecutive same-user schedules [A:Mar1-6], anchor at Mar1, maxSize=3 → only {Mar1,Mar2,Mar3} returned.
4. **Post-swap with adjacent periods**: Given schedules after swap where the swapped user has adjacent periods [B:Mar7-9, B:Mar10-12(different period)], maxSize=3 prevents collecting Mar10-12.
5. **No matching request**: Schedule not in any request's period → returns null.
6. **Rejected request**: Schedule in a rejected request's period → still returns the request (for display purposes).

### Helper:
Use the same `_schedule` helper as in `supabase_service_swap_test.dart`:
```dart
CleaningSchedule _schedule(String id, String userId, DateTime date) {
  return CleaningSchedule(id: id, userId: userId, date: date);
}
```

For ExtensionRequest, create a helper:
```dart
ExtensionRequest _request(String id, String scheduleId, String requesterId, String nextUserId, ExtensionRequestStatus status) {
  return ExtensionRequest(id: id, scheduleId: scheduleId, requesterId: requesterId, nextUserId: nextUserId, status: status, createdAt: DateTime.now());
}
```

## Don't
- Import or depend on Flutter widgets (this is pure Dart logic testing)
- Use `flutter_test` widget testing — use only `package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart` for `test()` and `expect()`
- Modify any production code
acceptance_criteria:
1. Test file created at test/screens/calendar_swap_detection_test.dart
2. At least 6 test cases covering pre-swap, post-swap, maxSize, adjacent periods, no match, and rejected request scenarios
3. All replicated functions match the logic in calendar_screen.dart (with maxSize fix from task coz.1)
4. flutter test test/screens/calendar_swap_detection_test.dart passes
5. flutter test passes (all tests)
6. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
7. flutter analyze passes
close_reason: 7dd3db6 Add unit tests for swap detection logic in calendar_screen

title: Epic: Limpy v2 — Renaming, UX fixes, real-time updates, notifications, and UI redesign
description:
## Goals
This epic covers all v2 improvements for the Limpy app:
1. Branding: Replace all CleanApp/flutter_cleanapp display names with Limpy
2. Admin panel fixes: Fix task ordering (ascending), add description field to tasks
3. Cleaning period: Change from weekly to 3-day periods with auto-date generation and countdown notifications
4. Real-time updates: App data should refresh without closing/reopening using Supabase Realtime
5. Prorroga calendar sync: When a prorroga is accepted, calendar dates must update and show swap icon
6. Notifications: Local notifications for comments, cleaning period countdown, and important events
7. UI/UX redesign: 3-dot menu in AppBar (theme, logout, profile), profile management screen, floating bottom nav bar

## Context
- Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Realtime)
- State management: StatefulWidget + setState (no Provider/Bloc)
- The tasks table needs a description_task TEXT column added in the DB
- No notification system exists yet — needs flutter_local_notifications package
- No real-time subscriptions exist — only manual fetch-on-load pattern

## Success Criteria
- All user-facing strings say Limpy, zero instances of CleanApp as display text
- Tasks appear 1,2,3... (ascending) in admin panel with editable descriptions
- Admin picks 1 date and 3 consecutive days auto-created; countdown notifications sent
- Data refreshes in real-time across all screens without app restart
- Accepted prorrogas update calendar entries with swap icon
- Local notifications for comments, cleaning reminders, and prorrogas
- AppBar has 3-dot menu; profile edit screen exists; bottom nav is floating with rounded corners
close_reason: All 12 tasks completed. Integration review passed. Quality gate green.

title: Replace AppBar actions with 3-dot PopupMenuButton
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

Replace the current AppBar actions (logout IconButton + theme toggle IconButton) with a single 3-dot PopupMenuButton (Icons.more_vert) that contains menu items for:
1. Cambiar tema (with current theme icon)
2. Administrar perfil (new — navigates to ProfileScreen, created in a separate task)
3. Cerrar sesion

### Current AppBar actions (lines 151-161):
```dart
actions: [
  IconButton(
    icon: const Icon(Icons.logout),
    onPressed: _logout,
    tooltip: 'Cerrar sesion',
  ),
  IconButton(
    icon: Icon(_themeModeIcon),
    onPressed: _toggleTheme,
    tooltip: 'Cambiar tema',
  ),
],
```

### Replace with:
```dart
actions: [
  PopupMenuButton<String>(
    icon: const Icon(Icons.more_vert),
    tooltip: 'Menu',
    onSelected: (value) {
      switch (value) {
        case 'theme':
          _toggleTheme();
        case 'profile':
          Navigator.push(
            context,
            MaterialPageRoute(
              builder: (_) => ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
            ),
          );
        case 'logout':
          _logout();
      }
    },
    itemBuilder: (context) => [
      PopupMenuItem(
        value: 'theme',
        child: ListTile(
          leading: Icon(_themeModeIcon),
          title: const Text('Cambiar tema'),
          contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
        ),
      ),
      PopupMenuItem(
        value: 'profile',
        child: const ListTile(
          leading: Icon(Icons.person),
          title: Text('Administrar perfil'),
          contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
        ),
      ),
      const PopupMenuDivider(),
      PopupMenuItem(
        value: 'logout',
        child: ListTile(
          leading: Icon(Icons.logout, color: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.error),
          title: Text('Cerrar sesion', style: TextStyle(color: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.error)),
          contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
        ),
      ),
    ],
  ),
],
```

### Import:
Add import for ProfileScreen (will be created in task .11). For now, if ProfileScreen doesn't exist yet, create a placeholder:
```dart
// Temporary — will be replaced by task .11
// If ProfileScreen doesn't exist, comment out the profile navigation and just show a SnackBar
```

Actually, to avoid blocking: if ProfileScreen doesn't exist yet, navigate to a simple placeholder or show a SnackBar saying 'Proximamente'. The profile task (.11) will create the actual screen.

### Approach for independence:
Since this task may run before the ProfileScreen task, handle the profile case gracefully:
- Try to import ProfileScreen. If it doesn't exist, use a TODO comment and show a SnackBar instead.
- Better approach: create a minimal stub file lib/screens/profile_screen.dart with just a Scaffold that says 'Administrar Perfil - Proximamente'. The full implementation will be done in task .11.

## Don't
- Do NOT remove the theme toggle functionality — move it into the menu
- Do NOT remove the logout functionality — move it into the menu
- Do NOT change the AppBar title
- Do NOT modify the bottom navigation bar

title: Make bottom navigation bar floating with rounded corners
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart (modify — optional)

## What to do

Redesign the bottom NavigationBar to be floating with rounded corners, giving it a modern card-like appearance.

### Current NavigationBar (app.dart lines 165-190):
Uses the default Material 3 NavigationBar which sits flush at the bottom of the screen.

### Replace with floating design:
Wrap the NavigationBar in a Container with margin and decoration to make it float:

```dart
bottomNavigationBar: Padding(
  padding: const EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 0, 16, 16),
  child: ClipRRect(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(24),
    child: NavigationBar(
      selectedIndex: _currentIndex,
      onDestinationSelected: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
      destinations: const [
        NavigationDestination(
          icon: Icon(Icons.home_outlined),
          selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.home),
          label: 'Inicio',
        ),
        NavigationDestination(
          icon: Icon(Icons.checklist_outlined),
          selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.checklist),
          label: 'Actividades',
        ),
        NavigationDestination(
          icon: Icon(Icons.calendar_month_outlined),
          selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.calendar_month),
          label: 'Calendario',
        ),
        NavigationDestination(
          icon: Icon(Icons.comment_outlined),
          selectedIcon: Icon(Icons.comment),
          label: 'Comentarios',
        ),
      ],
    ),
  ),
),
```

### Alternative approach using Container + decoration:
```dart
bottomNavigationBar: Container(
  margin: const EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 0, 16, 16),
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(24),
    boxShadow: [
      BoxShadow(
        color: Theme.of(context).shadowColor.withValues(alpha: 0.15),
        blurRadius: 8,
        offset: const Offset(0, 2),
      ),
    ],
  ),
  child: ClipRRect(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(24),
    child: NavigationBar(
      selectedIndex: _currentIndex,
      onDestinationSelected: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
      height: 70,
      destinations: const [
        // ... same destinations as above
      ],
    ),
  ),
),
```

### Theme update (optional) — lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart:
If the NavigationBar theme needs adjustment for the floating look, update the NavigationBarThemeData:
```dart
navigationBarTheme: NavigationBarThemeData(
  height: 70,
  indicatorShape: RoundedRectangleBorder(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(16),
  ),
),
```

## Don't
- Do NOT change the navigation destinations (icons, labels)
- Do NOT change the navigation logic (IndexedStack, _currentIndex)
- Do NOT use a BottomNavigationBar (old Material 2) — keep NavigationBar (Material 3)
- Do NOT add any new tabs
- Do NOT use deprecated withOpacity — use withValues(alpha: ...) instead
acceptance_criteria:
1. Bottom navigation bar has rounded corners (borderRadius >= 20)
2. Bottom navigation bar has horizontal and bottom margin (appears floating)
3. Navigation bar has a subtle shadow for depth
4. All 4 navigation destinations still work correctly
5. Selected/unselected icons still toggle correctly
6. Navigation bar looks good in both light and dark themes
7. No overflow or clipping issues on different screen sizes
8. flutter analyze reports no issues
9. flutter test — all tests pass
10. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes

title: Fix task ordering to ascending in admin panel
description:
## Files
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify)

## What to do

The tasks in the admin panel currently appear in the correct ascending order from the DB (ORDER BY sort_order ASC is the default). However, the Supabase .order() method defaults to ascending=true, which is correct.

Verify the current behavior: the `.order('sort_order')` calls in getTasks() (line 137) and getAllTasks() (line 146) already use ascending order by default. The issue may be that the sort_order values in the database are inverted or that the ReorderableListView in the admin panel is displaying them incorrectly.

### Investigation needed:
1. Check if the `.order('sort_order')` Supabase call returns ascending by default — it does (ascending: true is default)
2. The admin panel uses ReorderableListView which renders items in list order — so if the DB returns [1,2,3...], they should appear 1,2,3 top to bottom

### Explicit fix — make ascending explicit:
In lib/data/supabase_service.dart:
- Line 137: change `.order('sort_order')` to `.order('sort_order', ascending: true)`
- Line 146: change `.order('sort_order')` to `.order('sort_order', ascending: true)`

This makes the intent explicit and ensures task 1 appears at the top, task 2 below it, etc.

## Don't
- Do NOT change the ReorderableListView logic
- Do NOT change the sort_order values in the database
- Do NOT modify the task model
acceptance_criteria:
1. getTasks() in supabase_service.dart uses .order('sort_order', ascending: true)
2. getAllTasks() in supabase_service.dart uses .order('sort_order', ascending: true)
3. Tasks with sort_order 1 appear at the top of the admin list, sort_order 2 below, etc.

title: Implement 3-day cleaning period with auto-date generation
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify)
- lib/models/cleaning_schedule.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

Change the cleaning period system from weekly (1 date per schedule) to a 3-day period. When admin selects a date, 3 consecutive schedule entries are auto-created. The period is configurable but defaults to 3 days.

### 1. Schedule Management — lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart:
In `_showAddDialog()`:
- After admin picks a user and a date, auto-create 3 consecutive schedule entries (the selected date + 2 more days)
- Replace the single `createSchedule()` call with a loop that creates 3 schedules:
  ```dart
  for (var i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    final date = selectedDate.add(Duration(days: i));
    await SupabaseService.instance.createSchedule(
      CleaningSchedule(id: '', userId: selectedUser!.id, date: date),
    );
  }
  ```
- Add a label in the dialog: 'Se crearán 3 días consecutivos de aseo a partir de la fecha seleccionada'

### 2. Home Screen — lib/screens/home_screen.dart:
Change the current week detection logic to work with 3-day periods instead of weekly:
- Instead of finding the schedule for the current Monday-Sunday week, find any schedule entry where the date matches today or is within the user's active cleaning period
- The user is 'responsible' if there exists a schedule entry with their userId where the date is today or within the last 2 days (i.e., the 3-day window starting from the first date)
- Group consecutive schedule entries for the same user to determine the cleaning period

Simpler approach: find all schedules for the current user where date >= today - 2 days AND date <= today. If any exist and are not completed, the user is responsible.

Update the date display: instead of 'Semana del DD/MM al DD/MM', show 'Periodo de aseo: DD/MM - DD/MM' (first date to last date of the 3-day period).

### 3. Calendar Screen — lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart:
- Update the grouping logic: instead of grouping by week, group consecutive dates for the same user as a single 'period'
- Show 'Periodo del DD/MM al DD/MM' instead of 'Semana del DD/MM/YYYY'
- The 'current period' highlight should apply to any schedule entry where the date falls within today's active period

### 4. Service — lib/data/supabase_service.dart:
- Update `getCurrentWeekSchedule()` to `getCurrentPeriodSchedules()`:
  ```dart
  Future<List<CleaningSchedule>> getCurrentPeriodSchedules() async {
    final now = DateTime.now();
    final today = DateTime(now.year, now.month, now.day);
    final threeDaysAgo = today.subtract(const Duration(days: 2));
    final tomorrow = today.add(const Duration(days: 1));
    final data = await SupabaseConfig.client
        .from('schedules')
        .select()
        .gte('date', threeDaysAgo.toIso8601String().split('T').first)
        .lt('date', tomorrow.toIso8601String().split('T').first)
        .order('date');
    return data.map((json) => CleaningSchedule.fromJson(json)).toList();
  }
  ```

## Don't
- Do NOT delete existing schedule entries
- Do NOT change the schedule table schema
- Do NOT remove the weekly grouping entirely — just adapt it to 3-day periods
- Do NOT hardcode the period length everywhere — use a constant: `static const int cleaningPeriodDays = 3;` in SupabaseService or a config
acceptance_criteria:
1. Admin picks 1 date and 3 consecutive schedule entries are created for the same user
2. Home screen shows 'responsible' status when user has a schedule entry within the current 3-day period
3. Home screen displays period dates (start to end) instead of week dates
4. Calendar groups consecutive same-user dates as a single period
5. getCurrentPeriodSchedules() returns schedules within the 3-day window
6. Dialog shows informational text about 3-day auto-creation
7. flutter analyze reports no issues
8. flutter test — all tests pass
9. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
close_reason: Closed

title: Redesign sender view as WhatsApp-style chat with input bar at bottom
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do
Redesign the sender view (`_buildSenderView`) to look like a WhatsApp chat. Currently the input is at the TOP and messages are below in a ScrollView. Flip this: input bar pinned at BOTTOM, messages scrolling above.

### New layout structure for _buildSenderView
Replace the current `SingleChildScrollView` with a `Column` that uses `Expanded` for the message area:

```dart
Widget _buildSenderView() {
  return Column(
    children: [
      // 1. Small header with responsible info (compact)
      _buildSenderHeader(),
      // 2. Expanded scrollable message area
      Expanded(child: _buildSenderMessages()),
      // 3. Input bar pinned at bottom
      _buildSenderInputBar(),
    ],
  );
}
```

### _buildSenderHeader()
A compact bar showing the responsible user info. Use a Container with padding 12, showing:
- If `_responsible != null`: Row with CircleAvatar(CupertinoIcons.person_fill) + Column("Responsable: {name}", "{room}")
- If null: Text("No hay responsable asignado esta semana")
- Add a bottom Divider

### _buildSenderMessages()
A `ListView` (not SingleChildScrollView) that shows all conversations chronologically:
- Use `reverse: true` on the ListView so newest messages appear at the bottom (WhatsApp behavior)
- Reverse the entries list before passing to ListView so the order is correct
- For each entry in `_myCommentsWithReplies`:
  - Show the user's sent comment as a RIGHT-aligned bubble (isMe: true)
  - Show each reply as a LEFT-aligned bubble (isMe: false, senderLabel: responsible name)
  - Between conversations, add a small centered date/time chip or just SizedBox(height: 16)
- If `_myCommentsWithReplies` is empty, show a centered empty state: CupertinoIcons.bubble_left icon + "Envía un comentario anónimo" text
- Keep the existing `_buildChatBubble` helper method — reuse it

### _buildSenderInputBar()
A Container pinned at the bottom with:
- SafeArea(top: false) wrapper to handle bottom insets
- Container with padding and a top border (Divider or BoxDecoration border)
- Row containing:
  - Expanded TextField (single line, maxLength: 280, no counter, hintText: "Escribe un comentario...", rounded border)
  - SizedBox(width: 8)
  - IconButton or CupertinoButton with CupertinoIcons.paperplane_fill, calls _sendComment
- Remove the old multi-line TextField and FilledButton

### Update _sendComment
After successfully sending, also scroll to the bottom of the ListView. Use a `ScrollController` on the ListView and call `animateTo(0, ...)` (since the list is reversed, 0 is the bottom).

### Keep existing methods unchanged
- `_buildChatBubble` — keep as-is
- `_loadData` — keep as-is
- `_sendComment` — keep logic, just add scroll-to-bottom
- `_formatTime` — keep as-is
- All realtime subscriptions — keep as-is

## Don't

title: Create CommentChatScreen for responsible user individual conversation view
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/comment_chat_screen.dart (create)

## What to do
Create a new screen that the responsible user opens when tapping on an anonymous conversation. This is a WhatsApp-style individual chat view.

### Constructor parameters
```dart
class CommentChatScreen extends StatefulWidget {
  final Comment parentComment;
  final List<Comment> initialReplies;
  final CleaningSchedule schedule;
  final UserModel currentUser;
  
  const CommentChatScreen({
    super.key,
    required this.parentComment,
    required this.initialReplies,
    required this.schedule,
    required this.currentUser,
  });
}
```

### Layout — full WhatsApp-style chat
```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ CupertinoNavigationBar       │
│ ← Back    "Comentario anónimo"│
├──────────────────────────────┤
│                              │
│  [Anónimo bubble - left]     │
│                              │
│        [Tú bubble - right]   │
│                              │
│  [Anónimo bubble - left]     │
│                              │
│        [Tú bubble - right]   │
│                              │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│ Quick reply chips (Wrap)     │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│ [TextField        ] [Send]   │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

### Screen structure
Use a `Scaffold` (not CupertinoPageScaffold — we need SnackBar support) with:
- `appBar`: CupertinoNavigationBar with `middle: Text('Comentario anónimo')`, backgroundColor using `Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: isDark ? 1.0 : 0.85)`, border: null
- `body`: Column with:
  1. `Expanded` → ListView (reverse: true) showing all messages as chat bubbles
  2. Quick-reply suggestion chips in a Wrap (same 5 chips as current: "Ok, enterado 👍", "Entendido ✅", "Trabajo en eso 🔧", "¡Gracias por avisar! 😊", "Lo reviso pronto 🔍")
  3. Input bar at bottom (SafeArea wrapper)

### Messages in the ListView
- The `parentComment` is always the FIRST message (left-aligned, "Anónimo" label, surfaceContainerHighest background)
- Replies are shown chronologically after it
- Responsible's replies (senderId == currentUser.id) → right-aligned, primaryContainer, "Tú" label
- Any other messages → left-aligned, surfaceContainerHighest, "Anónimo" label
- Use a `_buildChatBubble` method identical to the one in comments_screen.dart (copy it — don't import from comments_screen since it's a private method)

### State management
- Store replies in a local `List<Comment> _replies` initialized from `widget.initialReplies`
- Subscribe to `RealtimeService.instance.onCommentsChanged` to reload replies
- On reload: call `SupabaseService.instance.getRepliesForComment(widget.parentComment.id)` to get fresh replies
- Use a `ScrollController` for the ListView

### Send reply
- TextField with single line, hintText: "Escribe una respuesta...", rounded border
- Send button: IconButton with CupertinoIcons.paperplane_fill
- On send: call `SupabaseService.instance.sendComment(widget.schedule.id, text, senderId: widget.currentUser.id, parentId: widget.parentComment.id)`
- Clear TextField after send
- Scroll to bottom (animateTo 0 since reversed)

### Quick-reply chips
- Wrap widget with 5 ActionChip widgets (same as current comments_screen)
- Tapping a chip fills the TextField with the chip text (same behavior)
- Show chips in a Container with horizontal padding 16 and vertical padding 8
- Add a top Divider above the chips

### Imports needed
```dart
import 'dart:async';
import 'package:flutter/cupertino.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/core/realtime_service.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/data/supabase_service.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/cleaning_schedule.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/comment.dart';
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/user_model.dart';
```

## Don't
- Import or reference comments_screen.dart (this is a standalone screen)
- Use ExpansionTile or Card wrappers for messages
- Add navigation logic (the parent screen handles pushing this route)
- Use CupertinoPageScaffold (need Scaffold for SnackBar)
acceptance_criteria:
1. CommentChatScreen file exists at lib/screens/comment_chat_screen.dart
2. Screen shows CupertinoNavigationBar with 'Comentario anónimo' title and back button
3. Messages displayed as chat bubbles: anonymous left-aligned, responsible right-aligned
4. Input bar pinned at bottom with TextField + send button
5. Quick-reply chips visible between messages and input bar
6. Realtime subscription reloads replies when comments table changes
7. Send reply works via SupabaseService.sendComment with correct parentId
8. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 2e672f2 Add CommentChatScreen for responsible user individual conversation view

title: Redesign responsible inbox as conversation list that navigates to CommentChatScreen
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do
Redesign the responsible user's inbox view (`_buildInboxView`) to show a WhatsApp-style conversation list. Each anonymous comment becomes a conversation preview card. Tapping opens the CommentChatScreen.

### New _buildInboxView layout
Replace the current SingleChildScrollView with a Column:
```dart
Widget _buildInboxView() {
  return Column(
    children: [
      // Compact header
      _buildInboxHeader(),
      // Conversation list
      Expanded(child: _buildConversationList()),
    ],
  );
}
```

### _buildInboxHeader()
A compact header showing:
- Icon CupertinoIcons.tray + "Buzón de Comentarios" title
- Subtitle: "{count} conversaciones" or "No tienes comentarios aún"
- Padding 16, centered, with bottom Divider

### _buildConversationList()
A `ListView.separated` showing each conversation as a tappable card/tile:

For each entry in `_commentsWithReplies`:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 👤  Comentario anónimo    Hace 2h   │
│     "Preview del mensaje..."         │
│     💬 2 respuestas                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Each item is a `ListTile` wrapped in a `Card`:
- `leading`: CircleAvatar with CupertinoIcons.person_crop_circle_badge_xmark, backgroundColor: surfaceContainerHighest
- `title`: Text("Comentario anónimo")
- `subtitle`: Text with the first ~50 chars of the comment message (truncated with ellipsis) + on a new line: reply count ("💬 {n} respuestas" or "Sin respuesta aún")
- `trailing`: Text with relative time (_formatTime), small gray text
- `onTap`: Navigate to CommentChatScreen via CupertinoPageRoute

### Navigation to CommentChatScreen
On tap, push a CupertinoPageRoute:
```dart
Navigator.of(context).push(
  CupertinoPageRoute(
    builder: (_) => CommentChatScreen(
      parentComment: comment,
      initialReplies: replies,
      schedule: _currentWeekSchedule!,
      currentUser: widget.currentUser,
    ),
  ),
);
```

Import the new screen:
```dart
import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/screens/comment_chat_screen.dart';
```

### Remove old methods
Delete these methods that are no longer needed:
- `_buildInboxCommentConversation` (replaced by conversation list + CommentChatScreen)
- `_buildSuggestionChip` (moved to CommentChatScreen in task 2)
- `_replyControllers` field and its disposal logic in dispose() (replies now happen in CommentChatScreen)

### Keep
- `_buildChatBubble` — still used by sender view
- `_sendReply` — can be removed since replies now happen in CommentChatScreen. Actually remove it since it's no longer called from this file.
- All data loading methods — keep as-is

### Cleanup
After removing the inbox conversation/reply methods, also remove:
- The `_replyControllers` map field (line 43)
- The disposal loop for reply controllers in dispose() (lines 72-74)
- The `_sendReply` method (lines 172-194)

## Don't
- Change the sender view (that's task 1)
- Change the data model or service methods
- Change _loadData or realtime subscriptions
- Change _buildChatBubble or _formatTime
acceptance_criteria:
1. Responsible view shows a list of conversation previews (not inline chats)
2. Each conversation shows: anonymous avatar, message preview, time, reply count
3. Tapping a conversation navigates to CommentChatScreen via CupertinoPageRoute
4. Old inline reply methods (_buildInboxCommentConversation, _buildSuggestionChip, _sendReply, _replyControllers) are removed
5. CommentChatScreen import is present
6. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test

title: Epic: Dark theme bars, floating bottom bar, password reset flow fix, iOS rounded borders
description:
## Issues

### 1. Dark theme — bars stay light
CupertinoNavigationBar and CupertinoTabBar use CupertinoColors.systemBackground which doesn't respect the Material dark theme. Need to use theme-aware colors from Theme.of(context).

### 2. Floating bottom bar with frosted glass + iOS rounded borders
Bring back the floating pill-shaped bottom bar (with margin and border radius) while keeping the frosted glass effect. Increase border radius globally for Cards, InputDecoration, buttons to iOS-style (16px+).

### 3. Password reset deep link not working
The onAuthStateChange listener only handles signedIn/tokenRefreshed/userUpdated but NOT passwordRecovery. When user clicks reset link, Supabase fires AuthChangeEvent.passwordRecovery which is not caught.

### 4. Reply suggestions already implemented
The reply suggestion chips were already added in epic tvs. No action needed.

## Constraints
- Don't modify Supabase logic or auth flow
- All UI text in Spanish
- Quality gate must pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix dark theme bars and make bottom bar floating with frosted glass
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Fix CupertinoNavigationBar dark theme color
The current code uses `CupertinoColors.systemBackground.withValues(alpha: 0.8)` which resolves to white regardless of theme. Replace with theme-aware color.

Change the CupertinoNavigationBar (line 350-365):
```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: Text(_currentTitle),
  trailing: CupertinoButton(
    padding: EdgeInsets.zero,
    child: const Icon(CupertinoIcons.bell),
    onPressed: () {
      _navigatorKey.currentState?.push(
        CupertinoPageRoute(builder: (_) => const NotificationsScreen()),
      );
    },
  ),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(
    alpha: 0.85,
  ),
  border: null,
),
```

### 2. Fix CupertinoTabBar dark theme + make it floating with frosted glass
Replace the entire bottomNavigationBar (lines 367-406) with a floating pill-shaped bar that uses theme-aware colors:

```dart
bottomNavigationBar: Container(
  margin: const EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 0, 16, 16),
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(28),
    boxShadow: [
      BoxShadow(
        color: Theme.of(context).shadowColor.withValues(alpha: 0.1),
        blurRadius: 12,
        offset: const Offset(0, 4),
      ),
    ],
  ),
  child: ClipRRect(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(28),
    child: BackdropFilter(
      filter: ImageFilter.blur(sigmaX: 20.0, sigmaY: 20.0),
      child: CupertinoTabBar(
        currentIndex: _currentIndex,
        onTap: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
        activeColor: CupertinoColors.activeBlue,
        backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(
          alpha: 0.7,
        ),
        items: const [
          BottomNavigationBarItem(
            icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.calendar),
            activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.calendar_today),
            label: '',
          ),
          BottomNavigationBarItem(
            icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.checkmark_square),
            activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.checkmark_square_fill),
            label: '',
          ),
          BottomNavigationBarItem(
            icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.house),
            activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.house_fill),
            label: '',
          ),
          BottomNavigationBarItem(
            icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.chat_bubble_2),
            activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.chat_bubble_2_fill),
            label: '',
          ),
          BottomNavigationBarItem(
            icon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.gear),
            activeIcon: Icon(CupertinoIcons.gear_solid),
            label: '',
          ),
        ],
      ),
    ),
  ),
),
```

Key changes:
- Container with margin (16px sides, 16px bottom) for floating effect
- BorderRadius.circular(28) for pill shape
- Subtle shadow for depth
- ClipRRect to clip the blur to the rounded shape
- BackdropFilter with blur(20, 20) for frosted glass
- `Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface` instead of `CupertinoColors.systemBackground` — this respects dark/light theme

## Don't
- Don't change any other files
- Don't modify the _screens list or any business logic
- Don't change the tab items or their icons
- Don't change the CupertinoNavigationBar trailing button behavior
acceptance_criteria:
1. CupertinoNavigationBar uses Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface for background (dark-theme aware)
2. Bottom bar is floating with 16px margin on sides and bottom
3. Bottom bar has pill shape (BorderRadius.circular(28))
4. Bottom bar has frosted glass effect (BackdropFilter with blur)
5. Bottom bar background uses Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface with alpha 0.7
6. In dark mode, both bars appear dark (not white/light)
7. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test

### 3. Pull-to-refresh on all 4 main tabs
Add RefreshIndicator (swipe-down gesture) to: Calendar, Home, Activities, and Comments screens. All four already have Future<void> reload methods ready.

- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart — legend + pull-to-refresh
- lib/screens/activities_screen.dart — task cards + pull-to-refresh
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart — pull-to-refresh
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart — pull-to-refresh

## Constraints
- All UI text in Spanish
- Quality gate must pass
- Don't change business logic or data models

title: Change calendar legend from horizontal Wrap to vertical card list
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

Replace the horizontal `Wrap` legend at the bottom of the calendar grid view (lines 822-868) with a vertical list of `Card` widgets, one per user.

A `Wrap` with `spacing: 8, runSpacing: 4` containing inline `Row` items with a 12x12 colored circle + text label. Items flow horizontally and wrap.

### New code
Replace the `Wrap` block (lines 824-868) with a `Column` of `Card` widgets:

```dart
if (_users.isNotEmpty)
  Padding(
    padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 16),
    child: Column(
      crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch,
      children: [
        for (final user in _users)
          Card(
            margin: const EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 4),
            child: InkWell(
              onTap: widget.currentUser.isAdmin
                  ? () => _showColorPicker(user)
                  : null,
              borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(12),
              child: Padding(
                padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(
                  horizontal: 12,
                  vertical: 8,
                ),
                child: Row(
                  children: [
                    Container(
                      width: 16,
                      height: 16,
                      decoration: BoxDecoration(
                        color: _getUserColor(
                          user.id,
                        ).withValues(alpha: 0.7),
                        shape: BoxShape.circle,

                    const SizedBox(width: 12),
                    Expanded(
                      child: Text(
                        user.name,

                    Text(
                      user.room,
                      style: textTheme.bodySmall?.copyWith(

                    if (widget.currentUser.isAdmin) ...[

                        Icons.color_lens,
```

### Key changes
1. `Wrap` → `Column` with `crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.stretch`

- Don't change any business logic
- Don't modify any other files

title: Add pull-to-refresh (RefreshIndicator) to Calendar and Home screens
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

### Calendar screen (calendar_screen.dart)

The calendar has two view modes inside an `Expanded` widget (lines 1000-1007):
- Grid mode: `SingleChildScrollView` wrapping `_buildGridView`
- List mode: `ListView.builder` via `_buildListView`

Wrap the `Expanded` child with `RefreshIndicator`:

```dart
Expanded(
  child: RefreshIndicator(
    onRefresh: _loadSchedules,
    child: _isGridView
        ? SingleChildScrollView(
            physics: const AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics(),
            padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8),
            child: _buildGridView(schedules, colorScheme, textTheme),
          )
        : _buildListView(items, colorScheme, textTheme),
  ),
),
```

Key points:
- `_loadSchedules()` already returns `Future<void>` (line 96) — perfect for `onRefresh`
- Add `physics: const AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics()` to the `SingleChildScrollView` in grid mode so pull-to-refresh works even when content doesn't fill the viewport
- The `ListView.builder` in list mode is already scrollable by default, so no physics change needed

### Home screen (home_screen.dart)

The build method (line 918) returns either a loading indicator or the main content. The main content has two states:
- State A (responsible, line 999-1001): `SingleChildScrollView`
- State B (free, line 1099): `SingleChildScrollView`

Both states return a `SingleChildScrollView`. Wrap each with `RefreshIndicator`:

For State A (line 999-1001), change:

title: Epic: Fix prórroga swap scope and notification scheduling
description:
## Goals

### 1. Prórroga swap — ensure only involved periods are swapped
The user reports that accepting a prórroga changes 'all periods' instead of just the two involved periods. While the current code uses maxSize:cleaningPeriodDays (3) to cap the swap, there are edge cases where _findPeriodSchedules could include more schedules than intended if the user has consecutive schedules without gaps (e.g., admin-created schedules). The fix should make the swap more robust by ensuring period boundaries are strictly enforced.

### 2. Notifications — stop firing on every app open
Currently, notifications fire every time the app opens because:
a) scheduleCleaningCountdown() fires an immediate show() for 'today' on every app launch (the _notificationsScheduled guard resets on restart)
b) _loadAnnouncements() fires notifications for ALL active announcements on first load because _knownAnnouncementIds starts empty
c) scheduleAt() uses Future.delayed which is lost when the app is killed — notifications never fire in the background
d) _notifiedIncomingRequestId resets on restart, causing duplicate prórroga notifications

The fix should:
- Use flutter_local_notifications zonedSchedule for reliable background delivery
- Persist notification state in SharedPreferences to prevent duplicates across app restarts
- Only fire notifications for genuinely NEW events, not existing state

## Success Criteria
- Prórroga swap only modifies the exact schedule rows in the two involved periods (requester + next user), never touching other periods or users
- Cleaning countdown notifications appear at 8 AM daily during the responsible period WITHOUT requiring the app to be open
- Opening the app does NOT fire duplicate notifications for already-known announcements, prórrogas, or cleaning reminders
- All tests pass
close_reason: All 3 tasks completed. Quality gate green: 60 tests pass, zero analyze issues, formatting clean.

title: Epic: Cycle-based schedule generation & calendar grid view
description:
## Goals
Add two major features to the calendar/schedule system:

1. **Cycle-based schedule generation (admin)** — When admin manages the calendar, provide a new option to generate schedules by cycles. Parameters:
   - Users: multi-select which users participate in the cycle
   - Start date: when the first period begins
   - Cleaning period per user: number of days (default 3)
   - Number of cycles: how many times to repeat the full rotation

   Each user gets a cleaning period of N days, starting 7 days after the previous user's start date. After all users complete one cycle, the next cycle repeats the same order.

   Example (3 users, 3-day period, start March 7):
   - Cycle 1: User1 Mar 7-9, User2 Mar 14-16, User3 Mar 21-23
   - Cycle 2: User1 Mar 28-30, User2 Apr 4-6, User3 Apr 11-13

2. **Calendar grid view** — Add a toggle in the calendar screen to switch between the current list view and a calendar grid view (month grid showing days with colored indicators for who has cleaning duty).

## Context
- Current schedule management screen (schedule_management_screen.dart) has a simple 'add single date' dialog that creates 3 consecutive days for one user
- Calendar screen (calendar_screen.dart) currently only has a list view with period grouping
- The 7-day gap between users means each user starts on the same weekday
- Schedules are stored as individual date rows in the DB (one row per day)
- SupabaseService.cleaningPeriodDays is currently hardcoded to 3

## Success Criteria
- Admin can generate schedules by cycles with all 4 parameters
- Preview of generated dates shown before confirming
- Calendar screen has a toggle between list and grid views
- Grid view shows a month calendar with colored day indicators
- All existing functionality preserved (edit, delete, prórrogas, realtime)

title: Add cycle generation dialog with preview and batch save to schedule management
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify — add batch create method)

## What to do

Add a new 'Generar por ciclos' option to the schedule management screen. This opens a full-screen dialog/page where the admin configures cycle parameters, previews the generated dates, and confirms to save them all to the database.

### Step 1: Add `createSchedulesBatch` to SupabaseService

Add a new method in the `// --- Schedules ---` section of `lib/data/supabase_service.dart` (after `createSchedule`, around line 141):

```dart
/// Inserts multiple cleaning schedules in a single batch operation.
Future<void> createSchedulesBatch(List<CleaningSchedule> schedules) async {
  await SupabaseConfig.client
      .from('schedules')
      .insert(schedules.map((s) => s.toJson()).toList());
}
```

### Step 2: Add 'Generar por ciclos' button to schedule management screen

In `schedule_management_screen.dart`, change the single FAB to a `Column` with two FABs (or use a `FloatingActionButton.extended` + a second small FAB). Better approach: replace the single FAB with a `SpeedDial`-style approach using two FABs stacked vertically:

Actually, simpler: Add a second action button in the AppBar:

```dart
appBar: AppBar(
  title: const Text('Gestionar Calendario'),
  leading: BackButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context)),
  actions: [
    IconButton(
      icon: const Icon(Icons.auto_awesome),
      tooltip: 'Generar por ciclos',
      onPressed: _showCycleGeneratorDialog,
    ),
  ],
),
```

Keep the existing FAB for single-date additions.

### Step 3: Create `_showCycleGeneratorDialog` method

This should navigate to a new full-screen dialog (use `Navigator.push` with a new `StatefulWidget` class defined in the same file, or use `showModalBottomSheet` with `isScrollControlled: true`).

**Recommended approach**: Use `Navigator.push` to a new private `_CycleGeneratorPage` widget defined at the bottom of the file. This gives full screen real estate.

### Step 4: Build `_CycleGeneratorPage` widget

A `StatefulWidget` with the following UI:

**AppBar**: 'Generar por Ciclos' with back button

**Body** (scrollable Column):

1. **User selection** — A section titled 'Usuarios' with a list of `CheckboxListTile` widgets, one per user from the `_users` list. Each shows `user.name — user.room`. Admin can check/uncheck users. At least 1 must be selected. Add a 'Seleccionar todos' / 'Deseleccionar todos' toggle button.

   State: `Map<String, bool> _selectedUsers` keyed by userId.

   **Reordering**: Add `ReorderableListView` so admin can drag to reorder the selected users. The order determines the rotation sequence. Show drag handles only for checked users.

   Actually, simpler: use a `List<UserModel> _orderedUsers` that the admin can reorder. Show all users with checkboxes. The order in the list is the rotation order.

   **Simplest approach**: Use `CheckboxListTile` in a regular `ListView`. The rotation order follows the order users appear in the list (which is alphabetical from `getUsers()`). Don't add reordering — keep it simple.

2. **Start date** — A row with 'Fecha inicial: DD/MM/YYYY' and a 'Seleccionar' button that opens `showDatePicker`. Default: today.

3. **Period days** — A row with 'Días de aseo por usuario:' and a number input (`TextFormField` with `keyboardType: TextInputType.number`). Default value: 3. Minimum: 1, maximum: 7.

4. **Number of cycles** — A row with 'Número de ciclos:' and a number input. Default: 1. Minimum: 1, maximum: 12.

5. **Preview button** — A `FilledButton` labeled 'Vista previa' that generates the preview. Disabled if no users selected.

6. **Preview section** — Below the button, show the generated schedule as a grouped list:
   - Group by cycle number: 'Ciclo 1', 'Ciclo 2', etc.
   - Within each cycle, show each user's period: 'UserName — DD/MM al DD/MM' (using first and last date of their period)
   - Use `Card` widgets with `ListTile` for each user period
   - Show total number of entries: 'Total: N entradas'

7. **Confirm button** — A `FilledButton` labeled 'Confirmar y guardar' at the bottom. Only visible after preview is generated. On tap:
   - Show a loading indicator
   - Call `SupabaseService.instance.createSchedulesBatch()` with the generated entries converted to `CleaningSchedule` objects (id: '', userId, date, isCompleted: false)
   - On success: show SnackBar '¡Calendario generado exitosamente!', pop back to schedule management
   - On error: show SnackBar with error

### Step 5: Use CycleGenerator

Import `package:flutter_cleanapp/utils/cycle_generator.dart` and call `CycleGenerator.generate()` when the preview button is tapped. Convert the selected users to the required `({String id, String name})` record format.

### Constructor for _CycleGeneratorPage:

```dart
class _CycleGeneratorPage extends StatefulWidget {
  const _CycleGeneratorPage({required this.users});
  final List<UserModel> users;
  
  @override
  State<_CycleGeneratorPage> createState() => _CycleGeneratorPageState();
}
```

Pass `_users` from the parent screen when navigating.

### All text in Spanish:
- 'Generar por Ciclos' (title)
- 'Usuarios' (section)
- 'Seleccionar todos' / 'Deseleccionar todos'
- 'Fecha inicial:' 
- 'Días de aseo por usuario:'
- 'Número de ciclos:'
- 'Vista previa'
- 'Ciclo N'
- 'Total: N entradas'
- 'Confirmar y guardar'
- '¡Calendario generado exitosamente!'

## Don't
- Do NOT modify calendar_screen.dart
- Do NOT modify any model files
- Do NOT change the existing add/edit/delete dialogs
- Do NOT add any new packages/dependencies
- Do NOT add reordering of users (keep it simple — alphabetical order from getUsers)

title: Epic: Fix auth flow, rename Departamento→Cuarto, add prórroga (extension request) system
description:
## Goals
1. **Fix auth bug**: After login/register, app stays on auth screen. Root cause: race condition in profile loading + missing error recovery in _loadCurrentUser(). The onAuthStateChange listener calls _loadCurrentUser() which queries profiles table with .single() — if the profile row doesn't exist yet (signup trigger delay) or the query fails, _isAuthenticated stays false and user is stuck.
2. **Rename Departamento → Cuarto**: All UI labels, hints, model field names, DB column, and variable names should use 'room' (cuarto) instead of 'apartment' (departamento).
3. **Prórroga (extension request) system**: Users can request an extension for their assigned cleaning week. This notifies the admin and the next person in the schedule. If the next person accepts, they swap — the requester moves to the next week and the acceptor takes the current week.

## Key Constraints
- No external state management (setState only)
- All UI text in Spanish
- Supabase backend with RLS
- Must pass: dart format, flutter analyze, flutter test
- Architecture: SupabaseService singleton, models with fromJson/toJson

## Scope
~12 tasks across auth fix, rename, DB migration, prórroga model+service+UI+notifications
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix auth flow: add retry logic, error recovery, and profile loading resilience
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify)

## What to do

### In `supabase_service.dart` — `getCurrentUser()`:
- Replace `.single()` with `.maybeSingle()` so it returns `null` instead of throwing when no profile row exists yet
- Add a retry mechanism: if `maybeSingle()` returns null AND `auth.currentUser` is not null, wait 1 second and retry up to 3 times (the signup trigger may not have completed yet)
- Signature stays: `Future<UserModel?> getCurrentUser() async`

### In `app.dart` — `_loadCurrentUser()`:
- Wrap the `SupabaseService.instance.getCurrentUser()` call in a try/catch
- On failure, set `_isAuthenticated = false` and show a SnackBar with "Error al cargar perfil. Intenta de nuevo."
- Add a retry button or auto-retry after 2 seconds

### In `app.dart` — `_checkAuth()`:
- Currently only checks `currentSession != null`. Also verify the session is not expired before calling `_loadCurrentUser()`

### In `app.dart` — `onAuthStateChange` listener:
- For `AuthChangeEvent.signedIn`, call `_loadCurrentUser()` (already does this — keep it)
- For `AuthChangeEvent.userUpdated`, also call `_loadCurrentUser()` to refresh profile data

### In `app.dart` — build method:
- When `_isAuthenticated == false` and `_isLoadingUser == false` and there IS a current auth session (edge case: profile load failed but user is authenticated), show a retry screen instead of AuthScreen. The retry screen should have a "Reintentar" button that calls `_loadCurrentUser()` and a "Cerrar sesión" button that calls `_logout()`.

## Dont
- Do NOT change the AuthScreen itself
- Do NOT add any external packages
- Do NOT change the onAuthStateChange event types beyond what is specified
acceptance_criteria:
1. After successful login with valid credentials, user reaches the main dashboard (NavigationBar visible) within 5 seconds
2. After successful registration, user reaches the main dashboard within 5 seconds (retry handles trigger delay)
3. If profile loading fails, user sees a retry screen with "Reintentar" and "Cerrar sesión" buttons — NOT the auth screen again
4. flutter analyze reports no issues
5. flutter test — all existing tests still pass
6. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes

title: Show prórroga status indicator on CalendarScreen schedule entries
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

Enhance the CalendarScreen to show when a schedule entry has a pending or accepted extension request.

### New imports:
- `import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/extension_request.dart';`

### New state:
- `List<ExtensionRequest> _extensionRequests = []`

### Changes to `_loadSchedules()`:
After loading schedules and users, also load extension requests:
```dart
final requests = await SupabaseService.instance.getExtensionRequestsForUser(widget.currentUser.id);
```
Wait — this only gets requests for the current user. For the calendar, we want to show indicators on ANY schedule that has a pending request. But non-admin users can only see their own requests due to RLS.

**Approach**: 
- For admin users: call `getAllExtensionRequests()` (from task .8)
- For non-admin users: call `getExtensionRequestsForUser(widget.currentUser.id)`
- Store in `_extensionRequests`

### Helper method:
```dart
ExtensionRequest? _getRequestForSchedule(String scheduleId) {
  try {
    return _extensionRequests.firstWhere((r) => r.scheduleId == scheduleId);
  } catch (_) {
    return null;
  }
}
```

### UI changes in the ListView itemBuilder:
For each schedule entry, check `_getRequestForSchedule(schedule.id)`:
- If a **pending** request exists: add a small `Chip` below the existing subtitle text saying "⏳ Prórroga pendiente" with `colorScheme.tertiaryContainer` background
- If an **accepted** request exists: add a small `Chip` saying "🔄 Prórroga aceptada" with `colorScheme.secondaryContainer` background
- If **rejected**: no indicator needed

Implementation: wrap the existing `subtitle` in a Column and add the Chip conditionally:
```dart
subtitle: Column(
  crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
  children: [
    Text('${user.room} — Semana del ${_formatDate(schedule.date)}'),
    if (request != null && request.isPending)
      Chip(
        label: const Text('Prórroga pendiente'),
        avatar: const Icon(Icons.schedule, size: 16),
        visualDensity: VisualDensity.compact,
        backgroundColor: colorScheme.tertiaryContainer,
      ),
    if (request != null && request.status == ExtensionRequestStatus.accepted)
      Chip(
        label: const Text('Prórroga aceptada'),
        avatar: const Icon(Icons.swap_horiz, size: 16),
        visualDensity: VisualDensity.compact,
        backgroundColor: colorScheme.secondaryContainer,
      ),
  ],
),
```

## Dont
- Do NOT modify the FAB or admin navigation
- Do NOT modify the month header logic
- Do NOT add any new screens or dialogs
acceptance_criteria:
1. Calendar entries with pending extension requests show a "Prórroga pendiente" chip
2. Calendar entries with accepted extension requests show a "Prórroga aceptada" chip
3. Calendar entries with no request or rejected requests show no extra indicator
4. Admin users see indicators on all schedules; regular users see only their own
5. Existing calendar layout (month headers, current week highlight, user border) is preserved
6. flutter analyze reports no issues
7. flutter test — all existing tests pass
8. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes

title: Add tests for ExtensionRequest model and update existing screen tests

title: Add SQL migration for extension_requests table and RLS policies
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/003_create_extension_requests.sql (create)

## What to do

Create a new SQL migration file that creates the `extension_requests` table for the prórroga system.

### Table schema:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.extension_requests (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.schedules(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  requester_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  next_user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'accepted', 'rejected')),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);
```

### RLS policies:
1. Enable RLS on the table
2. **SELECT**: Authenticated users can see extension_requests where they are the requester_id, next_user_id, OR they are an admin
3. **INSERT**: Authenticated users can insert rows where requester_id = auth.uid()
4. **UPDATE**: Authenticated users can update rows where next_user_id = auth.uid() (the person accepting/rejecting) OR they are an admin
5. **DELETE**: Only admins can delete

### Index:
- Add index on `schedule_id` for fast lookups
- Add index on `status` for filtering pending requests

## Dont
- Do NOT modify existing tables
- Do NOT modify existing migration files (001, 002)
- Do NOT add any Dart code in this task
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at supabase/migrations/003_create_extension_requests.sql
2. Table has all 7 columns: id, schedule_id, requester_id, next_user_id, status, created_at, resolved_at
3. RLS is enabled with 4 policies (select, insert, update, delete)
4. Status CHECK constraint includes pending, accepted, rejected
5. Foreign keys reference schedules and profiles with ON DELETE CASCADE
6. Two indexes exist (schedule_id, status)
7. SQL is syntactically valid (no typos in keywords)
close_reason: a9bccc1 Add SQL migration for extension_requests table and RLS policies

description:
## Files

## What to do

Create a new model class for extension requests (prórrogas).

### ExtensionRequestStatus enum:
```dart
enum ExtensionRequestStatus { pending, accepted, rejected }
```

### ExtensionRequest class:
```dart
class ExtensionRequest {
  final String id;
  final String scheduleId;
  final String requesterId;
  final String nextUserId;
  final ExtensionRequestStatus status;
  final DateTime createdAt;
  final DateTime? resolvedAt;
  
  const ExtensionRequest({...});
}
```

### Methods:
- `factory ExtensionRequest.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json)` — maps from Supabase snake_case columns
  - `id` ← `json["id"]`
  - `scheduleId` ← `json["schedule_id"]`
  - `requesterId` ← `json["requester_id"]`
  - `nextUserId` ← `json["next_user_id"]`
  - `status` ← `ExtensionRequestStatus.fromString(json["status"])`
  - `createdAt` ← `DateTime.parse(json["created_at"])`
  - `resolvedAt` ← `json["resolved_at"] != null ? DateTime.parse(json["resolved_at"]) : null`

- `Map<String, dynamic> toJson()` — for inserts (exclude id, createdAt, resolvedAt — server-generated):
  - `"schedule_id"`: scheduleId
  - `"requester_id"`: requesterId
  - `"next_user_id"`: nextUserId
  - `"status"`: status.name (the Dart enum name matches DB values: pending/accepted/rejected)

- `ExtensionRequest copyWith({ExtensionRequestStatus? status, DateTime? resolvedAt})`

- `bool get isPending => status == ExtensionRequestStatus.pending`

## Dont

- Do NOT import supabase_flutter in this file (its a pure model)

title: Add extension request CRUD methods to SupabaseService
description:
## Files
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify)

## What to do

Add 6 new methods to SupabaseService under a new `// --- Extension Requests ---` section at the bottom of the class.

### Import:
Add `import 'package:flutter_cleanapp/models/extension_request.dart';` at the top.

### Methods:

1. **`Future<List<ExtensionRequest>> getExtensionRequestsForUser(String userId)`**
   - Query `extension_requests` table where `requester_id == userId` OR `next_user_id == userId`
   - Order by `created_at` descending
   - Return mapped list of ExtensionRequest.fromJson

2. **`Future<List<ExtensionRequest>> getPendingExtensionRequests()`**
   - Query `extension_requests` table where `status == 'pending'`
   - Order by `created_at` descending
   - Return mapped list

3. **`Future<ExtensionRequest?> getPendingRequestForSchedule(String scheduleId)`**
   - Query `extension_requests` where `schedule_id == scheduleId` AND `status == 'pending'`
   - Use `.maybeSingle()`
   - Return ExtensionRequest.fromJson or null

4. **`Future<void> createExtensionRequest({required String scheduleId, required String requesterId, required String nextUserId})`**
   - Insert into `extension_requests` with the 3 required fields
   - Status defaults to 'pending' in DB

5. **`Future<void> acceptExtensionRequest(String requestId)`**
   - Update the extension_request row: set `status = 'accepted'`, `resolved_at = now()` (use `DateTime.now().toIso8601String()`)
   - Then perform the schedule swap:
     a. Fetch the extension request to get scheduleId, requesterId, nextUserId
     b. Fetch all schedules ordered by date
     c. Find the current schedule (scheduleId) — this is the requester's week
     d. Find the next schedule where userId == nextUserId and date is AFTER the current schedule's date
     e. Swap the user_ids: update current schedule's userId to nextUserId, update next schedule's userId to requesterId
   - This is the core swap logic. If the next schedule doesnt exist, just update the current schedule's userId to nextUserId (the requester loses their turn).

6. **`Future<void> rejectExtensionRequest(String requestId)`**
   - Update the extension_request row: set `status = 'rejected'`, `resolved_at = now()`

## Dont
- Do NOT modify any existing methods
- Do NOT add UI code
- Do NOT add any new packages
- Do NOT change the SupabaseService singleton pattern
acceptance_criteria:
1. SupabaseService has 6 new methods under "Extension Requests" section
2. getExtensionRequestsForUser filters by requester_id OR next_user_id using .or() filter
3. getPendingExtensionRequests filters by status=pending
4. getPendingRequestForSchedule uses .maybeSingle() and returns nullable
5. createExtensionRequest inserts with 3 required fields
6. acceptExtensionRequest updates status AND performs the schedule user_id swap
7. rejectExtensionRequest updates status and resolved_at only
8. Import for extension_request.dart is present
9. flutter analyze reports no issues
10. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
close_reason: c423fa2 Add extension request CRUD methods to SupabaseService

### New method `_requestExtension()`:
- Set `_isRequestingExtension = true`
- Find the next schedule after the current week where the userId is different from the current user (this is the "next person")
- If no next person found, show SnackBar: "No hay siguiente persona en el calendario para solicitar prórroga"
- Otherwise, call `SupabaseService.instance.createExtensionRequest(scheduleId: currentWeekSchedule.id, requesterId: currentUser.id, nextUserId: nextSchedule.userId)`
- On success: show SnackBar "Solicitud de prórroga enviada" and set `_hasExistingRequest = true`
- On error: show SnackBar with error message
- Set `_isRequestingExtension = false`

```dart
const SizedBox(height: 12),
OutlinedButton.icon(
  icon: const Icon(Icons.schedule_send),
  label: Text(_hasExistingRequest ? 'Prórroga solicitada' : 'Pedir Prórroga'),
  onPressed: (_hasExistingRequest || _isRequestingExtension) ? null : _requestExtension,
),
```
- When `_hasExistingRequest` is true, the button is disabled and shows "Prórroga solicitada"
