title: Build notification banner for next-in-line user to accept/reject prórroga
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

When the current user is the `next_user_id` on a pending extension request, show a prominent notification banner at the top of the HomeScreen (both State A and State B).

### New state variable:
- `ExtensionRequest? _incomingRequest` — a pending request where `nextUserId == currentUser.id`
- `UserModel? _requesterUser` — the user who requested the extension (for display)
- `bool _isResolvingRequest = false` — loading state for accept/reject buttons

### Changes to `_loadData()`:
After loading schedules, also:
1. Call `SupabaseService.instance.getExtensionRequestsForUser(currentUser.id)`
2. Find the first request where `status == pending` AND `nextUserId == currentUser.id`
3. If found, fetch the requester user info from the already-loaded users list (or call getUsers if not loaded)
4. Set `_incomingRequest` and `_requesterUser`

### New method `_acceptRequest()`:
- Set `_isResolvingRequest = true`
- Call `SupabaseService.instance.acceptExtensionRequest(_incomingRequest!.id)`
- On success: show SnackBar "Prórroga aceptada. Ahora eres el responsable esta semana."
- Call `_loadData()` to refresh everything (the schedule swap will have happened)
- Set `_isResolvingRequest = false`

### New method `_rejectRequest()`:
- Set `_isResolvingRequest = true`
- Call `SupabaseService.instance.rejectExtensionRequest(_incomingRequest!.id)`
- On success: show SnackBar "Prórroga rechazada"
- Set `_incomingRequest = null`, `_isResolvingRequest = false`

### New widget method `_buildExtensionBanner()`:
Returns a `Card` with `color: colorScheme.tertiaryContainer` containing:
- Icon: `Icons.swap_horiz` 
- Title: "Solicitud de Prórroga"
- Subtitle: "${_requesterUser!.name} (${_requesterUser!.room}) solicita que tomes su turno de aseo esta semana"
- Two buttons in a Row:
  - `FilledButton`: "Aceptar" with `Icons.check` — calls `_acceptRequest()`
  - `OutlinedButton`: "Rechazar" with `Icons.close` — calls `_rejectRequest()`
- Both buttons disabled when `_isResolvingRequest` is true

### Integration in build():
At the TOP of both the State A and State B `Column.children` lists, before any existing widgets, add:
```dart
if (_incomingRequest != null) ...[
  _buildExtensionBanner(),
  const SizedBox(height: 16),
],
```

## Dont
- Do NOT create a separate screen for this — its an inline banner
- Do NOT modify the admin card
- Do NOT modify the Pedir Prórroga button (task .6 handles that)
- Do NOT add navigation

Future<List<ExtensionRequest>> getAllExtensionRequests() async {

title: Epic: Selección de usuario en prórroga y bloqueo para aceptante
description: Actualmente el sistema de prórroga selecciona automáticamente al siguiente usuario en el calendario. Se necesita: (1) Permitir al usuario elegir con qué usuario intercambiar (de entre todos los que tienen turnos futuros, excluyéndose a sí mismo). (2) El usuario que acepta una prórroga (nuevo responsable) no puede pedir prórroga él mismo — se le desactiva el botón. Éxito: el usuario ve una lista de usuarios disponibles al pedir prórroga, elige uno, y el aceptante queda bloqueado de pedir prórroga.

title: Reemplazar selección automática de usuario por diálogo de selección en prórroga
acceptance_criteria:

5. Confirming creates the extension request with the selected user as nextUserId
6. If no users have future schedules, a snackbar shows "No hay siguiente persona..."
7. The cancel button in the selection dialog dismisses without action
8. The existing cancel/accept/reject prórroga flows still work correctly
9. `dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test` all pass

title: Bloquear botón de prórroga para usuario que aceptó una prórroga entrante
acceptance_criteria:

3. The check runs on every _loadData call (including realtime refreshes)

5. If the user has both accepted an incoming AND exceeded the requester limit, the accepted-incoming message shows (takes priority)
6. Users who have NOT accepted an incoming prórroga can still request normally

title: Epic: Fix prórroga swap — RLS policy blocks schedule user_id updates
description:
## Context
When a non-admin user accepts a prórroga, the schedule swap silently fails because of Supabase RLS policies on the schedules table.

## Root Cause
The schedules table has two UPDATE policies:
1. `Admins can manage schedules` — FOR ALL, only for admins
2. `Users can update own schedule completion` — FOR UPDATE, USING(user_id = auth.uid()) WITH CHECK(user_id = auth.uid())

When userB (nextUser) accepts a prórroga and the code tries to:
- `UPDATE schedules SET user_id = 'userB' WHERE id = 'a1'` (a1 has user_id=userA)
  → USING(userA == userB) = false → **silently rejected**
- `UPDATE schedules SET user_id = 'userA' WHERE id = 'b1'` (b1 has user_id=userB)  
  → USING(userB == userB) = true, but WITH CHECK(userA == userB) = false → **silently rejected**

Neither swap executes. The extension_request is marked as 'accepted' but no schedule rows change. The UI then shows the original user assignments with swap labels, creating the confusing display.

## Solution
Create a Supabase RPC function with SECURITY DEFINER that performs the swap server-side, bypassing RLS. The Flutter code calls this function instead of doing individual UPDATE statements.

## Key Files
- supabase/migrations/ (new migration for RPC function)
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart (modify acceptExtensionRequest to call RPC)
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (swap detection — may need adjustment)

## Success Criteria
- Non-admin users can accept prórrogas and the schedule swap actually executes
- Both periods are correctly swapped in the database
- The calendar list view shows the correct user for each swapped period
- The swap labels show the correct partner name
- Admin acceptance continues to work
- All existing tests pass
close_reason: All 3 tasks completed. RPC function accept_extension_swap with SECURITY DEFINER bypasses RLS. acceptExtensionRequest now calls RPC. Requester can cancel own requests. Quality gate green (81/81).

title: Create Supabase RPC function accept_extension_swap with SECURITY DEFINER
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/007_accept_extension_swap_rpc.sql (create)

## What to do

Create a new SQL migration that defines a Supabase RPC function `accept_extension_swap` that performs the entire prórroga acceptance + schedule swap in a single server-side transaction with `SECURITY DEFINER` to bypass RLS.

### Why
The current approach of doing individual `UPDATE schedules SET user_id = ...` from the Flutter client fails silently because:
- RLS policy `Users can update own schedule completion` requires `user_id = auth.uid()` for both USING and WITH CHECK
- When userB accepts a prórroga, they can't update schedules owned by userA (USING fails)
- They also can't change their own schedules' user_id to userA (WITH CHECK fails)
- Only admins can do this via the `Admins can manage schedules` FOR ALL policy

### SQL function

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- 7. RPC: accept_extension_swap
-- Performs the prórroga acceptance + schedule swap atomically.
-- Runs as SECURITY DEFINER to bypass RLS on schedules.
-- ============================================================

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.accept_extension_swap(
  p_request_id UUID
)
RETURNS VOID
LANGUAGE plpgsql
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
DECLARE
  v_request RECORD;
  v_anchor RECORD;
  v_requester_ids UUID[];
  v_next_anchor_id UUID;
  v_next_ids UUID[];
  v_period_days INT := 3;
  v_schedule RECORD;
  v_count INT;
BEGIN
  -- 1. Fetch and validate the request.
  SELECT * INTO v_request
  FROM extension_requests
  WHERE id = p_request_id AND status = 'pending';

  IF NOT FOUND THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Extension request not found or not pending: %', p_request_id;
  END IF;

  -- 2. Mark the request as accepted.
  UPDATE extension_requests
  SET status = 'accepted', resolved_at = now()
  WHERE id = p_request_id;

  -- 3. Find the anchor schedule.
  SELECT * INTO v_anchor
  FROM schedules
  WHERE id = v_request.schedule_id;

  IF NOT FOUND THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Anchor schedule not found: %', v_request.schedule_id;
  END IF;

  -- 4. Find the requester's period (up to v_period_days consecutive
  --    same-user schedules around the anchor).
  --    We collect IDs by walking backward and forward from the anchor
  --    in date order, stopping on user change, date gap > 1, or maxSize.
  WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT id, user_id, date,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY date) AS rn
    FROM schedules
    ORDER BY date
  ),
  anchor_rn AS (
    SELECT rn FROM ordered WHERE id = v_anchor.id
  ),
  -- Walk backward from anchor
  backward AS (
    SELECT o.id, o.date, o.rn
    FROM ordered o, anchor_rn a
    WHERE o.rn < a.rn
      AND o.user_id = v_anchor.user_id
      AND o.date >= v_anchor.date - (v_period_days - 1)
    ORDER BY o.rn DESC
  ),
  -- Walk forward from anchor
  forward AS (
    SELECT o.id, o.date, o.rn
    FROM ordered o, anchor_rn a
    WHERE o.rn > a.rn
      AND o.user_id = v_anchor.user_id
      AND o.date <= v_anchor.date + (v_period_days - 1)
    ORDER BY o.rn ASC
  )
  SELECT ARRAY_AGG(id ORDER BY date) INTO v_requester_ids
  FROM (
    -- Backward: only consecutive (gap <= 1 day)
    SELECT id, date FROM (
      SELECT b.id, b.date,
             LEAD(b.date) OVER (ORDER BY b.rn) AS next_date
      FROM backward b
    ) sub
    WHERE next_date IS NULL OR (next_date - date) <= 1
    UNION ALL
    -- Anchor itself
    SELECT v_anchor.id, v_anchor.date
    UNION ALL
    -- Forward: only consecutive (gap <= 1 day)
    SELECT id, date FROM (
      SELECT f.id, f.date,
             LAG(f.date) OVER (ORDER BY f.rn) AS prev_date
      FROM forward f
    ) sub
    WHERE prev_date IS NULL OR (date - prev_date) <= 1
  ) combined;

  -- Cap to v_period_days entries (take first N by date).
  IF array_length(v_requester_ids, 1) > v_period_days THEN
    v_requester_ids := v_requester_ids[1:v_period_days];
  END IF;

  -- 5. Find the next user's period anchor: first schedule belonging to
  --    nextUserId with date after the requester's period end.
  SELECT id INTO v_next_anchor_id
  FROM schedules
  WHERE user_id = v_request.next_user_id
    AND date > (SELECT MAX(date) FROM schedules WHERE id = ANY(v_requester_ids))
  ORDER BY date
  LIMIT 1;

  -- 6. Find the next user's period (same walk logic, capped).
  IF v_next_anchor_id IS NOT NULL THEN
    WITH anchor_info AS (
      SELECT date AS anchor_date FROM schedules WHERE id = v_next_anchor_id
    ),
    ordered AS (
      SELECT id, user_id, date,
             ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY date) AS rn
      FROM schedules
      ORDER BY date
    ),
    anchor_rn AS (
      SELECT rn FROM ordered WHERE id = v_next_anchor_id
    ),
    forward AS (
      SELECT o.id, o.date, o.rn
      FROM ordered o, anchor_rn a, anchor_info ai
      WHERE o.rn > a.rn
        AND o.user_id = (SELECT user_id FROM schedules WHERE id = v_next_anchor_id)
        AND o.date <= ai.anchor_date + (v_period_days - 1)
      ORDER BY o.rn ASC
    )
    SELECT ARRAY_AGG(id ORDER BY date) INTO v_next_ids
    FROM (
      SELECT v_next_anchor_id AS id, (SELECT date FROM schedules WHERE id = v_next_anchor_id) AS date
      UNION ALL
      SELECT id, date FROM (
        SELECT f.id, f.date,
               LAG(f.date) OVER (ORDER BY f.rn) AS prev_date
        FROM forward f
      ) sub
      WHERE prev_date IS NULL OR (date - prev_date) <= 1
    ) combined;

    IF array_length(v_next_ids, 1) > v_period_days THEN
      v_next_ids := v_next_ids[1:v_period_days];
    END IF;
  END IF;

  -- 7. Swap: set requester's period to nextUserId.
  UPDATE schedules
  SET user_id = v_request.next_user_id
  WHERE id = ANY(v_requester_ids);

  -- 8. Swap: set next user's period to requesterId.
  IF v_next_ids IS NOT NULL AND array_length(v_next_ids, 1) > 0 THEN
    UPDATE schedules
    SET user_id = v_request.requester_id
    WHERE id = ANY(v_next_ids);
  END IF;
END;
$$;

-- Grant execute to authenticated users.
GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.accept_extension_swap(UUID) TO authenticated;
```

### Important notes
- The function uses `SECURITY DEFINER` + `SET search_path = public` to safely bypass RLS
- The period-finding logic mirrors `_findPeriodSchedules` from supabase_service.dart
- The function is atomic (single transaction) — if any step fails, everything rolls back
- The `v_period_days` constant (3) matches `SupabaseService.cleaningPeriodDays`

## Don't
- Don't modify any existing migration files
- Don't modify any Dart files
- Don't change existing RLS policies (they're correct for normal operations)
- Don't add any other tables or columns
acceptance_criteria:
1. File supabase/migrations/007_accept_extension_swap_rpc.sql exists
2. Function accept_extension_swap(UUID) is defined with SECURITY DEFINER
3. Function SET search_path = public for security
4. Function marks the extension_request as accepted with resolved_at
5. Function finds the requester's period (up to 3 consecutive same-user schedules around anchor)
6. Function finds the next user's period (first consecutive group of nextUserId after requester's period)
7. Function swaps user_ids: requester period → nextUserId, next user period → requesterId
8. Function raises exception if request not found or not pending
9. GRANT EXECUTE to authenticated role
10. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 61d7328 Create Supabase RPC accept_extension_swap with SECURITY DEFINER

## Files

## What to do

### New implementation

Replace the entire method body with:

```dart
Future<void> acceptExtensionRequest(String requestId) async {
  await SupabaseConfig.client.rpc(
    'accept_extension_swap',
    params: {'p_request_id': requestId},
  );
}
```

### What this replaces
The old implementation:
1. Updated extension_requests status to 'accepted' (now done in RPC)
2. Fetched the request details (now done in RPC)
3. Fetched all schedules (now done in RPC)
4. Found requester's period via _findPeriodSchedules (now done in RPC)
5. Found next user's period (now done in RPC)
6. Did individual UPDATE statements for each schedule (now done in RPC)

All of this is now handled atomically server-side by the RPC function.

### Keep _findPeriodSchedules
Do NOT delete the `_findPeriodSchedules` private method — it may still be used by other code or tests. Only change the `acceptExtensionRequest` method body.

## Don't

title: Add RLS policy allowing requester to update own pending extension requests
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/007_accept_extension_swap_rpc.sql (modify — append to same migration)

## What to do

The current RLS policy on extension_requests only allows the `next_user_id` or admins to UPDATE. But the requester needs to be able to cancel their own pending request (by setting status to 'rejected'). The `cancelExtensionRequest` method in supabase_service.dart does:

```dart
await SupabaseConfig.client
    .from('extension_requests')
    .update({'status': 'rejected', 'resolved_at': now})
    .eq('id', requestId)
    .eq('status', 'pending');
```

This fails silently because the requester is NOT the next_user and is NOT an admin.

### Fix
Add a new RLS policy (or modify the existing one) to allow the requester to update their own pending requests. Append to the migration file:

```sql
-- Allow requester to update their own extension requests (for cancellation).
-- This enables the requester to cancel a pending request by setting status to 'rejected'.
CREATE POLICY "Requester can update own extension requests"
  ON public.extension_requests FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (requester_id = auth.uid())
  WITH CHECK (requester_id = auth.uid());
```

### Note
This is safe because:
- The requester can only update requests where they are the requester
- The WITH CHECK ensures the requester_id stays as auth.uid() (they can't change it)
- The actual cancellation logic in Dart only sets status='rejected' and resolved_at

## Don't
- Don't modify the existing 'Next user or admin can update extension requests' policy
- Don't modify any Dart files
- Don't change any other RLS policies
acceptance_criteria:
1. New RLS policy 'Requester can update own extension requests' exists in the migration
2. Policy is FOR UPDATE on extension_requests
3. Policy uses USING(requester_id = auth.uid()) and WITH CHECK(requester_id = auth.uid())
4. Existing 'Next user or admin can update extension requests' policy is unchanged
5. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: 572eb3d Add RLS policy allowing requester to update own pending extension requests

title: Epic: Supabase Backend Integration — Auth, Database Tables, and Admin Role
description:
## Goal
Integrate Supabase as the backend for CleanApp. Add authentication (email+password), create database tables (profiles, schedules, tasks, comments), implement role-based access (user vs admin), and add admin panels for managing users, schedules, and tasks.

## Success Criteria
- Users can register (role: user) and login with email+password
- App is gated behind authentication — must login to access
- All data comes from Supabase (profiles, schedules, tasks, comments)
- Admin users see extra controls: user management panel (from Home), schedule editing (from Calendar), task editing (from Activities)
- Regular users see the existing flow unchanged
- Supabase credentials loaded from .env.local
- App passes flutter analyze, dart format, and flutter test

## Key Constraints
- Supabase project URL: https://qtsymjokdedclwbkunnp.supabase.co
- Supabase anon key in .env.local (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_DEFAULT_KEY)
- Use supabase_flutter package for auth and database
- Use flutter_dotenv for env loading
- Spanish-language UI throughout
- Must not break existing UI patterns (theme, navigation structure)
- Database tables created via Supabase SQL editor (migrations documented in task specs)

title: Integrate role-based UI — add admin buttons to Home, Calendar, Activities screens
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/home_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/activities_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do
Update all screens to: (1) accept currentUser as a parameter, (2) use SupabaseService instead of MockData, and (3) show admin-only controls when user.isAdmin is true.

### 1. lib/app.dart changes:
Pass _currentUser to all screens that need it:
```dart
List<Widget> get _screens => [
  HomeScreen(
    currentUser: _currentUser!,
    onNavigateToActivities: () => setState(() => _currentIndex = 1),
  ),
  ActivitiesScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  CalendarScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
  CommentsScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
];
```

### 2. HomeScreen changes:
- Add `final UserModel currentUser;` parameter (required)
- Replace MockData.currentUser with widget.currentUser
- Load schedule data from SupabaseService (make it a StatefulWidget if needed, or use FutureBuilder)
- **Admin feature:** If currentUser.isAdmin, show an additional card below the user info card:
```dart
if (widget.currentUser.isAdmin) ...[
  const SizedBox(height: 16),
  Card(
    child: ListTile(
      leading: Icon(Icons.admin_panel_settings, color: colorScheme.primary),
      title: const Text("Panel de Administración"),
      subtitle: const Text("Gestionar usuarios del edificio"),
      trailing: const Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
      onTap: () => Navigator.push(context,
        MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => const UserManagementScreen()),
      ),
    ),
  ),
]
```

### 3. ActivitiesScreen changes:
- Add `final UserModel currentUser;` parameter (required)
- Load tasks from SupabaseService.instance.getTasks() instead of MockData
- Load current week schedule from SupabaseService
- **Admin feature:** If currentUser.isAdmin, add an IconButton in the header row:
```dart
Row(
  children: [
    Expanded(child: Text("Actividades de Aseo", ...)),
    if (widget.currentUser.isAdmin)
      IconButton(
        icon: const Icon(Icons.settings),
        tooltip: "Gestionar actividades",
        onPressed: () async {
          await Navigator.push(context,
            MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => const TaskManagementScreen()),
          );
          _loadTasks(); // refresh after returning
        },
      ),
  ],
)
```

### 4. CalendarScreen changes:
- Add `final UserModel currentUser;` parameter (required)
- Convert to StatefulWidget to load data from SupabaseService
- Load schedules and users from SupabaseService
- **Admin feature:** If currentUser.isAdmin, add a FloatingActionButton:
```dart
floatingActionButton: widget.currentUser.isAdmin
  ? FloatingActionButton(
      onPressed: () async {
        await Navigator.push(context,
          MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => const ScheduleManagementScreen()),
        );
        _loadSchedules(); // refresh after returning
      },
      tooltip: "Gestionar calendario",
      child: const Icon(Icons.edit_calendar),
    )
  : null,
```
Wrap the body in a Scaffold to support the FAB.

### 5. CommentsScreen changes:
- Add `final UserModel currentUser;` parameter (required)
- Replace MockData.currentUser with widget.currentUser
- Load comments from SupabaseService.instance.getCommentsForSchedule
- Send comments via SupabaseService.instance.sendComment
- Load current week schedule from SupabaseService

### General pattern for async data loading:
Each screen that needs Supabase data should:
1. Be a StatefulWidget (convert if needed)
2. Have `bool _isLoading = true` state
3. Load data in initState via a `_loadData()` method
4. Show CircularProgressIndicator while loading
5. Handle errors with try/catch and SnackBar

### Important: Remove MockData imports from all modified screens.
The only file that should still import MockData is mock_data.dart itself (keep it for reference but do not use it).

## Do not
- Delete mock_data.dart (keep for reference)
- Change the NavigationBar structure
- Change the theme toggle
- Add new screens (admin screens already exist from previous tasks)
- Break the auth gate logic in app.dart

title: Update all tests for Supabase integration and auth flow
description:
## Files
- test/app_test.dart (modify)
- test/screens/home_screen_test.dart (modify)
- test/screens/activities_screen_test.dart (modify)
- test/screens/calendar_screen_test.dart (modify)
- test/screens/comments_screen_test.dart (modify)
- test/screens/auth_screen_test.dart (create)

## What to do
Update all existing tests to work with the new Supabase-integrated screens. Since screens now require a currentUser parameter and load data from SupabaseService (which needs a real Supabase connection), tests need to be adapted.

### Strategy:
Since widget tests cannot connect to Supabase, we need to test what we can without a backend connection:

1. **AuthScreen tests** (test/screens/auth_screen_test.dart):
   Test the UI elements only (no actual auth calls):
   - "shows login form by default" — verify email, password fields and "Iniciar Sesión" button
   - "toggles to register form" — tap "Regístrate", verify name and apartment fields appear
   - "validates empty email" — submit empty form, verify validation message
   - "validates short password" — enter 3-char password, submit, verify validation

2. **Existing screen tests:**
   Since screens now require `currentUser` parameter, update test wrappers to pass a mock UserModel:
   ```dart
   const testUser = UserModel(id: "test-id", name: "Test User", apartment: "Depto 1A");
   const testAdmin = UserModel(id: "admin-id", name: "Admin User", apartment: "Depto 0A", role: UserRole.admin);
   ```
   
   For screens that now load data from SupabaseService asynchronously, tests may need to be simplified to just verify the widget builds without crashing and shows a loading state, since we cannot mock SupabaseService without a mocking framework.

   **Simplified test approach for each screen:**
   - Verify the widget can be constructed with required parameters
   - Verify it shows a loading indicator initially (CircularProgressIndicator)
   - Do NOT test data-dependent UI (that requires Supabase connection)

3. **App test:**
   The CleanApp widget now requires Supabase initialization. Since we cannot initialize Supabase in tests without credentials, simplify app_test.dart to only test what does not require Supabase:
   - Test AuthScreen renders (since it is shown when not authenticated)
   - Remove tests that require authenticated state

### Important:
- Import UserRole from user_model.dart
- Use package imports for all files
- Keep test structure clean and passing

## Do not
- Add mockito or any mocking packages
- Try to initialize Supabase in tests
- Delete test files — update them
- Test actual Supabase API calls

title: Create Supabase database tables via SQL migrations
description:
## Files
- supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql (create)

## What to do
Create a SQL migration file that documents the exact SQL to run in the Supabase SQL editor. The worker must also RUN this SQL against the Supabase project.

### Connect to Supabase:
Use the Supabase Management API or the `supabase` CLI if available. If neither is available, create the SQL file and document that it must be run manually in the Supabase dashboard SQL editor.

### SQL to create:

```sql
-- 1. Profiles table (extends auth.users)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.profiles (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  apartment TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user' CHECK (role IN ('user', 'admin')),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE public.profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Policies: users can read all profiles, update only their own
CREATE POLICY "Profiles are viewable by authenticated users"
  ON public.profiles FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own profile"
  ON public.profiles FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (auth.uid() = id);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage all profiles"
  ON public.profiles FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin')
  );

-- Auto-create profile on signup
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, name, apartment, role)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>name, ),
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>apartment, ),
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>role, user)
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user();

-- 2. Schedules table
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.schedules (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  date DATE NOT NULL,
  is_completed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

ALTER TABLE public.schedules ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Schedules are viewable by authenticated users"
  ON public.schedules FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage schedules"
  ON public.schedules FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin')
  );

CREATE POLICY "Users can update own schedule completion"
  ON public.schedules FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (user_id = auth.uid())
  WITH CHECK (user_id = auth.uid());

-- 3. Tasks table (defines the checklist items)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.tasks (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  sort_order INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

ALTER TABLE public.tasks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Tasks are viewable by authenticated users"
  ON public.tasks FOR SELECT
  TO authenticated
  USING (true);

CREATE POLICY "Admins can manage tasks"
  ON public.tasks FOR ALL
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin')
  );

-- Insert default tasks
INSERT INTO public.tasks (title, sort_order) VALUES
  (Lavar
acceptance_criteria:
1. File supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql exists
2. SQL creates 4 tables: profiles, schedules, tasks, comments
3. profiles table has columns: id (UUID PK), name, apartment, role (user/admin), created_at, updated_at
4. schedules table has columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (FK to profiles), date, is_completed, created_at
5. tasks table has columns: id (UUID PK), title, sort_order, is_active, created_at
6. comments table has columns: id (UUID PK), schedule_id (FK to schedules), message, created_at — NO sender_id
7. All tables have RLS enabled with appropriate policies
8. Trigger auto-creates profile on auth.users insert
9. Default 8 cleaning tasks are inserted
10. SQL uses IF NOT EXISTS to be idempotent

## What to do
Create an admin-only screen for managing the cleaning task checklist items. Accessible from a button on the ActivitiesScreen (added in the integration task).

### Class: `TaskManagementScreen` (StatefulWidget)

### State:

```

### Layout:
Scaffold with:
- AppBar: title "Gestionar Actividades", leading: back button

- Body: loading spinner or ReorderableListView of tasks

Each task is a Card with:
- ListTile:
  - leading: ReorderableDragStartListener with Icon(Icons.drag_handle)
  - title: Text(task.title)
  - subtitle: Text("Orden: ${task.sortOrder}" + (task.isActive ? "" : " — Inactiva"))
  - trailing: Row(mainAxisSize: min) of:
    - IconButton(Icons.edit, onPressed: _showEditDialog)
    - Switch(value: task.isActive, onChanged: _toggleActive) — to activate/deactivate

### `_showAddDialog()`:
Dialog with:
- TextFormField for title (label: "Nombre de la actividad", validator: not empty)
- "Agregar" FilledButton that calls SupabaseService.instance.createTask(title, _tasks.length + 1)

### `_showEditDialog(CleaningTask task)`:
Dialog with:
- TextFormField for title (pre-filled, label: "Nombre de la actividad")
- "Guardar" FilledButton that calls SupabaseService.instance.updateTask(task.id, title: newTitle)

### `_toggleActive(CleaningTask task, bool value)`:
Call SupabaseService.instance.updateTask(task.id, isActive: value), then _loadTasks()

### Reorder:
On ReorderableListView.onReorder, update sort_order for affected tasks:
```dart
void _onReorder(int oldIndex, int newIndex) async {
  if (newIndex > oldIndex) newIndex--;
  final task = _tasks.removeAt(oldIndex);
  _tasks.insert(newIndex, task);
  setState(() {});
  // Update sort_order for all tasks
  for (var i = 0; i < _tasks.length; i++) {
    await SupabaseService.instance.updateTask(_tasks[i].id, sortOrder: i + 1);
  }
  await _loadTasks();
}
```

### Load tasks: use SupabaseService.instance.getAllTasks() (includes inactive)

## Do not
- Import MockData
- Handle auth/role checking (assume admin access)
- Delete tasks permanently from this screen (only deactivate via isActive toggle)
- Modify activities_screen.dart (that is the integration task)

title: Epic: Responsibility-based UI gating for Activities and Comments
description:
## Goal
Ensure the Activities tab and Comments tab correctly reflect the user's responsibility status at all times, whether responsibility comes from the calendar or from an accepted prórroga swap.

## Current Problems
1. CommentsScreen._loadData() uses getCurrentWeekSchedule() which queries a Monday-Sunday week window, while _computeResponsibleStatus() in app.dart uses a 3-day rolling period window. This mismatch can cause the comments screen to show the wrong responsible person or fail to find the correct schedule after a prórroga swap.
2. ActivitiesScreen._loadTasks() has redundant prórroga-checking logic (lines 93-151) using a Monday-Sunday week window, duplicating and potentially contradicting the authoritative isResponsible flag already passed from app.dart.
3. After a prórroga swap, the Comments screen may still show the original calendar person as responsible instead of the new responsible person (the one who accepted the prórroga).

## Success Criteria
- Responsible user (by calendar OR accepted prórroga): sees task checklist in Activities tab + comment inbox (buzón) in Comments tab
- Non-responsible user (free by calendar OR by having their prórroga accepted): sees libre state in Activities tab + anonymous comment sender chat in Comments tab
- The Comments screen always identifies the correct current responsible person, even after prórroga swaps
- No redundant schedule/prórroga queries in child screens — they trust the isResponsible flag from app.dart

## Scope
- lib/app.dart — pass responsible user info and current schedule to CommentsScreen
- lib/screens/activities_screen.dart — simplify to trust isResponsible flag
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart — use period-based schedule lookup instead of week-based
- lib/data/supabase_service.dart — add period-based schedule query method

title: Fix CommentsScreen to use period-based schedule lookup and correct responsible person after prórroga swap
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

The CommentsScreen currently uses `getCurrentWeekSchedule()` which returns the first schedule in a Monday-Sunday week window. This has two problems:
1. It uses a different time window than the 3-day period used by app.dart
2. After a prórroga swap, the schedule userId is swapped, so getCurrentWeekSchedule() returns the schedule with the NEW responsible userId — but the Comments screen needs to find the schedule for the CURRENT period to correctly identify who is responsible.

### Change _loadData() to use getCurrentPeriodSchedule():

In `_loadData()` (line 96), replace:
```dart
final schedule = await SupabaseService.instance.getCurrentWeekSchedule();
```
with:
```dart
final schedule = await SupabaseService.instance.getCurrentPeriodSchedule();
```

This ensures the Comments screen uses the same 3-day period window as app.dart's `_computeResponsibleStatus()`, so the responsible person shown in the header is always consistent with the Activities tab and Home screen.

### Everything else stays the same:
- The rest of _loadData() correctly uses `schedule.userId` to find the responsible user
- The `widget.isResponsible` flag is already used to switch between inbox and sender views
- The sender view correctly shows the responsible person's name from the schedule
- The inbox view correctly shows the comment inbox

### Also add a realtime subscription for extensions:
Currently CommentsScreen subscribes to `onCommentsChanged` and `onSchedulesChanged` but NOT `onExtensionsChanged`. When a prórroga is accepted, the schedules are swapped (triggering onSchedulesChanged), but adding the extensions subscription ensures the screen reloads if the extension status changes before the schedule swap completes.

In initState(), after the existing subscriptions (around line 73), add:
```dart
_extensionsRealtimeSub = RealtimeService.instance.onExtensionsChanged
    .listen((_) {
      if (mounted) {
        _loadData();
      }
    });
```

Add the field declaration:
```dart
late final StreamSubscription<void> _extensionsRealtimeSub;
```

In dispose(), add:
```dart
_extensionsRealtimeSub.cancel();
```

## Don't
- Don't change the build() method or any UI widgets
- Don't change _sendComment() or _buildChatBubble()
- Don't change the constructor or widget parameters
- Don't remove any existing realtime subscriptions
- Don't change the inbox or sender view logic
acceptance_criteria:
1. _loadData() uses getCurrentPeriodSchedule() instead of getCurrentWeekSchedule()
2. CommentsScreen subscribes to RealtimeService.instance.onExtensionsChanged in initState
3. _extensionsRealtimeSub is declared as a field and cancelled in dispose()
4. The rest of _loadData() is unchanged (user lookup, comment loading, isResponsible check)
5. No UI changes — build(), _buildSenderView(), _buildInboxView() are unchanged
6. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: bf69092 Fix CommentsScreen to use period-based schedule lookup and subscribe to extensions

title: Inject cupertinoOverrideTheme at root to fix dark mode bars globally
description:
## Files
- lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart (modify)
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/comment_chat_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/user_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/extension_requests_screen.dart (modify)

## Problem
CupertinoNavigationBar and CupertinoTabBar read their default colors from CupertinoTheme, NOT from Material ThemeData. Even though we set backgroundColor explicitly on each widget, the internal BackdropFilter and color lerping mechanisms inside CupertinoNavigationBar override our colors. The current approach of manually setting backgroundColor/brightness/automaticBackgroundVisibility/enableBackgroundFilterBlur on every single CupertinoNavigationBar is fragile and still leaks light colors.

## Strategy
1. Strengthen the cupertinoOverrideTheme in darkTheme so Cupertino widgets natively adopt dark colors
2. Remove ALL manual dark-mode backgroundColor overrides from individual CupertinoNavigationBar and CupertinoTabBar widgets — let the theme do the work
3. Keep light mode unchanged

## What to do

### 1. app_theme.dart — Strengthen cupertinoOverrideTheme in darkTheme
Replace the current cupertinoOverrideTheme (lines 98-101):
```dart
cupertinoOverrideTheme: const CupertinoThemeData(
  barBackgroundColor: Colors.black,
  brightness: Brightness.dark,
),
```
With a more complete override:
```dart
cupertinoOverrideTheme: const CupertinoThemeData(
  brightness: Brightness.dark,
  barBackgroundColor: Color(0xDD121212),
  scaffoldBackgroundColor: Colors.black,
  primaryColor: CupertinoColors.activeBlue,
  textTheme: CupertinoTextThemeData(
    navTitleTextStyle: TextStyle(
      color: Colors.white,
      fontSize: 17,
      fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
    ),
    navLargeTitleTextStyle: TextStyle(
      color: Colors.white,
      fontSize: 34,
      fontWeight: FontWeight.w700,
    ),
  ),
),
```
The key change is `Color(0xDD121212)` for barBackgroundColor — this is a near-black with slight transparency that Cupertino expects. The `brightness: Brightness.dark` tells all Cupertino widgets to use dark defaults for icons, text, borders, etc.

### 2. app.dart — Simplify CupertinoNavigationBar (top bar, line 452)
Remove all the manual dark mode conditionals. Replace the entire CupertinoNavigationBar with:
```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: Text(_currentTitle),
  trailing: CupertinoButton(
    padding: EdgeInsets.zero,
    child: const Icon(CupertinoIcons.bell),
    onPressed: () {
      _navigatorKey.currentState?.push(
        CupertinoPageRoute(builder: (_) => const NotificationsScreen()),
      );
    },
  ),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? const Color(0xFF000000)
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  border: null,
  transitionBetweenRoutes: false,
),
```
Key changes:
- Remove `style: TextStyle(color: Colors.white)` conditional on middle — CupertinoTheme handles text color via navTitleTextStyle
- Remove `color: Colors.white` conditional on bell icon — CupertinoTheme handles icon color via primaryColor
- Remove `brightness:` parameter — inherited from CupertinoTheme
- Remove `automaticBackgroundVisibility:` — not needed when theme is correct
- Remove `enableBackgroundFilterBlur:` — not needed when theme is correct
- Keep backgroundColor override for light mode frosted glass effect; use opaque black for dark

### 3. app.dart — Simplify CupertinoTabBar (bottom bar, line 510)
Replace with:
```dart
child: CupertinoTabBar(
  currentIndex: _currentIndex,
  onTap: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
  activeColor: CupertinoColors.activeBlue,
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? const Color(0xFF000000)
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.7),
  inactiveColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? CupertinoColors.systemGrey
      : CupertinoColors.inactiveGray,
  border: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? const Border()
      : null,
  items: const [ ... keep existing items unchanged ... ],
),
```
Keep the BackdropFilter wrapper and Container margin/decoration as-is.

### 4. notifications_screen.dart — Simplify CupertinoNavigationBar (line 141)
Replace with:
```dart
navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: const Text("Notificaciones"),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? const Color(0xFF000000)
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  border: null,
),
```
Remove: style conditional, brightness, automaticBackgroundVisibility, enableBackgroundFilterBlur.

### 5. comment_chat_screen.dart — Simplify CupertinoNavigationBar (line 282)
Replace with:
```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: const Text("Comentario anónimo"),
  backgroundColor: isDark
      ? const Color(0xFF000000)
      : colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  border: null,
),
```
Remove: style conditional, brightness, automaticBackgroundVisibility, enableBackgroundFilterBlur.

### 6. user_management_screen.dart — Simplify CupertinoNavigationBar (line 104)
Replace with:
```dart
navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: const Text("Administrar Usuarios"),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? const Color(0xFF000000)
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  border: null,
),
```

### 7. extension_requests_screen.dart — Simplify CupertinoNavigationBar (line 150)
Same pattern with title "Gestionar Prórrogas".

## Dont
- Change light mode appearance
- Change any business logic, routes, or Supabase initialization
- Change main.dart
- Change the CupertinoTabBar items
- Remove the floating pill design of the bottom bar
acceptance_criteria:
1. In dark mode, CupertinoNavigationBar on ALL screens shows dark/black background with white text — driven by cupertinoOverrideTheme
2. In dark mode, CupertinoTabBar shows dark/black background with no light border
3. In light mode, all bars retain frosted glass / semi-transparent appearance
4. No manual dark-mode TextStyle(color: Colors.white) overrides remain on CupertinoNavigationBar middle text
5. cupertinoOverrideTheme in darkTheme includes brightness, barBackgroundColor, scaffoldBackgroundColor, primaryColor, and textTheme
6. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test
close_reason: cupertinoOverrideTheme strengthened, all manual dark overrides removed. Quality gate green.

### 3. notifications_screen.dart — Update card display for recordatorio
In `_buildAnnouncementCard` (line 68), update the icon and color logic to handle 3 types:

```dart
final (IconData icon, Color cardColor, Color contentColor) = switch (announcement.type) {
  AnnouncementType.update => (
    CupertinoIcons.arrow_down_circle_fill,
    colorScheme.primaryContainer,
    colorScheme.onPrimaryContainer,

    AnnouncementType.update => 'Actualización',

  ...
)
```

### 4. notifications_screen.dart — Update sort order
In `_loadAnnouncements` (line 31-37), update the sort to put updates first, then recordatorios, then avisos:
```dart
announcements.sort((a, b) {
  final order = {AnnouncementType.update: 0, AnnouncementType.recordatorio: 1, AnnouncementType.aviso: 2};
  final typeCompare = order[a.type]!.compareTo(order[b.type]!);
  if (typeCompare != 0) return typeCompare;
  return b.createdAt.compareTo(a.createdAt);
});
```

### 5. home_screen.dart — Update announcement banner for recordatorio
In `_buildAnnouncementBanner` (line 381), update the icon and color logic to handle 3 types, same pattern as notifications_screen.dart. Use CupertinoIcons.bell_fill for recordatorio, tertiaryContainer colors.

title: Reemplazar AlertDialog por pantalla completa en formularios de comunicados
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/admin/feedback_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Replace _showCreateAnnouncementDialog AlertDialog with full-screen dialog
Replace the current `showDialog<bool>` + `AlertDialog` pattern (lines 138-258) with `Navigator.push` to a full-screen route using `MaterialPageRoute(fullscreenDialog: true)`. The new route should return a `bool?` via `Navigator.pop(context, true)`.

The new route should be a `StatefulWidget` (or use StatefulBuilder inside a Scaffold) with:
- AppBar with title "Nuevo Comunicado", a close (X) button as leading, and a "Publicar" FilledButton as action
- Body: `SingleChildScrollView` with `Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16))` containing:
  - `TextField` for "Título" — `maxLength: 100`, `border: OutlineInputBorder()`
  - `SizedBox(height: 16)`
  - `TextField` for "Mensaje" — `maxLines: 8`, `minLines: 4`, `maxLength: 500`, `decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: "Mensaje", alignLabelWithHint: true, border: OutlineInputBorder())`
  - `SizedBox(height: 16)`
  - `Text("Tipo:")` + `SizedBox(height: 8)` + `SegmentedButton<AnnouncementType>` (same 3 segments as current — this task assumes t7i.1 and t7i.2 are already merged)
  - Conditional `TextField` for download link (when type is update)

The Scaffold approach allows the form to use the full screen width and height, making the text fields much more comfortable to use.

After the route returns `true`, execute the same createAnnouncement logic as current (lines 223-253).

### 2. Replace _showEditAnnouncementDialog AlertDialog with full-screen dialog
Same pattern as create dialog (lines 260-378). Replace with `Navigator.push` to a full-screen route:
- AppBar title: "Editar Comunicado", close (X) button, "Guardar" FilledButton action
- Same form layout as create dialog but pre-populated with existing announcement data
- After route returns true, execute the same updateAnnouncement logic

### 3. Implementation approach
Since both dialogs need StatefulWidget behavior (for the SegmentedButton state), create a private helper widget class at the bottom of the file:

```dart
class _AnnouncementFormRoute extends StatefulWidget {
  final String title;
  final String actionLabel;
  final String? initialTitle;
  final String? initialMessage;
  final AnnouncementType? initialType;
  final String? initialLink;
  // ...
}
```

Or alternatively, use a `StatefulBuilder` inside the Scaffold body to manage the `selectedType` state.

## Don't
- Change the Supabase service calls or their parameters
- Change the _buildAnnouncementsTab list view or card layout
- Change the TabBar or tab structure
- Remove any existing functionality (activate/deactivate, delete)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping "Nuevo comunicado" FAB opens a full-screen dialog (not an AlertDialog) with AppBar showing "Nuevo Comunicado"
2. The message field has maxLines: 8 and minLines: 4, making it visually much larger than before
3. Tapping edit on a comunicado opens a full-screen dialog (not an AlertDialog) with AppBar showing "Editar Comunicado"
4. The edit dialog pre-populates title, message, type, and link fields with existing data
5. Creating a new comunicado works correctly (appears in list after publish)
6. Editing an existing comunicado works correctly (changes persist after save)
7. The close (X) button dismisses without saving
8. The SegmentedButton for type selection works correctly in both create and edit
9. Download link field only appears when type is Actualización
10. `dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test` all pass
close_reason: 1133bf7 Replace AlertDialog with full-screen routes for announcement forms

title: Epic: Bug fixes — tab labels, password reset flow, reply suggestions, cupertino_icons font

## Issues to fix

### 1. Tab bar labels and dynamic titles
Remove text labels from CupertinoTabBar (icons only). Show the current screen title in the CupertinoNavigationBar: 'Calendario', 'Actividades', 'Limpy', 'Comentarios', 'Configuración'.

### 2. Password reset deep link flow
When user clicks password reset email link and arrives via limpy://reset-callback, they need a dedicated password change UI — not just the settings tab. Currently it just switches to the Configuración tab which doesn't highlight the password section.

### 3. Reply suggestion chips
Add quick-reply suggestion chips for the responsible user in the comments inbox. Suggestions: 'Ok, enterado', 'Entendido', 'Trabajo en eso', '¡Gracias por avisar!', 'Lo reviso pronto'.

### 4. CupertinoIcons font package
Add cupertino_icons to pubspec.yaml dependencies so CupertinoIcons render correctly instead of showing Chinese characters/boxes.

### 5. INTERNET permission
Already present in all AndroidManifest.xml files — no action needed. User needs to run flutter clean && flutter pub get.

## Constraints
- Don't modify any Supabase logic or auth flow
- All UI text in Spanish
- Quality gate must pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Add quick-reply suggestion chips for responsible user in comments inbox
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/comments_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

### Add suggestion chips above the reply text field in _buildInboxCommentTile

In the _buildInboxCommentTile method (around line 399), add a row of suggestion chips ABOVE the reply input row (before the Padding with the TextField + send button, around line 457).

Add this widget between the existing replies and the reply input:
```dart
// Quick reply suggestions
Padding(
  padding: const EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 8, 16, 0),
  child: Wrap(
    spacing: 8,
    runSpacing: 4,
    children: [
      _buildSuggestionChip('Ok, enterado 👍', replyController),
      _buildSuggestionChip('Entendido ✅', replyController),
      _buildSuggestionChip('Trabajo en eso 🔧', replyController),
      _buildSuggestionChip('¡Gracias por avisar! 😊', replyController),
      _buildSuggestionChip('Lo reviso pronto 🔍', replyController),
    ],
  ),
),
```

### Add _buildSuggestionChip helper method
Add this method to _CommentsScreenState:
```dart
Widget _buildSuggestionChip(
  String text,
  TextEditingController controller,
) {
  return ActionChip(
    label: Text(
      text,
      style: const TextStyle(fontSize: 12),
    ),
    visualDensity: VisualDensity.compact,
    onPressed: () {
      controller.text = text;
    },
  );
}
```

When a chip is tapped, it fills the reply text field with the suggestion text. The user can then edit it or tap send directly.

## Don't
- Don't modify any business logic or Supabase calls
- Don't change the sender view (_buildSenderView)
- Don't change the reply sending logic (_sendReply)
- Don't modify any other files
- Don't add suggestions to the non-responsible user's comment form
acceptance_criteria:

2. 5 suggestion chips appear above the reply text field in each inbox comment tile
3. Suggestions are: 'Ok, enterado 👍', 'Entendido ✅', 'Trabajo en eso 🔧', '¡Gracias por avisar! 😊', 'Lo reviso pronto 🔍'
4. Tapping a chip fills the reply text field with the suggestion text
5. Chips only appear in the inbox view (responsible user), not in the sender view
6. Quality gate passes: dart format --set-exit-if-changed . && flutter analyze && flutter test

title: Add cupertino_icons dependency to pubspec.yaml
description:
## Files
- pubspec.yaml (modify)

## What to do

Add `cupertino_icons: ^1.0.8` to the dependencies section in pubspec.yaml, after the existing `app_links` line (line 16).

The dependencies section should look like:
```yaml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  supabase_flutter: ^2.8.4
  flutter_dotenv: ^5.2.1
  flutter_local_notifications: ^19.0.0
  url_launcher: ^6.3.2
  app_links: ^6.4.0
  cupertino_icons: ^1.0.8
```

After editing, run `flutter pub get` to verify it resolves.

## Context
The app uses CupertinoIcons throughout (CupertinoIcons.house, CupertinoIcons.gear, etc.) but the `cupertino_icons` package was never added to pubspec.yaml. This causes the icons to render as Chinese characters or crossed boxes on real devices because the CupertinoIcons font file is missing.

## Don't
- Don't modify any other files
- Don't change any existing dependencies
- Don't run flutter clean (that's for the user to do locally)
acceptance_criteria:

title: Epic: Prórroga UX improvements — confirmation, cancellation, and per-cycle limit
description:
## Goals
Three UX improvements to the prórroga system:

### 1. Confirmation dialog before requesting
When user taps 'Pedir Prórroga', show a confirmation dialog explaining they will swap their cleaning period with the next user. Show the next user's name. Only proceed if user confirms.

### 2. Cancel pending prórroga
If the user has a pending prórroga (not yet accepted/rejected), show a 'Cancelar Prórroga' button instead of the disabled 'Prórroga solicitada'. Tapping it cancels (deletes or rejects) the pending request. This requires a new service method.

### 3. One prórroga per cycle limit
A user can only request ONE prórroga per consecutive cycle. If they already have an accepted prórroga for the current cycle (determined by checking accepted requests where the user is the requester and the schedule date is within the current cycle window), block the request and show a message: 'Has excedido las prórrogas permitidas en este periodo'.

A 'cycle' is defined as one full rotation of all users. The simplest way to determine this: check if the user has any accepted extension request where the anchor schedule's date is within a reasonable window (e.g., the last N*7 days where N is the number of users in the cycle). For simplicity, we can define it as: the user cannot have more than 1 accepted prórroga between their current period and the previous time they were assigned.

### Simpler cycle limit definition:
Count accepted prórrogas where requester_id = currentUser AND the request was created after the user's previous completed period. If count >= 1, block. Even simpler: count accepted prórrogas where requester_id = currentUser AND status = accepted AND the schedule_id references a schedule whose date is >= the start of the current cycle. For MVP: just check if there's any accepted prórroga for the current user in the last 30 days (configurable). If yes, block.

## Context
- Home screen has 'Pedir Prórroga' button (line 605-612 of home_screen.dart)
- _requestExtension() creates the request directly without confirmation
- _hasExistingRequest tracks pending requests but doesn't allow cancellation
- No cycle limit exists currently

## Success Criteria
- Confirmation dialog shown before creating prórroga request
- User can cancel a pending prórroga
- User blocked from requesting if they already have an accepted prórroga in the current cycle
- All UI text in Spanish
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Limpy v2.1 — Bug fixes, calendar grouping, app icon, comment cleanup
description:

title: Fix profile navigation from 3-dot PopupMenuButton
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)

## What to do

The 'Administrar perfil' option in the 3-dot PopupMenuButton does nothing when tapped. The root cause is that PopupMenuItem wraps a ListTile child, and the ListTile's InkWell absorbs the tap gesture, preventing the PopupMenuItem's onTap/value selection from firing.

### Fix:
Replace the ListTile children inside PopupMenuItem with simple Row widgets that don't have their own tap handlers. The PopupMenuItem already handles the tap — the child should be a passive display widget.

### Current code (lines 181-215 in app.dart):
The itemBuilder returns PopupMenuItem widgets with ListTile children. ListTile has its own InkWell which conflicts with PopupMenuItem's tap detection.

### Replace each PopupMenuItem child from ListTile to Row:
```dart
itemBuilder: (context) => [
  PopupMenuItem(
    value: 'theme',
    child: Row(
      children: [
        Icon(_themeModeIcon),
        const SizedBox(width: 12),
        const Text('Cambiar tema'),
      ],
    ),
  ),
  PopupMenuItem(
    value: 'profile',
    child: const Row(
      children: [
        Icon(Icons.person),
        SizedBox(width: 12),
        Text('Administrar perfil'),
      ],
    ),
  ),
  const PopupMenuDivider(),
  PopupMenuItem(
    value: 'logout',
    child: Row(
      children: [
        Icon(Icons.logout, color: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.error),
        const SizedBox(width: 12),
        Text(
          'Cerrar sesion',
          style: TextStyle(color: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.error),
        ),
      ],
    ),
  ),
],
```

### Also fix the profile navigation to use async/await properly:
The current code uses .then() which may lose context. Change to:
```dart
case 'profile':
  final changed = await Navigator.push<bool>(
    context,
    MaterialPageRoute(
      builder: (_) => ProfileScreen(currentUser: _currentUser!),
    ),
  );
  if (changed == true && mounted) _loadCurrentUser();
```

Note: the onSelected callback needs to be async for this. Change:
```dart
onSelected: (value) {
```
to:
```dart
onSelected: (value) async {
```

## Don't
- Do NOT change the menu items or their order
- Do NOT change the theme toggle or logout logic
- Do NOT modify ProfileScreen itself
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tapping 'Administrar perfil' in the 3-dot menu navigates to ProfileScreen
2. ProfileScreen displays correctly with current user data
3. After saving profile changes and returning, user data is reloaded
4. Theme toggle still works from the menu
5. Logout still works from the menu
6. All three menu items respond to taps
7. flutter analyze reports no issues
8. flutter test — all tests pass
9. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
close_reason: e3f7e79 Fix profile navigation from 3-dot PopupMenuButton

title: Group calendar entries by cleaning period into single cards
description:
## Files
- lib/screens/calendar_screen.dart (modify)

## What to do

The calendar currently renders one Card per schedule row. Since each cleaning period creates 3 consecutive schedule entries for the same user, the calendar shows 3 separate cards. Instead, group consecutive same-user schedules into a single card showing the date range.

### Current behavior (lines 168-189):
The code iterates over sorted schedules and creates one `_ScheduleEntry` per schedule. Each entry becomes a Card in the ListView.

### Required changes:

1. **Replace `_ScheduleEntry` with `_PeriodEntry`** — a new sealed class variant that holds a `List<CleaningSchedule>` (the grouped schedules) and a `UserModel`:
```dart
final class _PeriodEntry extends _ListItem {
  _PeriodEntry({required this.schedules, required this.user});
  final List<CleaningSchedule> schedules;
  final UserModel user;
}
```

2. **Group consecutive same-user schedules** — After sorting schedules by date ascending (line 168-169), iterate and group consecutive entries with the same `userId` into a single `_PeriodEntry`. Algorithm:
   - Walk the sorted list. While the next schedule has the same `userId` as the current group, add it to the group.
   - When the userId changes (or list ends), emit a `_PeriodEntry` with the accumulated group.
   - Month headers should be emitted based on the **first** date of each group.

3. **Update the Card rendering** (lines 231-296) to use `_PeriodEntry`:
   - The subtitle should show the date range: `'<room> — Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY al DD/MM/YYYY'` using the first and last schedule dates in the group. If the group has only 1 schedule, show just `'<room> — Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY'`.
   - `_isCurrentPeriod` should return true if **any** schedule in the group falls within the current period window.
   - `_isCurrentUser` should check the first schedule's userId (they're all the same).
   - `_getRequestForSchedule` should check **all** schedules in the group and return the first non-null result.
   - The `isCompleted` trailing icon should show if **all** schedules in the group are completed.
   - The `currentUser` border highlight should apply if the group belongs to the current user.

4. **Remove the old `_ScheduleEntry` class** — it's replaced by `_PeriodEntry`.

### Edge cases:
- A single schedule (not part of a 3-day period) should still render correctly as a group of 1.
- If the same user has two non-consecutive periods (e.g., dates 1-3 and 10-12), they should be two separate cards because other users' schedules appear between them.
- Month headers: if a period spans two months (e.g., Jan 31, Feb 1, Feb 2), emit the month header based on the first date only.

## Don't
- Do NOT change the data fetching logic (`_loadSchedules`)
- Do NOT change the FAB or admin navigation
- Do NOT modify SupabaseService or any model files
- Do NOT change the Realtime subscription logic
- Do NOT change the extension request (prórroga) chip display logic — just adapt it to check all schedules in the group
acceptance_criteria:
1. Calendar shows 1 card per cleaning period (grouped consecutive same-user schedules) instead of 3 separate cards
2. Card subtitle shows date range: 'Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY al DD/MM/YYYY' for multi-day periods
3. Card subtitle shows single date: 'Periodo del DD/MM/YYYY' for single-schedule groups
4. Current period highlighting works correctly (checks any schedule in group)
5. Current user border highlighting works correctly
6. Completed check icon shows only when ALL schedules in group are completed
7. Prórroga chips still display correctly
8. Month headers still appear correctly (based on first date of each group)
9. Single-schedule groups render correctly
10. Non-consecutive same-user periods appear as separate cards
11. flutter analyze reports no issues
12. flutter test — all tests pass
13. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
close_reason: c68916b Group calendar entries by cleaning period into single cards

title: Change app icon to icon_limpy.png on all platforms using flutter_launcher_icons
description:
## Files
- pubspec.yaml (modify — add flutter_launcher_icons dev dependency and config)
- icon_limpy.png (read — source icon at project root, 96x96 RGBA PNG)
- android/app/src/main/res/mipmap-*/ic_launcher.png (generated)
- ios/Runner/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/* (generated)
- macos/Runner/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/* (generated)
- web/favicon.png (generated)
- web/icons/* (generated)
- windows/runner/resources/app_icon.ico (generated)
- linux/ (generated if applicable)

## What to do

Replace the default Flutter app icon with `icon_limpy.png` on all platforms. The source image is only 96x96 pixels — use the `flutter_launcher_icons` package to handle resizing and platform-specific icon generation.

### Steps:

1. **Add `flutter_launcher_icons` as a dev dependency**:
```bash
flutter pub add --dev flutter_launcher_icons
```

2. **Add configuration to `pubspec.yaml`** at the root level (not under dependencies):
```yaml
flutter_launcher_icons:
  android: true
  ios: true
  macos: true
  web:
    generate: true
    image_path: "icon_limpy.png"
    background_color: "#ffffff"
    theme_color: "#ffffff"
  windows:
    generate: true
    image_path: "icon_limpy.png"
    icon_size: 48
  image_path: "icon_limpy.png"
  min_sdk_android: 21
  remove_alpha_ios: true
```

3. **Run the icon generator**:
```bash
dart run flutter_launcher_icons
```

4. **Verify** the generated icons exist in all platform directories.

### Important notes:
- The source icon is 96x96 which is small. The package will upscale it for larger sizes (like iOS 1024x1024). This is acceptable — the user provided this icon knowing its size.
- If `flutter_launcher_icons` fails or produces poor results due to the small source size, fall back to manual resizing with ImageMagick (`convert` command) for each required size.
- The `remove_alpha_ios: true` flag is needed because iOS doesn't allow transparency in app icons.

### Android icon sizes needed:
- mipmap-mdpi: 48x48
- mipmap-hdpi: 72x72
- mipmap-xhdpi: 96x96
- mipmap-xxhdpi: 144x144
- mipmap-xxxhdpi: 192x192

### iOS icon sizes needed (in AppIcon.appiconset):
- 20x20 @1x, @2x, @3x
- 29x29 @1x, @2x, @3x
- 40x40 @1x, @2x, @3x
- 60x60 @2x, @3x
- 76x76 @1x, @2x
- 83.5x83.5 @2x
- 1024x1024 @1x

### Web icons needed:
- favicon.png (16x16 or 32x32)
- Icon-192.png, Icon-512.png
- Icon-maskable-192.png, Icon-maskable-512.png

### macOS icons needed (in AppIcon.appiconset):
- 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024

### Windows:
- app_icon.ico

## Don't
- Do NOT modify any Dart source code
- Do NOT change the app name or package identifier
- Do NOT remove icon_limpy.png from the project root
- Do NOT change any other pubspec.yaml settings (dependencies, flutter section, etc.)
acceptance_criteria:
1. App icon on Android uses icon_limpy.png (all 5 mipmap densities have new icon)
2. App icon on iOS uses icon_limpy.png (all sizes in AppIcon.appiconset updated)
3. App icon on macOS uses icon_limpy.png (all sizes in AppIcon.appiconset updated)
4. Web favicon.png and web/icons/ use icon_limpy.png
5. Windows app_icon.ico uses icon_limpy.png
6. flutter_launcher_icons is added as dev_dependency in pubspec.yaml
7. flutter_launcher_icons config is present in pubspec.yaml
8. flutter pub get succeeds
9. flutter analyze reports no issues
10. flutter test — all tests pass
11. dart format --set-exit-if-changed . passes
close_reason: b300ee6 Change app icon to icon_limpy.png on all platforms

title: Epic: Force fully dark bars in dark mode across all screens
description:
## Goal
The user reports that the top and bottom bars still look too light in dark mode. Even though Colors.black is set as backgroundColor, CupertinoNavigationBar and CupertinoTabBar have internal rendering mechanisms (automatic background visibility, backdrop filter blur, internal borders) that override the color and make bars appear lighter than expected.

Fix ALL bars across ALL screens to be as dark as possible in dark mode, even if it means losing the frosted glass/opacity effect.

## Screens affected
1. app.dart — main shell CupertinoNavigationBar + CupertinoTabBar (PRIORITY)
2. notifications_screen.dart — CupertinoNavigationBar
3. comment_chat_screen.dart — CupertinoNavigationBar
4. user_management_screen.dart — CupertinoNavigationBar
5. extension_requests_screen.dart — CupertinoNavigationBar
6. feedback_screen.dart — Material AppBar
7. task_management_screen.dart — Material AppBar
8. schedule_management_screen.dart — Material AppBar

## Success Criteria
- In dark mode, ALL navigation bars (top and bottom) appear fully dark/black
- No frosted glass bleed-through or light tinting in dark mode
- Light mode bars remain unchanged (keep frosted glass effect)
- Quality gate passes
close_reason: All bars fully dark in dark mode. Quality gate green: 57 tests, 0 analyze issues.

title: Force fully dark/black bars in dark mode — main shell and all pushed screens
description:
## Files
- lib/app.dart (modify)
- lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/notifications_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/comment_chat_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/user_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/extension_requests_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/feedback_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/task_management_screen.dart (modify)
- lib/screens/admin/schedule_management_screen.dart (modify)

## Problem
Even though Colors.black is set as backgroundColor on CupertinoNavigationBar and CupertinoTabBar, the bars still appear lighter than expected in dark mode. This is because:
1. CupertinoNavigationBar has internal `_CupertinoNavigationBarBackdropFilter` that applies blur and color lerping
2. CupertinoTabBar has an internal top border (`_kDefaultTabBarBorderColor`) that adds a light line
3. The `automaticBackgroundVisibility` feature lerps the bar color with the scaffold background
4. BackdropFilter in the bottom bar wrapper still captures light content behind it

## What to do

### Strategy
For dark mode: use fully opaque Colors.black everywhere, disable ALL blur/transparency/automatic features, and add `border: Border.all(color: Colors.transparent)` to CupertinoTabBar to kill the default light border.

### 1. app_theme.dart — Ensure dark theme has black AppBar
In the dark theme (line 91+), update the appBarTheme to force black background:
```dart
appBarTheme: const AppBarTheme(
  centerTitle: true,
  scrolledUnderElevation: 0,
  backgroundColor: Colors.black,
  foregroundColor: Colors.white,
  surfaceTintColor: Colors.transparent,
),
```
This ensures Material AppBar screens (feedback, task management, schedule management) also get black bars in dark mode.

### 2. app.dart — Main shell top bar (CupertinoNavigationBar, line 452)
The current code already sets Colors.black and disables blur. Keep it but also add `border: const Border()` in dark mode to prevent any default border:
```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: Text(
    _currentTitle,
    style: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
        ? const TextStyle(color: Colors.white)
        : null,
  ),
  trailing: CupertinoButton(
    padding: EdgeInsets.zero,
    child: Icon(
      CupertinoIcons.bell,
      color: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
          ? Colors.white
          : null,
    ),
    onPressed: () {
      _navigatorKey.currentState?.push(
        CupertinoPageRoute(builder: (_) => const NotificationsScreen()),
      );
    },
  ),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.black
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  brightness: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Brightness.dark
      : null,
  border: null,
  automaticBackgroundVisibility:
      Theme.of(context).brightness != Brightness.dark,
  enableBackgroundFilterBlur:
      Theme.of(context).brightness != Brightness.dark,
  transitionBetweenRoutes: false,
),
```
This is already correct. Keep as-is.

### 3. app.dart — Main shell bottom bar (CupertinoTabBar, line 490)
The CupertinoTabBar has an internal default top border that shows as a light line. Override it:
```dart
child: CupertinoTabBar(
  currentIndex: _currentIndex,
  onTap: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
  activeColor: CupertinoColors.activeBlue,
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.black
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.7),
  inactiveColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? CupertinoColors.systemGrey
      : CupertinoColors.inactiveGray,
  border: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? const Border()
      : null,
  items: const [ ... ],
),
```
The key addition is `border: const Border()` in dark mode — this removes the default light top border that CupertinoTabBar renders.

Also, in the Container wrapper (line 490), remove the boxShadow in dark mode entirely (shadows on a black bar against a black background are invisible but can cause rendering artifacts):
```dart
decoration: BoxDecoration(
  borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(28),
  boxShadow: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? null
      : [
          BoxShadow(
            color: Theme.of(context).shadowColor.withValues(alpha: 0.1),
            blurRadius: 12,
            offset: const Offset(0, 4),
          ),
        ],
),
```

### 4. notifications_screen.dart — Already has dark mode treatment. Keep as-is.

### 5. comment_chat_screen.dart — Fix CupertinoNavigationBar (line 282)
Current code uses `colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: isDark ? 1.0 : 0.85)`. Replace with the standard dark mode pattern:
```dart
appBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: Text(
    "Comentario anónimo",
    style: isDark ? const TextStyle(color: Colors.white) : null,
  ),
  backgroundColor: isDark
      ? Colors.black
      : colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  brightness: isDark ? Brightness.dark : null,
  border: null,
  automaticBackgroundVisibility: !isDark,
  enableBackgroundFilterBlur: !isDark,
),
```
Note: `isDark` is already computed in this file as `Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark`.

### 6. user_management_screen.dart — Fix CupertinoNavigationBar (line 104)
Replace:
```dart
navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: const Text("Administrar Usuarios"),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  border: null,
),
```
With:
```dart
navigationBar: CupertinoNavigationBar(
  middle: Text(
    "Administrar Usuarios",
    style: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
        ? const TextStyle(color: Colors.white)
        : null,
  ),
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.black
      : Theme.of(context).colorScheme.surface.withValues(alpha: 0.85),
  brightness: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Brightness.dark
      : null,
  border: null,
  automaticBackgroundVisibility:
      Theme.of(context).brightness != Brightness.dark,
  enableBackgroundFilterBlur:
      Theme.of(context).brightness != Brightness.dark,
),
```

### 7. extension_requests_screen.dart — Fix CupertinoNavigationBar (line 150)
Same pattern as user_management_screen. Replace with dark mode aware version using title "Gestionar Prórrogas".

### 8. feedback_screen.dart — Material AppBar (line 549)
Add explicit dark mode colors:
```dart
appBar: AppBar(
  title: const Text("Gestionar Comunicados"),
  elevation: 0,
  scrolledUnderElevation: 0,
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.black
      : null,
  foregroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.white
      : null,
  bottom: TabBar(
    controller: _tabController,
    tabs: const [
      Tab(icon: Icon(Icons.feedback_outlined), text: "Comentarios"),
      Tab(icon: Icon(Icons.campaign_outlined), text: "Comunicados"),
    ],
  ),
),
```

### 9. task_management_screen.dart — Material AppBar (line 212)
Add dark mode colors:
```dart
appBar: AppBar(
  title: const Text("Gestionar Actividades"),
  leading: BackButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context)),
  elevation: 0,
  backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.black
      : null,
  foregroundColor: Theme.of(context).brightness == Brightness.dark
      ? Colors.white
      : null,
